<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Poppet Attendant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4514)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Poppet attendants are among the most common form of poppets. Most attendants can be found in creative trades, especially among tailors and cobblers.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 19

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Poppet_Attendant.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 0">[Poppet Attendant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4514)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Construct" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=563" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 256

**Perception** +5; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); one language their creator speaks

**Skills**
[Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +6, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +5, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +5, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +6

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +0

**Con** +2

**Int** +0

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Simple Doll** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) The poppet attendant looks like an ordinary doll, fooling others into leaving them alone. When they're in their place of business, the poppet attendant can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) without [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) or [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62). Once a creature realizes that the poppet attendant is alive, the attendant can't Hide from them in this way again.

**Items**
[Artisan's Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2703), [Repair Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2747), [Shears](/Weapons.aspx?ID=119), [Sling](/Weapons.aspx?ID=72) (10 sling bullets)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 15 

**Fort** +9 

**Ref** +3 

**Will** +6 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 17

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)

**Weaknesses**
fire 2

**Pincushion** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The poppet attendant would take piercing damage; **Effect** The poppet directs the implement to a soft part of its body, gaining resistance 5 against the triggering piercing damage.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shears +8 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+2 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+2 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
sling +6 ([Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 50 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 1d6+ bludgeoning

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-615" />
name: Poppet Attendant

**AC** 15

</row>

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)

**Weaknesses**

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
staff +9 ([two-hand 1d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)),
**Damage** 1d4+4 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661)),
**Damage** 1d4+4 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hand crossbow +9 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),

- **Cantrips (1st)**

- **1st**
[Dizzying Colors](/Spells.aspx?ID=1500), [Mending](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597), [Sleep](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675)

**Magic Hat** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** one per day; **Requirements** The poppet mage has a free hand; **Effect** The poppet mage pulls off their hat, and with a jaunty display, pulls one of the following items from their hat: a [lesser glue bomb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3295), a [lesser smoke ball](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3360), or a [_minor healing potion_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2943). This consumable lasts for 1 hour before becoming inert.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Praskith](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4516)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These strange amalgams of plant and fungus with animal tendencies form where the natural world is corrupted by foul magic or frayed planar boundaries. Praskiths are instinctive ambush predators who seek out lightly traveled forest paths and lie in wait in the undergrowth. A praskith swallows prey as quickly as possible and lets its paralytic digestive juices finish the meal, swiftly restoring itself with the nourishment provided by the trapped creature. When collected, neutralized, and refined, the digestive fluids of a praskith form a lacquer that retains some of the creature's acid resistance.

 A praskith comes into being with a rudimentary understanding of the [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119) language, but in some cases it might manifest knowing a different tongue, such as [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), or even [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165). Regardless, praskiths have little patience for chattering with potential meals, though some determined fey and primal spellcasters have been known to form alliances or even closer bonds with praskiths. Befriending a praskith requires great patience and plentiful food—and gifts of food must be alive when handed over.

 When finished feeding, a praskith slips into a quiescent state for a full day, one of the few times it's safe to approach. Upon rousing, it seeks the deep forest to regurgitate the less digestible portions of its meal. These remains can contain valuables that survived digestion and indicate that a praskith's hunting grounds lie nearby.

 Praskiths dwell in deep woods and jungles. Well-fed praskiths develop fruiting bodies that cast forth millions of spores. Spores that happen to alight on carrion grow into new praskiths.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fungus](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 23

**[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 23

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Tendriculos.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 7">[Praskith](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4516)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Huge" />
<trait label="Fungus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=614" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=668" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 257

**Perception** +15; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69)

**Languages**
[Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119); (can't speak any language)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +14

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +7

**Dex** +2

**Con** +5

**Int** -2

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +0
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 25 

**Fort** +17 

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +13 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 120

</row>

**Immunities**
[acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524)

**Resistances**
piercing 5, slashing 5

**Weaknesses**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 5

**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** <actions string="Reaction" /> Vine only.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
mouth +18 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d10+11 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
vine +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d6+11 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Praskith Venom**  ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 21 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) (1 round); Stage 2 clumsy 2 and [slowed 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) (1 round); **Stage 3** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) (1 round)

**Rampant Growth** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** A creature the praskith has Swallowed Whole has taken damage since the end of the praskith's last turn, and the praskith hasn't used any other actions this turn; **Effect** The praskith regains 3d8 Hit Points and recovers from the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) and [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) conditions. It reduces any [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) value it has by 2.

**[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) Large, 2d10+7 acid plus praskith venom, Rupture 14

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Praskith Lacquer</title>

Objects coated with praskith lacquer gain resistance 5 to acid. Making and applying praskith lacquer requires a 5th-level formula, the [Alchemical Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=5117) skill feat, and the fluid from the stomach of a praskith. A successful DC 20 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) check and 4 days of work are enough to protect 1 Bulk of items with no other cost in materials, but the fluid is used up even on a failed check.
</aside>
name: Praskith

Akizendris gnaw at sources of knowledge and lore, gleefully corrupting and altering them to vex scholars across the planes with contradictions and untruths. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Akizendri](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4517) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 258 **Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), entropy sense ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Protean](/Languages.aspx?ID=33); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) (touch only) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +9, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +9, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +10, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +11, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +9, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +9 **Str** +3 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +1 **Entropy Sense** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [prediction](/Traits.aspx?ID=674)) An akizendri can anticipate the most likely presence of a creature through a supernatural insight into chaotic probabilities and chance. This grants them the ability to sense creatures within the listed range. [_Veil of privacy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1739) prevents a creature from being detected via entropy sense automatically (without a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check). --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +11 **Will** +10 **HP** 40 ([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 1) **Resistances** precision 3, protean anatomy 6 **Protean Anatomy** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A protean's vital organs shift and change shape and position constantly. Immediately after the protean takes acid, electricity, or sonic damage, they gain the listed amount of resistance to that damage type. This lasts for 1 hour or until the next time the protean takes damage of one of the other types (in which case their resistance changes to match that type), whichever comes first.
The protean is immune to polymorph effects unless they're a willing target. If [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) or [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66), the protean automatically recovers at the end of their next turn as new sensory organs grow to replace the compromised ones. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet, swim 25 feet; [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Melee** jaws +12 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+3 piercing plus garbled thoughts **Melee** tail +12 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d6+3 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 20, attack +12 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Caustic Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1461), [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=1673), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715) - **3rd** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) - **Constant (4th)** [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The akizendri takes on the appearance of any Small or smaller creature (page 360). This doesn't change its Speed or its attack and damage bonuses with its Strikes, but might change the damage type its Strikes deal. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** 1d8+3 bludgeoning, DC 20 **Garbled Thoughts** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) A creature hit by the akizendri's bite Strike must attempt a DC 20 Will save.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature is also [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round. **Text Immersion** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) As a 1-minute activity, the akizendri physically immerses itself in a page of text it's touching, changing the message of the text in the process. It can exit the book at any point by Dismissing this ability, at which point it appears in a space adjacent to the text. If it does so to begin combat, it rolls a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check for initiative. As long as it remains immersed in the text, the akizendri has no body. It can communicate telepathically with a creature as long as the creature touches the book or scroll that contains it. It can sense nearby creatures using its entropy sense, but not in any other way, nor can it use any [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), or [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) actions or speak aloud. If the object it's immersed in is destroyed, the akizendri reappears in an adjacent square and is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93).
Andals Of Chaos Those who seek secret knowledge beyond their means can bargain with akizendris to provide them with rare tomes in exchange for delivering the immersed akizendri into a library via another book. From there, the akizendri can vandalize books to its heart's content without being detected.
name: Akizendri

Esoboks are brute hunters and pugnacious sentinels who serve as the guard dogs of the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39). These squat, powerful quadrupeds have a frill of dark feathers around their distinctive heads which resemble a crocodile skull. Esoboks rarely bother with those who are truly dead, allowing the dead of the Boneyard to go about their business while remaining watchful for danger. Though cunning when sniffing out threats to the Boneyard or to their psychopomp handlers, they're among the least intelligent of the psychopomps and rarely speak except to utter growling threats. The wise listen when an esobok makes a threat, as it won't do so twice. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Esobok](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4521) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 262 **Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 60 feet, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +8, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +8, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +9, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +4, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +8, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +10 **Str** +3 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** -3 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +8 **Will** +8 **HP** 55 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) **Resistances** [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 5, [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 5 --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** jaws +12 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 1d10+3 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) and shepherd's touch **Melee** claw +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 1d6+3 slashing plus shepherd's touch **Divine Innate Spells** DC 17 - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (×3; self only) **Pounce** The esobok Strides and then makes a Strike. If it began this action hidden, it remains hidden until after the Strike. **Wrench Spirit** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631)) **Requirement** A creature is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) by the esobok's jaws; **Effect** The esobok releases the target from the [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) but wrenches its spirit free as it does so. The creature must attempt a DC 20 Will save. Creatures without souls (such as most [constructs](/Traits.aspx?ID=563)) and creatures whose bodies and souls are one (such as most [celestials](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=600), and [monitors](/Traits.aspx?ID=655)) who roll a failure or critical failure on the save get a success instead.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93).
**Failure** The esobok wrenches the target's soul from its body into its jaws. [Mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=652) [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creatures of level 2 or lower are destroyed, other undead creatures are stunned for 1 round, and all other creatures are [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85). At the end of each of its turns, a creature paralyzed by this effect can attempt a new save to end the effect. The paralysis ends automatically if the esobok attempts a jaws Strike or speaks
**Critical Failure** As failure, but as long as a creature is stunned or paralyzed, it's also [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94). **Shepherd's Touch** An esobok’s Strikes affect [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) creatures with the effects of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune and deal 1d6 void damage to living creatures and 1d6 vitality damage to [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722).
name: Esobok

markdown:
Not all spirits who enter the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39) realize they have died. Catrinas meet these souls, helping to convince them of the finality of their fate to ease a spirit's passing. Catrinas are more likely to intervene when a mortal can't accept their death. They perform their task to keep the afterlife calm rather than out of true compassion for a mortal's grief. Catrinas rarely visit [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26), typically to help an extremely important mortal pass on. Catrinas resemble skeletons dressed in bright flowers and colorful dresses, giving them a simultaneously festive and macabre appearance. Though most catrinas present as feminine, masculine catrinas still dress in bright colors and carry garlands of flowers. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Catrina](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4522) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 263 **Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 60 feet **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 120 feet, [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +14, [Boneyard Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +11, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +14, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +14, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +12, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +11, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +12 **Str** +0 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +12 **Will** +13 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 75 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) **Resistances** [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 5, [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 5 **Calming Presence** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631)) 30 feet. A creature that begins its turn within the area must attempt a DC 18 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to calming presence for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature's attack rolls take a –1 status penalty for 1 round.
**Failure** Any [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) effects that would affect the creature are suppressed, and the creature can't use [hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=300). If the creature is subjected to hostility from any other creature, it ceases to be affected by calming presence and is temporarily immune to calming presence for 24 hours.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but hostility doesn't end the effect. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 2d8+2 bludgeoning plus shepherd's touch **Divine Innate Spells** DC 22 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will; self only) - **3rd** [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) - **4th** [Talking Corpse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712) (at will), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Compel Condemned** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The catrina telepathically compels a creature within 30 feet to approach and allow the catrina to kiss them, in preparation for using Kiss of Death. The target must attempt a DC 22 Will save.
**Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Compel Condemned for 24 hours.
**Failure** The creature must spend each of its actions to move closer to the catrina as quickly as possible while avoiding obvious dangers. If the compelled creature is adjacent to the catrina, it stays still and doesn't act. If the creature takes any damage, the effect ends and the creature is temporarily immune to Compel Condemned for 24 hours. This effect lasts for 1 round, but if the catrina uses this ability again on subsequent rounds, it extend the duration by 1 round for all affected creatures.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but damage does not end the effect. **Kiss of Death** ([Death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The catrina gives a long, passionate kiss to an [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) or willing creature, dealing 3d6 void damage. Any creature damaged by the same catrina's Kiss of Death for 3 consecutive rounds becomes [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) and is [dying 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69). **Shepherd's Touch** A psychopomp's Strikes affect [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) creatures with the effects of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune and deal 1d6 void damage to living creatures and 1d6 vitality damage to [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722).
Dealing With Grief Death often comes with great emotional distress. While catrinas handle these feelings for souls in the afterlife, the emotions of the deceased's loved ones can become a complication. In most cases, a different type of psychopomp—calacas—will handle the matter in the mortal [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26). Similar to catrinas in appearance, these skeletal psychopomps make use of song to ease the feelings of mortals. In extreme cases, such as the threat of a loved one pursuing vile necromancy to restore the dead, a catrina can establish a brief communication between the dead and their loved ones.
name: Catrina

Ancient tales tell that the elemental lord [Atreia](/Deities.aspx?ID=686) created his six-winged children to steward mortal prayers into the realms of the divine. These elementals work hand-in-hand with celestial and fiendish deific servants to accept sacrifices and offerings, but they also bless contrite mortals with absolution and purification, helping them to change the fate that awaits them after death. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Anguished Flame](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4523) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 30 **Perception** +28 **Languages** [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +24, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +27, [Deity Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +25, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +27, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +27, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +23, [Plane of Fire Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +25, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +23 **Str** +5 **Dex** +7 **Con** +5 **Int** +4 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +8 **Eternal Luminosity** ([light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640)) An anguished flame naturally sheds brilliant light like a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2760). When other creatures target the anguished flame, they ignore the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2404), [fog](/Rules.aspx?ID=2815), mist, and [smoke](/Rules.aspx?ID=2845).
**Purifying Flame** An anguished flame can [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=2399) without a [healer's toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2727), instead healing the wounded with the gentle light of their touch. --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +24 **Will** +25 **HP** 260 **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) **Weaknesses** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 10, [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) 10 **Solar Flare** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it takes 2d6 fire damage (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). On a failed save, it also becomes [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) until the end of its next turn. The anguished flame can activate or deactivate this aura by using a single action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait.
**Vulnerable to Blasphemy** If a creature the anguished flame can see and hear spends 1 action with the [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642) trait blaspheming against the gods, the anguished flame becomes [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 until they Collect a Prayer from that creature. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** flaming wing +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)), **Damage** 3d10+11 fire **Ranged** shining ray +27 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d6+6 fire plus 3d6 spirit **Divine Innate Spells** DC 30, attack +22 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585) - **7th** [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530) (×2), [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Collect Prayer** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The anguished flame compels a creature they can see within 60 feet, who must attempt a DC 30 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature immediately uses its reaction to pray.
**Failure** The creature prays. It is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 and can't use reactions for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature is slowed 2. **Focus Gaze** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Requirements** The anguished flame's solar flare aura is active; **Effect** The anguished flame fixes their fiery eyes on a creature they can see within 30 feet. The target must immediately attempt a Fortitude save against the anguished flame's solar flare. If the creature was already [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) by solar flare before attempting its save, a failed save causes it to become [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) until the end of its next turn.
Ages In Darkness Over the eon of Lord [Atreia's](/Deities.aspx?ID=686) imprisonment inside the Garnet Brand, temples to the Lambent King lay dormant and decaying across the [Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=31), and within those temples, his children, known as ygnaires, began to fade. Without the light of their Lord of Fire, these elementals who waned became known as anguished flames. They eventually turned to unmoving bronze, frozen until Atreia's light shone down on them again.
name: Anguished Flame

markdown:
Shokis shepherd wayward souls to the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39). They take great pride in traveling to [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) (and sometimes beyond) to hunt down those who have evaded [Pharasma's](/Deities.aspx?ID=290) judgment, whether they be malicious foes or unfortunate [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722). A shoki is often transported closer to their quarry by a more powerful psychopomp, but they have the ability to return once they've completed their mission. A shoki resembles an aged humanoid with jet-black skin, curling ram's horns, and a large snail's shell on their back. Some believe that a shoki keeps souls within that shell for transport back to the Boneyard, while in reality, that power resides in their infused staff. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Shoki](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4524) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 264 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 60 feet **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Boneyard Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +19, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +20, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +20, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +20, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +16, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +19, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +14 **Str** +4 **Dex** +1 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +5 **Items** countless [religious symbols](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2745), [Staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12) --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +14 **Will** +21 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) **HP** 150 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) **Resistances** [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 10, [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 10 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** staff +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)), **Damage** 2d4+6 bludgeoning plus shepherd's touch **Divine Innate Spells** DC 28, attack +20 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Frostbite](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689), [Vitality Lash](/Spells.aspx?ID=1744) - **2nd** [Calm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will; self only) - **4th** [Holy Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1557) (×3), [Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=1647) - **5th** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) (×3), [Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1601) - **6th** [Spirit Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1685) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (self and locked soul only; to the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39) only) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Infuse Staff** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A shoki's staff becomes a _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) staff_ and is treated as if it were [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=372) while the shoki wields it. A shoki's staff has Hardness 14 and HP 56 (BT 28) while possessed by the shoki and Hardness 5 and HP 20 (BT 10) while out of the shoki's possession. A shoki whose staff is taken or destroyed can infuse a new one with an hour of work. **Soul Lock** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631)) **Requirements** The shoki doesn't have a soul locked within their staff; **Effect** The shoki attempts to capture the soul of a creature on the brink of death: an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creature, a creature with the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition, or a creature that died within the last minute. The target must attempt a DC 32 Will save with the following results.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune to Soul Lock.
**Success** The shoki's staff tugs at the creature's soul but doesn't trap it. If the creature is living, it becomes [doomed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) (or increases its doomed condition by 1). If the creature is a corporeal undead, it becomes [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71). If the creature is an [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) undead, it becomes [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94). The creature then becomes temporarily immune to Soul Lock for 24 hours.
**Failure** The shoki captures the creature's soul in its staff. If the creature is living, it dies. If the creature is a corporeal undead, its body becomes an inanimate corpse. While the soul is locked in the staff, the target can't be returned to life or undeath or rejuvenate through any means, save for powerful magic, such a [_wish_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=125) ritual. If the shoki's staff is destroyed or the shoki wills it, the soul is released. A shoki's staff can only hold one soul at a time. **Shepherd's Touch** A psychopomp's Strikes affect [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) creatures with the effects of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune and deal 2d6 void damage to living creatures and 2d6 vitality damage to [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722).
Tools Of The Trade Shokis utilize numerous tools to aid them in their work, including religious symbols, magic, soul-trapping staves, and false empathy—though shokis spout impassioned speeches and play upon mortal emotions, they hold no compassion for the dead.
name: Shoki

markdown:
Eseneths, commonly known as soul-stitchers, are dispassionate surgeons who repair damaged souls. They operate with professional efficiency, manifesting large stitching needles composed of spirit energy to do their necessary work. An eseneth appears as a slim, hairless, gray-skinned humanoid with no facial features. Patches of dark, raised flesh dot their upper chest and shoulders. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Eseneth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4525) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 265 **Perception** +29; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 120 feet **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +33, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +33, [Boneyard Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +29, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +31, [Sewing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +35, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +33 **Str** +8 **Dex** +8 **Con** +5 **Int** +6 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +32 **Will** +27 **HP** 290 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) **Resistances** [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 15, [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 15 **Sudden Stitch** **Trigger** A creature within the eseneth's reach successfully Strikes the eseneth; **Effect** The eseneth attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) the triggering creature. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 40 feet; [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Melee** spirit needle +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d10+14 piercing plus shepherd's touch and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Ranged** spirit needle +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 3d10+14 piercing plus shepherd's touch **Divine Innate Spells** DC 38, attack +30 - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will; self only) - **4th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will) - **8th** [Spirit Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1685) (×3) - **Constant (4th)** [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Mend Soul** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The eseneth restores 25 HP to itself or an [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) creature it has grabbed. **Seize Soul** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631)) **Requirements** The eseneth has a corporeal creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90); **Effect** The eseneth tries to yank the soul out of the required creature. The eseneth attempts an Athletics check against the target's Fortitude DC.
**Critical Success** The eseneth grabs the target's soul. The body is released and is [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) for 2 rounds. When the body ceases being paralyzed, its soul returns instantly, and the target wakes. The soul—grabbed by the eseneth—is [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632), is invisible, has a fly Speed equal to the creature's Speed, and otherwise has all the same statistics. It can't attack, cast spells, or attempt any skill checks that require a physical body other than checks to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 38), and it must always maintain line of effect to its body.
**Success** As critical success, but the paralysis ends after 1 round.
**Failure** The target remains grabbed or restrained, but its soul remains in its body.
**Critical Failure** The target is no longer grabbed or restrained. **Shepherd's Touch** A psychopomp's Strikes affect [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) creatures with the effects of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune and deal 2d6 void damage to living creatures and 2d6 vitality damage to [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722). **Shred Soul** **Frequency** once per round; **Requirements** The eseneth has an [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90); **Effect** The eseneth deals 3d10+14 force damage to the required creature. **Spirit Grasp** An eseneth can [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) creatures despite being corporeal. The eseneth uses [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) to Grapple incorporeal creatures as normal but can't use Athletics for other actions against incorporeal creatures, like [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382).
Spirit Needles An eseneth's spirit needles are composed of the psychopomp's essence. The needles form and dissipate at the eseneth's will and exist only as long as the eseneth does.
name: Eseneth

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Radiant Warden](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4526)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The enigmatic and strange radiant wardens were constructed thousands of years ago to protect observatories and scholars against the incursion of alien aggressors from the Dominion of the Black. Over time, their roles as guardians expanded to include watching over any region where the laws of time and space have worn thin, particularly near portals and permanent gates between planets, planes, or dimensions. 

Named for both the radial nature of the concentric rings that make up their bodies as well as the glowing radiance of their attacks, radiant wardens carry on their orders, defending sites from invasion with singleminded purpose.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 38

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/RadiantWarden.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 17">[Radiant Warden](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4526)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Gargantuan" />
<trait label="Construct" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=563" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 266

**Perception** +30; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
any one ancient language (such as [Jistkan](/Languages.aspx?ID=59))

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +32, [Astronomy Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +36, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +33, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +32

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +9

**Dex** +6

**Con** +5

**Int** +6

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +0
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 40 

**Fort** +32 

**Ref** +29 

**Will** +28 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 300

</row>

**Immunities**
[bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=60), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [nonlethal attacks](/Rules.aspx?ID=2311), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)

**Resistances**
mental 15, physical 15 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915))

**Gatekeeper Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) 60 feet. A creature that uses a [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) ability within the aura's [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) or enters it via a teleportation ability must succeed a DC 38 Will save or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 and have its destination changed to a point of the radiant warden's choosing within the aura. On a successful save, the creature arrives as intended but is still sickened 1.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, fly 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hammer +32 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)),
**Damage** 3d12+15 bludgeoning plus radiant blow

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
radiant beam +31 ([Force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 4d10+6 force

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 38
- **5th**
[Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will)
- **7th**
[Planar Seal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1635), [Wall of Force](/Spells.aspx?ID=1749)
- **8th**
[Collective Transposition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1978) (×3), [Pinpoint](/Spells.aspx?ID=1633)
- **9th**
[Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1720)

**Orrery** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) Until it acts, the radiant warden appears to be an orrery (or similar large mechanical contraption, such as a telescope). It has an automatic result of 53 on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks and DCs to convincingly pass as such a machine.

**Radiant Blast** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The radiant warden releases a 50-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of bright energy that deals 10d12 force damage (DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The radiant warden can't use Radiant Blast for 1d4 rounds.

**Radiant Blow**   When a creature is hit by the radiant warden's hammer Strike, a flash of radiant energy attempts to anchor the creature in place. The creature must attempt a DC 38 Will save; on a failure, the creature can't use any [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) effects for 1 minute. On a critical failure, the creature is also permanently [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons//">Ancient Intellects</title>

Each radiant warden's animating force consists of raw vitality energy fused to the soul of a willing sacrifice—usually an astronomer or scholar near the end of their natural life. Focused now on protecting a site from any intrusion— including by curious archaeologists or adventurers—a radiant warden might pause before an attack if approached peacefully. Unfortunately, these constructs are prone to speaking in vague riddles or complex mathematical diatribes that can be as confusing as they are intriguing. Often, discussions break down as frustration mounts on either side (or both).
</aside>
name: Radiant Warden

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Trained Raven](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4527)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These omnivorous birds are cunning and opportunistic scavengers. Capable of solving simple puzzles to retrieve desired items, ravens gather at the periphery of civilization, raiding it as needed when they're not hunting in the wilds. Ravens are known for trickery and often trained to further this instinct. Trained ravens will wait for an opening and peck their foes in vulnerable spots.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 13

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Raven.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature -1">[Trained Raven](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4527)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Tiny" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 267

**Perception** +5; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69)

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +5, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +5, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +5

<row gap="medium">
**Str** -3

**Dex** +3

**Con** +0

**Int** -4

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Cunning** A raven can use simple items as tools, such as poking a stick at an opening to tease out a piece of food. They're also quite adept at stealing objects. A raven can't use [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) to [Palm an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=2409), [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=2411), or [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=2412), but it can use [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) to [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2410) light objects that it can carry in its beak or talons or to accomplish other relatively simple tasks.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 15 

**Fort** +2 

**Ref** +7 

**Will** +5 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 7

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 10 feet, fly 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
beak +7 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d4—1 piercing

**Sneak Attack**   A raven's melee Strikes deal an additional 1d4 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Raven Traders</title>

Given enough time to develop the relationship and establish what you want, it's possible to engage a raven in a sort of trade. Placing a small, shiny object where the raven can take it might entice it to grab the prize and fly off, returning 3d6 minutes later with something from its own stash. Convincing a raven to trade requires a successful DC 20 [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) check to [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2400) and, of course, a raven who has the inclination to trade with you in the first place.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-616" />
name: Trained Raven

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Raven Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4528)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A flock of ravens is known as an unkindness. Certainly, the name lives up to its meaning when a swarm of ravens decides to work together. In most cases, a raven swarm like the one presented here won't attack larger foes, but when manipulated by supernatural forces or simple desperation born from hunger, an unkindness of ravens can be a surprisingly dangerous threat.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 3">[Raven Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4528)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
<trait label="Swarm" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=707" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 267

**Perception** +9; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69)

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +10, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +10

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +3

**Con** +0

**Int** -4

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +0
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 19 

**Fort** +7 

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +9 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 30

</row>

**Immunities**
precision, [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=78)

**Resistances**
bludgeoning 2, piercing 5, slashing 5

**Weaknesses**
area damage 5, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 5

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 10 feet, fly 40 feet

**Enraged Cunning**   There are few things as dangerously persistent in the natural world as an angry unkindness of ravens. A raven swarm can hound its prey through most barriers. Simple latches, unsecured chimney flues, loosely shuttered windows, and similar obstacles rarely keep an unkindness away. A raven swarm attempts a [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) check to bypass many of these simple obstructions, typically against DC 20.

**Swarming Beaks** <actions string="Single Action" />  The ravens' angry pecking deals 2d8 piercing damage to each enemy in the swarm's space (DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature that critically fails its save is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1d4 rounds as the ravens focus their attacks on the target's vulnerable face.

</column>

The ravener presented here was once an [ancient cinder dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4347). **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 47 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 47 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 45 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 [Ravener](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4529) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 270 **Perception** +37; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407)) 60 feet, smoke vision, soulsense 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +32, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +39, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +38, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +38, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +40, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +32, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +35 **Str** +9 **Dex** +5 **Con** +9 **Int** +5 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +8 **Smoke Vision** Smoke doesn't impair a cinder ravener's vision; it ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from smoke. --- **AC** 47 **Fort** +38 **Ref** +34 **Will** +37 +2 status to all saves vs. [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) **HP** 500 ([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 20, [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 20 **Cowering Fear** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 42. A ravener's frightful presence causes creatures to cower in fear as well. As long as a creature is at least [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2 or more as a result of the ravener's frightful presence, it's also [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) from the fear.
**Soul Ward** An intangible field of necromantic energy protects a ravener from total destruction. This soul ward has 200 Hit Points. Whenever a ravener would be reduced below 1 Hit Point, all damage in excess of what would reduce them to 1 Hit Point is instead dealt to their soul ward. If this damage reduces the soul ward to fewer than 0 Hit Points, the ravener is [destroyed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=46). A soul ward's Hit Points can be restored only via specific ravener abilities such as Consume Soul, Void Breath, or vicious criticals. A ravener who goes more than a week without successfully using Consume Soul to feed on a dying creature starves, and their soul ward loses 1d4 Hit Points each day until they feed. If the ravener's soul ward loses all its Hit Points while the ravener still has more than 1 HP, they become a [ravener husk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=784).
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** Jaws only
**Discorporate** ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Trigger** The ravener takes excess damage to their soul ward but still has at least 51 Hit Points in their soul ward; **Effect** The ravener draws deeply into their soul ward, discorporating their body into soul energy to escape. They take 50 damage to their soul ward and their physical body vanishes, reappearing 1d4 hours later in a random location within 1 mile from the location where they used Discorporate. --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 180 feet **Melee** jaws +39 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+13 piercing plus 2d6 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) and 2d6 void **Melee** horn +37 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+17 slashing plus 2d6 void **Melee** claw +39 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+13 slashing plus 2d6 void **Melee** tail +37 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+13 slashing plus 2d6 void **Melee** wing +37 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+13 slashing plus 2d6 void **All Becomes Flame** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The ravener curses a creature within 60 feet to have their magic replaced with primordial flames. The creature must attempt a DC 42 Will save. Regardless of the result, the target becomes temporarily immune for 1 day.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [cursed](/Traits.aspx?ID=567) for 1 round. While cursed, any spells that the creature casts gain the [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) trait and have their damage type changed to fire damage, regardless of the original damage type or types of the spell. Additionally, any magical items that the cursed target holds or wields are affected in the same manner, such as changing the cold damage of a [_frost_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2839) rune to fire damage. The cursed creature can attempt to temporarily suppress the curse as an action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait. If the creature succeeds a DC 42 Will save, the curse is suppressed until the end of their turn.
**Failure** As success, but the curse's duration is 1 hour.
**Critical Failure** As success, but the curse's duration is 1 day, and the DC to suppress the curse increases to DC 44. **Consume Soul** ([Death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Trigger** A living creature within 30 feet of the ravener dies; **Effect** The ravener tears the creature's soul from its body with their maw and gulps it down. The dying creature must attempt a DC 44 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The ravener tears off a small chunk of the creature's soul. If the victim is restored to life, they are [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 in addition to any other side effects of returning to life. The ravener adds a number of Hit Points to their soul ward equal to half the creature's level.
**Failure** As success, but the creature's soul is ravaged. The creature is drained 3 and the ravener adds a number of Hit Points to their soul ward equal to the creature's level.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the ravener devours the entire soul. The victim can't be restored to life as long as the ravener exists except via powerful magic such as a [_wish_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=125) ritual, and the ravener adds a number of Hit Points to their soul ward equal to twice the creature's level. **Stoke the Flames** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The ravener intensifies nearby fires. Every foe within 60 feet taking [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) takes 5d6 fire damage. **Void Breath** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The ravener breathes a blast of necrotic flame that deals 20d6 fire damage plus 4d6 [persistent void damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) (DC 44 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature that fails its save is also [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (or drained 2 on a critical failure). If a creature is drained by the ravener's Void Breath, the ravener's soul ward gains 5 HP. The ravener can't use Void Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Vicious Criticals** The ravener treats an attack roll as a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20, as long as the attack roll was a success. Additionally, whenever the ravener makes a critical hit with one of their Strikes, the target must succeed at a Fortitude save or gain the [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 condition. If the target already has a drained value of greater than 0, their drained value instead increases by 1, to a maximum of drained 4. Whenever the ravener applies drain to a creature in this way, their soul ward gains 5 Hit Points.
Ravener Spellcasters Instead of gaining the vicious criticals ability, a ravener spellcaster gains additional spellcasting prowess. When creating your own ravener spellcaster, give it the spellcasting ability of a spellcaster roughly 2 levels higher than a normal spellcasting dragon of its kind. This typically means that if the original dragon had two spells prepared of its highest rank, you should add one more spell of that rank and then two spells of the next highest rank, while if it had three spells prepared of its highest rank, you would add three spells of the next highest rank (if applicable, add only a single 10th-rank spell). Either way, increase its cantrip rank by 1. If the ravener is unusually young, you might be able to use spells from the relevant dragon spellcasters sidebar, but for a typical ancient dragon, consider the following spells to fill in the new slots, depending on which rank of spells you need. As always, a spellcasting ravener's spells should come from the same tradition as the original dragon.
  • **10th** [_ manifestation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1592)
  • **9th** [_ massacre_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1596), [_seize soul_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1664), [_telepathic demand_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2036)
  • **8th** [_ disappearance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1490), [_hidden mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1556), [_quandary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1644)
  • **7th** [_ energy aegis_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1512), [_execute_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1519), [_spell riposte_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2027)
name: Ravener

markdown:
Raveners require a steady diet of souls, and a ravener that's unable to feed for too long eventually cannibalizes their own soul. Should a ravener's soul ward ever be reduced to 0 Hit Points by hunger while the ravener has more than 1 Hit Point (see Soul Ward), they lose all traces of their former identity (losing most of its unique traits, including the one matching its former tradition) and descend into a feral, nearly mindless state. Even if a ravener husk later consumes soul energy, the transformation can be reversed only via Ravenous Repast. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 37 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Ravener Husk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4530) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 271 **Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), soulsense 60 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +22, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +28 **Str** +8 **Dex** +0 **Con** +6 **Int** -5 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +22 **Will** +26 **HP** 325 ([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 10 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 31
**Boneshatter **Trigger** The ravener husk takes any amount of bludgeoning damage** **Effect** The ravener's brittle bones shatter, spraying bone shards everywhere.
Every creature within a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) of the ravener husk takes 7d6 piercing damage (DC 31 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 180 feet **Melee** jaws +29 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+16 piercing plus 2d6 void **Melee** claw +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d4+16 slashing plus 2d6 void **Void Breath** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The ravener husk breathes a torrent of void energy that deals 16d6 void damage in a 40-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). They can't use Void Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Ravenous Repast** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The ravener husk makes a jaws Strike against a deceased creature that has been dead no longer than 1 minute, was [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), and was at least level 15 in life. The ravener attempts a DC 5 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333); if successful, they transform back into a ravener with 1 Hit Point in their soul ward.
name: Ravener Husk

These androgynous, river-dwelling fey delight in manipulating the emotions of those unfortunate enough to fall into their grasp, using humiliation to break victims' wills. Rusalkas enjoy keeping their broken toys nearby, both for continuing entertainment and to aid in their defense, as their captives' misery often drives them to become obsessively loyal to these fey. If a person ever escapes a rusalka's clutches, the rusalka will likely seek them out and shame them for “abandoning” their home, all in the hopes their victim regresses into a distressed mental state and returns to captivity. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Rusalka](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4531) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 272, [Pathfinder #147: Tomorrow Must Burn](/Sources.aspx?ID=8) pg. 81 **Perception** +22; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +21, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +24, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +25, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +21, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +21, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +23, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +25 **Str** +4 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +25 **Will** +21 **HP** 230 **Resistances** fire 10 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 15 **Blurred Form** A rusalka is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) while underwater. --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 50 feet; [_water walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1756) **Melee** tresses +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+10 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 35 - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will), [Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606) (at will) - **5th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463) (at will), [Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1473) (at will) - **Constant (6th)** [Water Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=1756) **Beckoning Call** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The rusalka cries out a compelling invitation. Each non-fey creature within a 300-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 29 Will save. The effect lasts for 1 round, but if the rusalka uses Beckoning Call again on subsequent rounds, the duration extends by 1 round for all affected creatures. Once a creature succeeds at any save against Beckoning Call, that creature is temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) and must spend each of its actions to move closer to the rusalka, avoiding obvious dangers. If a beckoned creature is adjacent to the rusalka, it stays still and doesn't act. If attacked by the rusalka, the creature is freed from captivation at the end of the rusalka's turn.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but if attacked by the rusalka, the creature can attempt a new save only at the start of its next turn, rather than being freed at the end of the rusalka's turn. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** 2d8+10 bludgeoning, DC 32 **Entangling Tresses** A rusalka can have up to eight creatures [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) within their tresses at a time. **Flowing Hair** The rusalka attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check against the Fortitude save of each creature they have [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) by their tresses. The rusalka moves each creature they succeed against up to 10 feet and each creature they critically succeed against up to 20 feet. This movement must all be within reach of its tresses. **Shameful Touch** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The rusalka touches a creature within 5 feet using their hand, stirring up memories of regret and shame. The target must attempt a DC 35 Will save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected. **Success** The target is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1. **Failure** The creature is sickened 1 and [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1. **Critical Failure** The creature is sickened 1, stunned 1, and it must use its first action on its next turn to Strike itself, automatically hitting.
Blue Week Those living in areas where rusalkas dwell know well to avoid the water during the week-long period in early Sarenith when the fey become particularly active, a time known in many regions as Blue Week. Most villages prohibit swimming and fishing during this time, though the prohibition is difficult to enforce, as it takes place during the height of good weather. Particularly superstitious folk take care to lock their doors from both within and without.
name: Rusalka

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Sabosan](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4532)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Sabosans are intelligent, bat-like humanoids who live in warm forests and drink the blood of other creatures, particularly people. They have thin, emaciated torsos and broad, leathery wings that can reach a span of almost 20 feet. Sabosans' heads, necks, shoulders, and upper chests are covered with red or dark-brown fur that obscures their stretched-thin flesh. Though their ears are large and pointed like a bat's, and they can echolocate as bats do, their vision is also quite strong, enough so that they can use it to easily track quarry in low light.

Some naturalist scholars believe sabosans are distant descendants of humans who were afflicted with vampirism but managed to avoid succumbing to undeath. Others posit they were once a cult of demon worshippers whose twisted rites transformed them into their current forms. No matter their true origins, sabosans have infamous reputations among towns and cities south of Golarion's equator. Even mere rumors of sabosans in an area are enough to set off city-wide hunts, and the truly superstitious aren't above setting fires near every grotto, nook, and foxhole they come across to smoke out the nocturnal creatures.

Sabosans hunt during the twilight hours or just after dark, when their echolocation gives them an edge over sleeping prey. They're capable hunters but indiscriminate when it comes to food sources; their rapid metabolisms mean sabosans must eat nearly 20 pounds of meat and fruit per day, supplemented, of course, with copious amounts of blood.

Sabosans' obscure faith reveres two deities: the slain Demon Lord Vyriavaxus, Lord of Shadows, and the nearly forgotten sun god Easivra. Vyriavaxus has an obvious link with the creatures, with his appearance as a giant bat, but their connection to the sun god hints at a complex depth in sabosan beliefs.

([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

The creature must succeed at a DC 22 Fortitude save or become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1. The sabosan gains a number of [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) equal to the number of Hit Points lost by the creature due to its drained condition.

**Fell Shriek** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541)) The sabosan emits a deafening cry in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). Non-sabosan creatures in this area must each succeed at a DC 22 Fortitude save or be [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) for 1 minute.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">The Sabosan Kingdom</title>

Sabosans were not always so confined to the edges of the wilderness. Once, many of their kind occupied the stone metropolis of Jaytirian in the heart of the Mwangi Jungle and defended it against the bestial forces of the dread Gorilla King. However, over the last few hundred years, some unknown force drove the sabosans out, and now they roam the Mwangi Expanse in dwindling numbers, searching for a new home.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Esipil](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4533)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Among the least of the sahkils, esipils delight in spreading fears and uncertainty among those who live with animals that could turn on them. They generally appear to their prey as some kind of benign creature, such as a domesticated dog or cat, but once they gain trust and get their victims alone, they transform into a terrifying monstrosity that looks part dog and part worm, with tendrils of viscera the creature uses as both a tongue and a weapon.

Of all sahkils, esipils are most likely to ingratiate themselves with mortals, sometimes serving powerful spellcasters or simply living among unsuspecting victims, biding their time until they strike.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Nucol](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4534)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Representing the fear of parasites and contagion, nucols appear as violent, pestilence-ridden boars. Their furry hides are covered in oozing pustules that exude a rancid smell. They pollute their victim's body and mind, spreading not only fear but a mind-altering affliction that exacerbates the victim's feelings of anxiety and self-doubt. 

Though very aggressive, nucols are also capable of cunning manipulation. After they infect a victim with potent insecurity, they'll offer to remove the affliction for a price. Many of these deals are esoteric in nature, driving the victim to despair and forcing them to give up something they cherish. The sinister nucol can even reinfect its victim after completing the bargain, starting the cycle over and driving them further into hopelessness. However, a canny negotiator can turn the tables on the fiend and free themselves from their grasp, allowing them to recover from their deep misery.

These gaunt, insectile sahkils torment their foes by focusing on a widespread fear of insects and creeping, crawling vermin. Unlike their more subtle cousins, wihsaaks don't lurk in the periphery and instead directly engage their targets, using their unnerving buzzing to disorient and terrify. When encountering multiple foes, wihsaaks attempt to spread fear to everyone before slashing at them with their devastating claws. Many wihsaaks bear heads that resemble the body of a locust, but some may appear as other widely feared insectile creatures. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Wihsaak](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4535) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 275 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +15 **Str** +4 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 **Easy to Call** A sahkil's level is considered 2 lower for the purpose of being conjured by the [_binding circle_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=110) ritual (and potentially other rituals, at the GM's discretion), but it's always free to attack or leave instead of negotiate unless the primary caster's check is a critical success. --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +15 **Will** +14 **HP** 105 **Immunities** [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 5 **Swarmwalker** Swarms of animals and other unintelligent creatures instinctively leave a wihsaak alone. A wihsaak is immune to the damage from and effects of swarms with an Intelligence of –5. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** claw +17 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d10+7 slashing plus 1d4 spirit **Divine Innate Spells** DC 23 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) - **2nd** [Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455), [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) - **3rd** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Vomit Swarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=2041) - **4th** [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) **Droning Distraction** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Effect** The wihsaak beats its wings rapidly, creating a buzzing drone that numbs creatures' minds. **Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is confused for 1 round and stupefied 2 for 1 minute. **Skip Between** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) The sahkil moves from [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) to the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34) or vice-versa. While in the Ethereal Plane, they're unable to affect anything in the Universe, and they cannot be seen by beings or abilities in the Universe, unless these have an ability that can gaze into the Ethereal Plane. While on the Material Plane the inverse is true for anything on the Ethereal Plane. A summoned sahkil can't use Skip Between.
