<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Pakalchi](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4536)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Pakalchis strive to intensify their prey's inherent insecurity over personal and emotional bonds, playing on the threat of those relationships falling into ruin. They employ tricks of all kinds, such as leaving trails of false evidence or spinning rumors to upend the life of their intended target. These sahkils are among the most manipulative of their kind, pulling strings, both literal and figurative, on their victims over stretched-out periods of time, exhilarating in the despair and fear for as long as possible.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ximtal](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4537)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The struggles of loneliness and the anxiety that accompanies that feeling plague many creatures, and ximtals delight in exploiting this fear for their own perverse gain. Appearing as a hybrid of a rat and crab with grasping tentacles, these scheming fiends manipulate their victims over long spans of time, like pakalchis, to savor the most fear they can. Ximtals use subtlety to entrap their prey and often don't directly engage their targets. They use insecurities as a tool and force poor behavior in mortals they set their sights on. They prefer to focus their machinations on strong-willed and well-intentioned mortals, hoping to erode their pride and self-worth and ultimately lead them to eschew altruistic efforts.

Rather than focusing on a single victim, a ximtal might instead seek out a particular cause relevant to the sahkil's appetites. They often wander the multiverse seeking to sabotage societal crusades or suppress divergent ideologies. They particularly delight in exploiting the fears of groups of like-minded individuals.

While most ximtals work alone, they sometimes enlist the assistance of [pakalchis](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1283), who share similar tastes in mortal fear. Together these two types of fiends can dissolve individual self-worth, relationships, and even entire organizations.

<column gap="tiny">

([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 36

The affected creatures can't perceive their allies or interact with them in any way, and they can move into allies' spaces as if their allies simply weren't there. Allies similarly can't perceive or interact with the affected creatures with one exception: an ally can target an affected creature with an effect that specifically targets curses such as a 4th-rank [_cleanse affliction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467). Every 24 hours, an affected creature can attempt a new saving throw to end this effect.

**Sensory Fever**  ([[disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) A ximtal's withering attacks cause a debilitating disease that targets the senses; Saving Throw DC 36 Fortitude; **Stage 1** creature loses one sense determined randomly: hearing, sight, smell, or taste (1 day); **Stage 2** creature loses an additional sense from the stage 1 list (1 day); **Stage 3** creature loses an additional sense from the stage 1 list (1 day); **Stage 4** creature loses the last sense from the stage 1 list and any special senses, such as [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) or [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) (1 day); **Stage 5** all lost senses are permanent unless restored via sound body or a similar effect

**Skip Between** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) The sahkil moves from [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) to the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34) or vice-versa. While in the Ethereal Plane, they're unable to affect anything in the Universe, and they cannot be seen by beings or abilities in the Universe, unless these have an ability that can gaze into the Ethereal Plane. While on the Material Plane the inverse is true for anything on the Ethereal Plane. A summoned sahkil can't use Skip Between.

markdown:
Among the strongest of their kind aside from the sahkil tormentors, kimenhuls work their craft to foment despair in those who fear failure, forming cycles of self-loathing. These powerful sahkils focus their attention on mortals who are seemingly at the peak of their ability yet harbor secret fears of inadequacy. A kimenhul's predations can leave an indelible mark on their victims. The kimenhul whispers threats and sends fears of crushing failure to their prey, seemingly originating from their target's own mind, a trauma that can be difficult to bear without help. The fiends torment prey as long as the hapless victims live, using their Eternal Fear ability every day to psychically remind their previous victims of their failings. Some unique kimenhuls find themselves in positions of leadership in Xibalba (see sidebar), where they carve out their own small kingdoms and direct groups of sahkils to help them find mortals to torment. They rule these nightmare kingdoms through terror, often delighting in tormenting new petitioners or scheming ways to work against immortal foes. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Kimenhul](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4538) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 278 **Perception** +35; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +36, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +33, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +34, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +38, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +33, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +35, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +36 **Str** +10 **Dex** +8 **Con** +9 **Int** +5 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +7 **Easy to Call** A sahkil's level is considered 2 lower for the purpose of being conjured by the [_binding circle_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=110) ritual (and potentially other rituals, at the GM's discretion), but it's always free to attack or leave instead of negotiate unless the primary caster's check is a critical success. --- **AC** 45 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) **Fort** +33 **Ref** +32 **Will** +35 +4 status bonus vs. [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effects **HP** 425 **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 20 **Feed on Fear** The kimenhul regains 30 Hit Points at the start of its turn as long as any [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) creature is within 100 feet of it.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** If the triggering creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76), the kimenhul can make two claw Strikes against the creature instead of one Strike. --- **Speed** 45 feet, climb 25 feet **Melee** jaws +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 4d12+18 piercing plus 3d6 spirit **Melee** claw +38 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 4d8+18 slashing plus 3d6 spirit and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 42 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) - **9th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1471), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (at will), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524) (at will), [Mask of Terror](/Spells.aspx?ID=1595) (at will), [Phantasmagoria](/Spells.aspx?ID=1629), [Phantasmal Calamity](/Spells.aspx?ID=1630), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) (at will), [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1754) - **Constant (9th)** [Hidden Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1556), [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Eternal Fear** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The kimenhul contorts its faces and presents itself to its enemies in a terrifying and traumatic display that causes lingering fear. Each creature within 100 feet that can observe the kimenhul must attempt a DC 42 Will save. They are then temporarily immune for 10 minutes.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 3.
**Failure** The target becomes frightened 3 and is fleeing as long as it’s frightened. Even after recovering from the initial experience, the trauma is lodged in the target’s mind for 1 year. Once per day, the kimenhul can communicate telepathically with the target for 1 minute as long as both creatures are on the same plane. Any time a creature under the effect of Eternal Fear is in a stressful situation (such as combat or intense social pressure), they must succeed at a DC 11 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) or become frightened 2. While Eternal Fear lasts, the target always becomes fleeing as long as it’s frightened, regardless of the source of the fear. The target can attempt a new saving throw each week to remove these effects, but they can otherwise be removed by only powerful magic such as a [_wish_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=125) ritual.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the effects are permanent, and the target doesn’t get to attempt a weekly save to end the effect. **Frightening Flurry** The kimenhul makes one jaws Strike and two claw Strikes against a single target, in any order. The target becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) with a condition value equal to the number of Strikes that hit it, to a maximum of frightened 3 if all three Strikes hit. **[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=73)** claw **Skip Between** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) The sahkil moves from [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) to the [Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34) or vice-versa. While in the Ethereal Plane, they're unable to affect anything in the Universe, and they cannot be seen by beings or abilities in the Universe, unless these have an ability that can gaze into the Ethereal Plane. While on the Material Plane the inverse is true for anything on the Ethereal Plane. A summoned sahkil can't use Skip Between. **Snatch Between** When using Skip Between, the kimenhul can bring along any creatures it has [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77). **Unsettled Mind** Any creature affected by any of a kimenhul's mental spells or abilities becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 3 for the duration of that effect and for 1d4 rounds thereafter.
name: Kimenhul

They reroll the saving throw with a +1 status bonus; they must use the second result. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** spear +5, **Damage** 1d6+2 piercing **Ranged** sling +7 ([range increment 50 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d4+2 bludgeoning **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 17 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585) - **1st** [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Sanctuary](/Spells.aspx?ID=1660)

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Sceaduinar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4540)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Strange creatures born from jagged crystals in the heart of the Void, sceaduinar are fueled by its void energy and driven to extinguish all life. Resembling crystalline gargoyles with serrated limbs and sharp, bat-like faces, sceaduinar stand about 7 feet tall and weigh around 100 pounds. 

These malevolent beings sometimes gather in great packs of their own kind. Despite their intelligence, they tend to act like cunning, feral beasts for the most part. Sceaduinar sail through the great empty spaces of their home plane, seeking to destroy any sparks of life that find their way into that deadly realm—even the twisted sparks found in undead creatures. 

When they discover portals to other planes, sceaduinar swarm through in great numbers, slaughtering all they come across. While dwelling outside the Void is uncomfortable for sceaduinar, they can exist for extended periods of time apart from their home. Of course, the feeling of a plane where void energy isn't the rule doesn't improve these creatures' attitude, and as a result, they tend to be particularly cruel and violent when encountered in such realms.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 28

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Sceaduinar.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 7">[Sceaduinar](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4540)</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=523" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 281

**Perception** +15; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 120 feet

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +17, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +13, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +6

**Con** +4

**Int** +2

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +0
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 25 

**Fort** +16 

**Ref** +18 

**Will** +14 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 100
([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83) (page 363))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68)

**Weaknesses**
[vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) 10, Resistances physical 5 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915))

**Void Child** Sceaduinar have neither souls nor the ability to create. A sceaduinar is immune to effects that target a soul (such as [_seize soul_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1664) or a [_resurrect_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=123) ritual) or that require knowledge of a creature's identity (such as [_scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1662)), and critically fails [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, fly 60 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d6+4 piercing plus 2d6 void and drain life

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
wing +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d6+4 slashing plus 2d6 void

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 25
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745)
- **2nd**
[Silence](/Spells.aspx?ID=1674)
- **3rd**
[Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=1548), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) (×3)
- **4th**
[Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724)

**Drain Life**  ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) When the sceaduinar damages a living creature with its jaws Strike, the sceaduinar gains 5 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) and the creature must succeed at a DC 25 Fortitude save or become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1. Further damage dealt to the creature by the sceaduinar increases the drained value by 1 on a failed save, to a maximum of drained 4.

**Entropic Touch**   Void damage dealt by a sceaduinar damages [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) and creatures with [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83) as if it were vitality damage. The sceaduinar's melee Strikes have the benefits of the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune on attacks against [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) undead.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Sceaduinar Crystals</title>

Sceaduinar congregate on the great crystalline knots of void energy that accrete in the Void. Sceaduinar prod treelike crystal growths to produce dangerous items akin to miniaturized black holes or great structures that echo with eerie chimes. Sceaduinar also stimulate these aggregations of crystals to yield other sceaduinar and similar hateful creatures of unlife.
</aside>
name: Sceaduinar

markdown:
When a mortal dies, their soul travels to the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39) in the Outer Planes where they're judged by [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=290), the goddess of the dead. Once judged, their soul is sent on to their final reward or punishment and, in the process, transformed into a creature known as a shade. This transformation grants the soul a new body, one whose shape is determined by the prevailing philosophical forces of the plane to which they're sent. A shade's memories from their former life usually fade, reduced to a few hazy fragments akin to half-remembered dreams. Regardless of the shade's size, power, or nature in life, they're a Medium creature in their afterlife. Existence as a shade can last for eons but isn't necessarily eternal. Deities, powerful denizens of the Great Beyond, or even the Outer Planes can further alter a shade's nature by either reducing them into raw quintessence, spiritual essence that's then used to expand a plane's physical manifestation, or by transforming them into a new form of supernatural life such as a [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=551), [monitor](/Traits.aspx?ID=655), or [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600). Should a shade die, their body breaks down in a process akin to decay, their form reverting to the elements that make up their plane. This represents the true end of a soul's journey—their life essence reuniting with the heart of the Great Beyond to be recycled into [Creation's Forge](/Planes.aspx?ID=27), fueling the creation of new souls. **[Recall Knowledge - Shade](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46), [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Shade](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4541) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 282 **Perception** +7; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** see Planar Incarnation **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +7, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +7 **Str** +2 **Dex** +2 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 15 **Fort** +7 **Ref** +7 **Will** +7 **HP** 22 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Planar Incarnation** All shades are formed from and personify the nature of the plane on which they manifest, and their statistics are adjusted as summarized below. They also gain any trait associated with creatures from their plane.
[**Abaddon**](/Planes.aspx?ID=37) **(The Hunted)** The hunted appear as Medium-sized versions of themselves from their prior lives, and they exist to be pursued and consumed by [daemons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=386); **Sanctification** [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521); **Language** [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); **Additional Ability** [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 5; **Melee** fist +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Astral Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=33) (The Untethered)** These appear as astrally projected versions of their mortal forms, yet they lack a silver cord; **Language** any one spoken in life (such as [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155)); **Additional Ability** [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) 20 feet; **Melee** fist +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Axis](/Planes.aspx?ID=38) (The Remade)** The remade appear as parchment-skinned humanoids covered in lines of close black script; **Language** [Utopian](/Languages.aspx?ID=36); **Additional Ability** [immune](/Rules.aspx?ID=2313) to [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) and [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598); **Melee** fist +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39) (The Dead)** The dead appear as animated skeletons of the type of creature they were when they were alive; **Language** [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34); **Additional Ability** [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 3 to piercing and slashing; **Melee** claw +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 slashing
[**Creation's Forge**](/Planes.aspx?ID=27) **(The Enlightened)** The enlightened appear as diaphanous, radiant versions of their mortal selves; **Language** [Jyoti](/Monsters.aspx?ID=708); **Additional Ability** fast healing 5; **Melee** glowing touch +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 vitality
**Dead Vault (The Caged)** The caged are sent to Rovagug's prison plane, the Dead Vault, and appear as chained versions of their mortal bodies, save that their faces are hideous insectile visages; **Sanctification** unholy; **Language** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114); **Additional Ability** [Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=73) bite (page 361); **Melee** bite +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 piercing
[**Dreamlands**](/Planes.aspx?ID=47) **(The Dreamers)** The dreamers appear as their ideal appearance, age, and gender from their mortal bodies; **Language** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164); **Additional Ability** +1 circumstance bonus to all saving throws; **Melee** fist +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Elysium](/Planes.aspx?ID=40) (The Chosen)** The chosen appear as idealized versions of their mortal selves with glowing eyes; **Sanctification** holy; **Language** [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); **Additional Ability** immune to [_blindness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1453) and [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65); **Melee** fist +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Ethereal Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=34) (The Terrorized)** The terrorized appear as sickly, haunted-looking versions of their mortal selves; **Language** any one spoken in life (such as Common); **Additional Ability** immune to [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) and [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331); **Melee** fist +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=41) (The Elect)** The elect have golden halos and ghostly wings, but they otherwise appear as their mortal forms; **Sanctification** holy; **Language** Empyrean; **Additional Ability** fly Speed 20 feet; **Melee** wing +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42) (The Damned)** The damned look like terribly scarred or mutilated versions of their mortal selves; **Sanctification** unholy; **Language** [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117); **Additional Ability** immune to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604); **Melee** jaws +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 piercing
**[Maelstrom](/Planes.aspx?ID=43) (The Shapeless)** The shapeless appear as half-melted reflections of their former mortal selves; **Language** [Protean](/Languages.aspx?ID=33); **Additional Ability** immune to [critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2314) and precision; **Melee** fist +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36) (The Mutilated**) The mutilated appear as they did in their mortal lives yet are covered in wounds or partially adorned or wrapped in chains; **Sanctification** unholy; **Language** [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=166); **Additional Ability** immune to bleed and fear; **Melee** chain +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Nirvana](/Planes.aspx?ID=44) (The Cleansed)** The cleansed take on animal forms that match their personalities in life; **Sanctification** holy; **Language** Empyrean; **Additional Ability** speed 35 feet; **Melee** claw +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 slashing
**[Outer Rifts](/Planes.aspx?ID=45) (The Larvae)** The larvae appear as maggot-like grubs with the face the shade had in life; **Sanctification** unholy; **Language** Chthonian; **Additional Ability** immune to [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) and [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669); **Melee** jaws +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 piercing
**[Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=29) (Air Pneuma)** Air pneuma appear as versions of their mortal selves composed of air; **Language** [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111); **Additional Ability** fly Speed 20 feet; **Melee** gust +7 (air), **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=30) (Earth Pneuma)** Earth pneuma appear as versions of their mortal selves built of earth or stone; **Language** [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109); **Additional Ability** [burrow Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2348) 20 feet; **Melee** fist +7 ([earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585)), **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Plane of Fire](/Planes.aspx?ID=31) (Fire Pneuma**) Fire pneuma appear as versions of their mortal selves shaped from flame; **Language** [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110); **Additional Ability** immunity to fire; **Melee** cinder +7 (fire), **Damage** 1d8+2 fire
**[Plane of Metal](/Planes.aspx?ID=24) (Metal Pneuma)** Metal pneuma appear as versions of their mortal selves made of various, often sharpened, metals; **Language** [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112); **Additional Ability** resistance 3 to electricity; **Melee** claw +7 ([metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d8+2 slashing
**[Plane of Water](/Planes.aspx?ID=32) (Water Pneuma)** Water pneuma appear as versions of their mortal selves formed of flowing water; **Language** [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113); **Additional Ability** [amphibious](/MonsterTemplates.aspx?ID=24), [swim Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2351) 20 feet; **Melee** current +7 ([water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)), **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25) (Wood Pneuma)** Wood pneuma appear as versions of their mortal selves carved from wood; **Language** [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108); **Additional Ability** 8 additional Hit Points and [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) 2 to fire and [axes](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1); **Melee** fist +7 ([wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508)), **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
**[Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) (The Remnants)** These are among the rarest of shades; appearing as plain, bland versions of their mortal selves; **Language** any one spoken in life (such as Common); **Additional Ability** [ferocity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=63); **Melee** fist +7, **Damage** 1d8+2 bludgeoning
First World Shades A soul sent to the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=35) doesn't become a shade. Rather, they immediately reincarnate into a [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=599) creature whose temperament and role match their mortal personalities. This process tends to leave souls who incarnate into the First World with slightly more memories than most shades, yet still not enough that they remember who they once were.
Other Shades Each plane (save the Void) has its own category of shade, but not every plane present in the Pathfinder setting is listed here. Certainly, planes of your own design aren't represented! You can use the wide range of types and descriptions here as inspiration for creating new shades: each needs a name, appearance, language, additional ability, and attack. For instance, [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=281) maintains a demiplane called Cynosure that straddles [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) and [Elysium](/Planes.aspx?ID=40). Shades here are known as the chosen dreamers and appear as idealized, glowing versions of their mortal selves; they speak [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), are immune to [confusion](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94), and deal bludgeoning damage with their fists.
name: Shade

markdown:
Shae are wispy, tenebrous creatures native to the Plane of Shadow. Their amorphous bodies appear in constant states of flux. They cast no shadows of their own, instead exchanging umbral energy with nearby shadows that shrink and grow alongside them. Most shae wear clothing spun from shadows that shift with them, though diplomats sometimes don more conventional garments while entertaining outsiders. Their most distinctive apparel is their white stone masks, which shae don only when they must put on a discernible “face” to interact with humanoids. They do so begrudgingly, considering themselves superior to humanoids. But donning masks allows them to be more easily understood and thus keep their interactions with their perceived lessers as brief as possible. The dynamic between a shae and humanoids shifts if humanoids come to worship a shae, however. A mortal who shows a shae deference is worth keeping around, so many shae amass cults of personality or expansive entourages. To even arrange a meeting with such a self-important shae can be challenging, requiring one to deal with numerous sycophants who insist on vetting the newcomer before wasting their shae's precious time. According to shae lore, they have transcended the material world and now embody a cosmological equilibrium of reality and illusion. Their claims to metaphysical ascendance and knowledge of the secrets of shadows entice many mortal supplicants to join shae courts and cults. In the Shae language, their name means “unbound,” symbolizing their belief that their ephemeral nature frees them from the moral and social strictures binding other sentient creatures. Making a virtue of capriciousness, shae feel little obligation to fulfill oaths or obey laws, so making a compact with a mortal means little to them. [Shae](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4542) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 284 **Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118), [Shae](/Languages.aspx?ID=71), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +9, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +11, [Netherworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +13 **Str** +3 **Dex** +5 **Con** +1 **Int** +3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 **Shadow Shift** Being made partially of shadow themselves, shae are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) in [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) or [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2404) even to creatures that can see clearly in those light levels. **Items** [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358) (5) --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +11 **Will** +10 **HP** 45 **Immunities** precision **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 5, [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 5 **Counterattack** **Trigger** The shae is targeted by an attack from an adjacent creature that misses due to the shae being [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62); **Requirements** The shae is aware of the attack; **Effect** The shae makes a melee Strike against the attacker.
**Slip** **Trigger** A creature moves adjacent to the shae; **Effect** The shae teleports to an unoccupied space adjacent to another creature they can see within 30 feet. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 35 feet; swift steps, tenebral form **Melee** dagger +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+5 piercing and 1d6 cold **Ranged** dagger +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+5 piercing and 1d6 cold **Occult Innate Spells** DC 21 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745) - **4th** [Vapor Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1738) (at will) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (self only, to [Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36) or [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) only) **Bide** The shae prepares to take action against their foes, watching their opponent and waiting for the right opportunity to respond. The shae gains a second reaction until the start of their next turn, though they still can't use more than one reaction on the same triggering action. **Swift Steps** The shae's movement doesn't trigger reactions. **Tenebral Form** The shae can [_Fly_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534) at full Speed in vapor form.
Children Of Shadow Though rare, shae occasionally engage in relationships with mortals, resulting in children born as fetchlings. Though shae maintain a cool superiority over their mortal children, fetchlings who join a shae's cult are placed in positions of authority over other mortals and receive blatantly preferential treatment.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Shoggoth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4543)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

While raving fanatics and doomsaying prophets desperately claim the monstrous shoggoth to be nothing more than a drug-induced vision or unreal nightmare, the truth is altogether more dire. Shoggoths exist, yet they tend to keep to the deepest of ocean trenches or the remotest caverns and ruins, only emerging to spread chaos and destruction in their slimy wakes. 

The first shoggoths were created by an alien species to serve as mindless beasts of burden. Their vast bulk and amorphous nature made them useful for many kinds of labor, and their ability to spontaneously form new eyes, mouths, or other organs made them incredibly versatile. Eventually, they developed enough intelligence to rebel against their masters, and now they lurk in the lightless deeps. 

A shoggoth has goals unfathomable to humanoid beings. They remember their eons of servitude, and comparedWhile raving fanatics and doomsaying prophets desperately claim the monstrous shoggoth to be nothing more than a drug-induced vision or unreal nightmare, the truth is altogether more dire. Shoggoths exist, yet they tend to keep to the deepest of ocean trenches or the remotest caverns and ruins, only emerging to spread chaos and destruction in their slimy wakes. The first shoggoths were created by an alien species to serve as mindless beasts of burden. Their vast bulk and amorphous nature made them useful for many kinds of labor, and their ability to spontaneously form new eyes, mouths, or other organs made them incredibly versatile. Eventually, they developed enough intelligence to rebel against their masters, and now they lurk in the lightless deeps. A shoggoth has goals unfathomable to humanoid beings. They remember their eons of servitude, and compared to their mysterious masters, other sentient beings are mere specks that crawl upon the surface, indistinguishable from animals. When they roll their hideous body over a band of explorers, engulfing them in a gelatinous press of gnawing teeth, it's not so much evil as uncaring. 

Shoggoths can become the object of worship for cults dedicated to chaos and entropy. They don't respond to this worship but can be counted on to consume any victim the cult sacrifices to them. Rumors of shoggoths with even greater intellects are, one would hope, just that, for the damage one capable of higher reasoning could wreak upon the world is unsettling to say the least.

[bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59), [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64), [critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2314), [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66), precision, [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696)

A shoggoth constantly voices syllables and mutterings that mortals weren't meant to hear. A creature entering the aura or starting its turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 38 Will save or become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round (2d4 rounds on a critical failure). A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 24 hours.

**[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** <actions string="Single Action" />  2d10+15 bludgeoning, DC 40

**Eat Away**   A creature that begins its turn inside the shoggoth takes 9d6 acid damage.

**[Engulf](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=61)** <actions string="Two Actions" />  DC 40, 6d6 acid, Escape DC 40, Rupture 40

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Tekeli-Li</title>

While a shoggoth's cacophony is an eldritch mix of sound and dangerous secrets, the phrase “tekeli-li” is the most oft-repeated cry, and these mysterious words are always discernible among their vocalizations. Sometimes, wild birds that dwell in places haunted by shoggoths cry out this strange phrase, while near other shoggoth dwellings, it can be heard on blasts of frozen wind. Attempts to translate the phrase, even via magic, have only ever met with failure, as if the words themselves resist revealing their secrets to the world.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Shriezyx](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4544)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Long ago, Thassilonian wizards created shriezyx in twisted experiments to act as guards and test subjects. These 300-pound aberrations have outlived their creators and mostly make their homes inside Thassilonian ruins, caverns, and deep in the Darklands. Their bodies are covered in hard, extremely flammable chitin that molts as they grow.

While a shriezyx looks like a bestial, three-eyed spider, the truth behind these creatures is far more grotesque. Instead of spinning silk, a shriezyx spits strands of unformed, sticky flesh from its mouth. When fresh, theses fleshy strands are coated in a nerve-numbing toxin that slows prey. The weblike flesh-spittle coats shriezyx lairs and nests, wrapped around the bones for their former meals. Fortunately for those who stumble into these lairs, a shriezyx's toxin dissipates when exposed to air, leaving the fleshy webs only sickeningly sticky. Unfortunately for those same travelers, shriezyx are often communal creatures that gather in large numbers and are quick to attack intruders.

Deros and other subterranean peoples sometimes keep shriezyx as guards or mounts, using food and threats of fire to keep them in line. Most shriezyx owners are quick to dispose of any eggs, fearing a swarm could grow beyond their control, but some breeders have realized that a shriezyx's offspring retain much of its parent's demeanor and save the eggs of the most loyal (or most fire-fearing) shriezyx in hopes of creating a profitable, easierto- control herd. While crueler masters might use shriezyx as test subjects, alchemists often see these creatures as beloved pets. Their grotesque webs make great alchemical reagents and provide ethically sourced flesh for experiments, while the molted exoskeleton can be used to create bombs and other explosives.

([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

</column>

</row>

</column>

---

**Melee**

**Damage** 2d6+5 piercing damage plus numbing toxin

**Melee**

claw +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 2d4+5 slashing damage

**Clicking Scurry** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The shriezyx [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) or [Climbs](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), and then makes a claw Strike

**Flesh Web** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The shriezyx's shoots a fleshy web at a target within 30 feet. The target must succeed at a DC 20 Reflex save or become [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) and exposed to numbing toxin. Due to the grotesque nature of the webbing, the target becomes [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 and can't reduce its sickened condition until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 20).

**Numbing Toxin**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 20 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d4 poison damage and [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** 1d6 poison damage and clumsy 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** 1d8 poison damage, clumsy 2, and [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 (1 round)

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Infestation Problems</title>

Shriezyx are resilient, and even if trapped in collapses, they can survive for an extraordinary length of time with little to eat. When a public works project in Magnimar revealed a cavern in the Irespan, swarms of shriezyx were let loose in the city. Since then, it's common practice to use specialized tools to listen for the telltale sounds of a shriezyx's clicking claws before demolishing any old, hollowed structures or bunkers.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Skaveling](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4545)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

One must perform a hideous ritual to make a skaveling. Though sometimes called ghoul bats, they're specifically crafted undead rather than true ghouls. The bloodsucking [urdefhans](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=629)  of the Darklands raise giant bats on a toxic fungus and the flesh of ghouls—specifically their brains. Upon reaching maturity, these giant bats are ritually slain via poison. While this rots away the flesh of most creatures, the specially prepared bats immediately rise from death as skavelings. 

Despite their tattered wings and sagging skin, skavelings are quite capable of flight, even when carrying a creature on their back. Urdefhans often use them as mounts. Their intelligence is more advanced than that of a typical giant bat, and in combat they behave more like allies than animals, capable of making tactical decisions.

[bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 15 feet, fly 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fangs +15,
**Damage** 2d8+8 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
wing +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 2d4+8 bludgeoning

**Bone-Chilling Screech** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The skaveling unleashes a horrifying screech that chills the very bones of those close enough to feel it. The screech can be heard for miles, but each creature in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must also attempt a DC 22 Will save. The skaveling can't use Bone-Chilling Screech again for 1d4 rounds. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Bone-Chilling Screech for 24 hours. **Success** The creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1. **Failure** The creature is frightened 2. **Critical Failure** The creature is frightened 2 and [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1 by fear.

**Consume Flesh** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The skaveling is adjacent to the corpse of a creature that died within the last hour; **Effect** The skaveling devours a chunk of the corpse and regains 1d6 Hit Points plus 1d6 for every 2 levels the skaveling has. They can regain Hit Points from any given corpse only once.

**Feast on Fear** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([occult Trigger The skaveling deals damage to a frightened creature with a fangs Strike](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) Effect The skaveling draws power from the fear infusing a creature's flesh. The frightened creature must attempt a DC 22 Fortitude saving throw. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected. **Success** The creature takes a –5-foot status penalty to its Speed, and the skaveling gains a +5-foot status bonus to their Speeds until the end of their next turn. **Failure** The creature takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speed, and the skaveling gains a +10-foot status bonus to their Speeds until the end of their next turn. **Critical Failure** The creature is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1, and the skaveling can immediately [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304), or [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) as a free action; this movement doesn't trigger reactions.

**Swift Dart** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) The skaveling [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) up to half their Speed. This movement doesn't trigger reactions.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Skaveling Intellect</title>

As a curious side effect of their creation, skavelings absorb many memories of the [ghoul](/Monsters.aspx?ID=218) brains on which they were fed. While merely an oversized animal in life, in undeath these memories coalesce into a strange form of intelligence that affords skavelings the ability to speak and reason, all the better to serve their urdefhan masters. Skavelings remain loyal to urdefhans and never take actions in a fight that would harm their creators.
</aside>
name: Skaveling

Created from the exoskeleton of a [giant stag beetle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2853), this mindless husk can cut a foe in half using its powerful mandibles. The abdomen of this crawling undead is empty, and more than one necromancer has used this space to hide valuable cargo.

**Resistances**
[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 5, [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) 5, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 5, piercing 5, slashing 5

<actions string="Single Action" />
foot +15,
**Damage** 2d6+7 bludgeoning

**Abdomen Cache**   The abdomen of a beetle carapace can be fitted with a simple hinge, allowing it to open and be used as storage. The abdomen can hold up to one Medium or smaller creature, a Large or smaller swarm, or a similar amount of cargo. The beetle or a creature stored in it can Interact to open the hatch. If the beetle carapace takes a critical hit by a bludgeoning weapon, roll a DC 10 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333). If the check fails, the cache is breached, and its contents spill out of the beetle. The hatch can also be [Forced Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375) (DC 22 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36)).

**[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** <actions string="Single Action" />  2d8+2 piercing, DC 24

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Crawling Cargo</title>

Undead make for the most common passengers inside a beetle carapace's abdomen, but swarms often pack tight inside the creatures. Insects tend to be more comfortable around undead than other types of animals. The following creatures make good options for combat encounters, in which they can be released at a dramatic moment for a grotesque surprise. 

[Army ant swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2825) 

[Centipede swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2876)

[Cockroach swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4301)

[Fen mosquito swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4481)

[Scorpion swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3176)

[Spider swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3206)

[Wasp swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3234)
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Skeletal Titan](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4550)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Only the powerful and foolish would raise the bones of a mighty titan or similarly gargantuan creature as a skeleton. Skilled practitioners see this as a waste of a powerful body and instead imbue them with magic that allows them to fly. The wise know it's nearly impossible to control such a colossus and that it's just as likely to crush its creator underneath its mighty foot as it is to smite their foes.

<column gap="tiny">

**Immunities**

**Resistances**
[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 10, [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) 10, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10, piercing 15, slashing 15

**Melee**

**Damage** 2d10+13 plus bone debris

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 31
- **Constant (7th)**
[Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534)

**Bone Debris**   The bones a skeletal titan throws are large enough to clutter the battlefield. When the skeletal titan hits a creature with a bone attack, the projectile becomes [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) in the square the creature occupies (or, if the creature occupies more than one square, one square it occupies of the titan's choice). If the titan misses with a bone attack, instead a random square adjacent to the creature becomes difficult terrain.

**Mountain Slam** <actions string="Three Actions" />  The skeletal titan slams its mountain sword into the ground. The shock wave reverberates, dealing 3d12+13 bludgeoning damage to all creatures in a 20-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature that fails its save is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).

**[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=81)** <actions string="Three Actions" />  Huge or smaller, foot, DC 33

</column>

name: Skeletal Titan

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Skull Peeler](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4551)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Skull peelers, despite their ferocious and well-deserved moniker, are considered by many to be downright adorable, especially when viewed from a safe distance. Their soft, dappled brown fur helps them hide in forest canopies, and they have shimmering insectile wings and big eyes that draw in the faintest beams of light. At first glance, a skull peeler looks like a cuddly pet or a wizard's familiar. Any illusions of domesticating such a beast are quickly dismissed upon seeing how a skull peeler eats, however.

Skull peelers have evolved to hang motionlessly in treetop canopies as they wait until appropriate prey appears, usually long-necked dinosaurs but also brachiating primates and large birds. The skull peeler then lashes out with its long tongue, severing the creature's head from its body and pulling the detached cranium back into its hungry paws. It then uses its claws to crack open the cranial cavity—hence its name—before lapping up the tasty insides.

Despite skull peelers' gruesome eating habits, some enterprises and individuals can't resist the urge to add these beasts to their menageries. [Fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=599) and other creatures with ties to the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=35), such as [gnomes](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=61), can occasionally coax a skull peeler into a form of domestication. While the adorable beasts can never be fully tamed due to their hyper-evolved hunting instincts, they can be bribed with food and, if kept satiated, displayed on a perch or indoor terrarium as an example of their master's cunning and deadliness. As often as not, these pseudo-domesticated skull peelers end up devouring a guest, pet, or their would-be owner, but this possibility doesn't stop up-and-coming crime lords from attempting to tame the little predators. Skull peelers kept in well-managed zoos fare somewhat better, but these clever creatures don't always stay in their cages, which has led to wild skull peelers in places travelers might not expect.

<column gap="tiny">

([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

</column>

</row>

**HP** 75

</row>

**Snatch Skull** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The skull peeler is using Perfect Camouflage, and a creature moves into a space within 15 feet of it; **Effect** The skull peeler [Leaps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) toward the triggering creature and Strikes with its tongue. If this Strike is successful, the skull peeler automatically [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) (page 361) the target with its tongue.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, climb 15 feet, fly 10 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tongue +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [fatal d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=597), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d4+8 slashing plus 1d8 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +17,
**Damage** 2d10+8 slashing

**Anticoagulant**   The skull peeler's razor-sharp tongue is coated in an anticoagulant substance that makes wounds it inflicts particularly hard to close. The DC of the [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to end the [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) from a skull peeler's tongue Strike is 16, or 11 with appropriate assistance.

**Perfect Camouflage** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Requirements** The skull peeler is in a treetop or standing on a tree limb; **Effect** Until the next time it acts, the skull peeler hangs perfectly still, blending into the treetop surroundings. It has an automatic result of 36 on [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks and DCs to Hide from any creature more than 10 feet away from it.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">A Grim Nature</title>

While explorers and adventurers who encounter skull peelers in the wild often assume the small beasts resulted from some ill-advised magical experiment by a foolish [wizard](/Classes.aspx?ID=39), they're actually a naturally occurring species. Though their unusual assembly of characteristics might carry some minor [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=35) influence, skull peelers evolved from other tree-dwelling mammals over thousands of years. In their current form, they're successful predators in their environment, mostly feeding on long-necked dinosaurs—prey that other, much larger carnivores still struggle to bring down!
</aside>
name: Skull Peeler

A slithering pit is a strange, nearly invisible ooze with an extradimensional space for its digestive system, which mimics the appearance of an acidfilled stone pit. It slowly dissolves its captives in stomach acid until they can be digested. A slithering pit can go weeks without feeding, affording it patience. Thriving in dilapidated areas, the slithering pit takes up position among the plentiful potholes where it can easily pass for just another blemish. It crawls across ramshackle cobblestone streets and damp underground complexes, waiting for unwary prey to stumble by and fall in. **[Recall Knowledge - Ooze](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Slithering Pit](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4552) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 293 **Perception** +9; no vision, [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +10 **Str** +7 **Dex** -5 **Con** +7 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 **Transparent** A slithering pit is so clear it's difficult to spot. A successful DC 30 [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) check is required to notice a stationary slithering pit, and a creature must be Searching to attempt this check. A creature that walks into the pit's space might fall into any active Dimensional Pit. --- **AC** 14 (10 from inside the Dimensional Pit) **Fort** +18 **Ref** +6 **Will** +11 **HP** 220 **Immunities** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [critical hits](/Rules.aspx?ID=2314), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) **Breach Vulnerability** Ingesting an extradimensional space like that found in a spacious pouch deals 6d8 force damage to the slithering pit and its occupants. The slithering pit then immediately uses Out You Go. --- **Speed** 10 feet **Melee** pseudopod +18 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+9 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Dimensional Pit** ([Extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=596), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The slithering pit opens an extradimensional, 20-foot-deep pit that covers its own space and all adjacent squares unless they're walls or similar blocking terrain. Any other creature occupying or entering pit spaces must succeed at a DC 22 Reflex save or [fall](/Rules.aspx?ID=2352) into the pit, taking damage from the fall (typically 10 bludgeoning damage). Any creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) by the slithering pit falls in and is no longer grabbed, even if it was outside the pit squares.
While a Dimensional Pit is open, the slithering pit is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81), can't be forced to move, and can make pseudopod Strikes originating from the walls of the pit. A creature that starts its turn at the bottom of the pit takes 2d6 acid damage. [Climbing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) the walls of the pit requires a DC 22 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check. When the slithering pit dies, the Dimensional Pit closes and creatures inside are ejected, with the effects of Out You Go. **Flurry of Pods** The slithering pit makes a single pseudopod [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) against each target within range it doesn't already have [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77). These attacks count toward the slithering pit's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after all of these attacks. **Out You Go** The slithering pit closes all pit spaces it created using Dimensional Pit, ejecting all its occupants onto the ground into random free spaces where the pit opened. Each occupant takes 4d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).
A Living Pit, Really? Slithering pit lore is as strange and confusing as the creature itself. Was it created by a [wizard](/Traits.aspx?ID=735) in need of a handy garbage disposal? Did it result from some unfortunate accident involving hungry [oozes](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=453) and one spacious pouch too many? Why do its insides mimic the appearance of stone, but without the same toughness? Is it some form of camouflage, letting them pass for an oftignored hazard? So many questions...
name: Slithering Pit

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Sea Snake](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4553)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These lithe snakes like to frequent the shallow waters of tropical seas. Their 4-foot-long, blue-green bodies easily blend into the water where they lurk to ambush prey. Sea snakes are highly venomous but often choose not to inject their venom when biting, so encounters with them rarely result in fatalities. 

Tales speak of massive sea snakes that swim in deeper waters and stalk ships, waiting for sailors to fall overboard or even climbing over the side to snatch them from the deck.

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A solitary snake might be no cause for alarm, but a hissing mass of frenzied snakes can make even seasoned adventurers shudder. Rat snakes can reach lengths of up to 10 feet, and they gather en masse to hibernate as well as to breed. Though nonvenomous, these territorial snakes will strike anything that threatens them.

The frightening mass of shining scales, gleaming eyes, and fangs dripping with venom that constitute a viper swarm has brought an end to many an unlucky adventurer. Those who are lucky may receive a warning strike without venom before being attacked in earnest.

These powerful serpents infest bogs and lowlands. Despite being over 16 feet long and weighing more than 200 pounds, they can climb trees in seconds. Emperor cobras ward off predators by flaring their hoods and hissing at attackers. Like many venomous snakes, they hunt by striking prey with their poison bite, retreating until their victims die, and then returning to swallow them whole. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Emperor Cobra](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4556) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 295 **Perception** +13; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +11 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +4 **Int** -4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -2 --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +11 **Will** +9 **HP** 80 --- **Speed** 25 feet, climb 25 feet, swim 25 feet **Melee** fangs +15 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+8 piercing plus emperor cobra venom **Emperor Cobra Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 22 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d8 poison damage (1 round); **Stage 2** 1d8 poison damage and [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (1 round); **Stage 3** 2d6 poison damage and drained 2 (1 round) **Flare Hood** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The emperor cobra flares its hood. Each non–emperor cobra creature within a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 22 Will save. The creature is then temporarily immune for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is frightened 3.
name: Emperor Cobra

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Soulrider](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4557)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Although soulriders can grow quite large, most of them are narrow creatures only a few feet long. Even once they've attached to a soul and passed between planes, they must complete their adaptation to the new environment before beginning their growth.

These smallest soulriders might be found anywhere in the mortal Universe with a portal to the [Outer Sphere](/Rules.aspx?ID=308) or a history of summoning magic. Large batches of spawn in the wilderness are often culled by natural predators, but individual spawn can survive for years and travel many miles as they search for a host.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 13

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Soulrider.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature -1">[Soulrider](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4557)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Tiny" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=523" />
<trait label="Soulrider" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=871" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 296

**Perception** +5; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +4, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +4

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +3

**Con** +2

**Int** -3

**Wis** +2

**Cha** -1
</row>

**Planar Adaptation** If the soulrider has followed a soul to its final destination, it takes on traits appropriate to that plane. These soulriders gain the [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=551) and [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600) and [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), or [monitor](/Traits.aspx?ID=655) traits as appropriate to the destination.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 14 

**Fort** +5 

**Ref** +8 

**Will** +2 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 8

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) 1

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, swim 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
sucker +6 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [reach 0 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** soul attach

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [reach 0 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [sanctified](/Traits.aspx?ID=519)),
**Damage** 1d4 bludgeoning plus 1 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)

**Propulsive Launch** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The soulrider [Leaps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) up to 40 feet, then makes a sucker [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306). If it's in the air and not attached to a creature after the Strike, it [falls](/Rules.aspx?ID=2352).

**Soul Attach**  ([Spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) When a soulrider succeeds at a sucker Strike against a target with a soul capable of facing judgment, its sucker attaches it to that soul. While attached, both the soulrider and the host creature are [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58), and the soulrider moves with its host until the soulrider dies or the host pulls it loose ([Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) DC 15). If the host dies while the soulrider is attached, the soulrider disappears immediately to follow the soul leaving the body. A creature returned to life before reaching its final destination generally returns with any attached soulrider.

**Tail Thrash** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** The soulrider is attached to a creature's soul; **Effect** The soulrider makes a tail Strike against the creature whose soul it's attached to, then one against another creature adjacent to the original target. These Strikes count towards the soulrider's multiple attack penalty, but it doesn't increase until after the second attack.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-620" />
name: Soulrider

markdown:
When soulriders absorb sufficient planar energy, they become gravid with countless tiny eggs. Such soulriders instinctively return to [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) via the Dead Roads to lay their spawn, although the exact metaphysical process has yet to be fully understood. [Fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=600) and others keen to enter the mortal world have begun capturing and studying spawning soulriders, though such experiments carry their own risks, for soulriders prevented from returning to the Universe will continue to grow larger as they absorb more energy. Once back in the Universe, spawning soulriders become fiercely protective of their near-translucent eggs and tiny spawn, using their remora-like maws to fend off potential threats. Anyone disturbing a soulrider in the process of spawning might find out what it feels like to have pieces of their soul torn away. Planar AdaptationsSpawning soulriders gain an additional ability based on their planar adaptation. Soulriders with the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) and [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=551) traits gain Celestial Flare. Soulriders with the [fiendish](/Traits.aspx?ID=600) and [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) traits gain Fiendish Lunge. Soulriders with the [monitor](/Traits.aspx?ID=655) trait gain Monitor Escape. Soulriders that followed a soul to a more esoteric plane have different abilities at the GM’s discretion, but such creatures are uncommon. **Celestial Flare** ([holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) Each enemy within 30 feet of the soulrider takes 2d6 spirit damage (DC 17 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). Creatures that fail the save are blinded for 1 round. The spawning soulrider can't use Celestial Flare again for 1 minute. **Fiendish Lunge** ([unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) The spawning soulrider [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) or [Swims](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) twice, making a tail Strike at any point during its movement. The Strike deals an additional 1d4 spirit damage. **Monitor Escape** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [teleport](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) The soulrider's form blurs as it exploits loopholes in the multiverse. It teleports to an empty space within 60 feet. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Spawning Soulrider](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4558) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 297 **Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +6 **Str** +1 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** -2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -1 **Planar Adaptation** A spawning soulrider has traits appropriate to the planar energy it's absorbed: [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=551) and [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600) and [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), or [monitor](/Traits.aspx?ID=655).
**Swim the Dead Roads** ([exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=595), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) In a process that takes 1 week, a spawning soulrider can travel through channels in the multiverse only it can sense, moving from the Outer Sphere plane whose energy it has absorbed to the Dead Roads that connect the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39) to the mortal Universe. From there, it travels to a random place in [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) that can support life. --- **AC** 15 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +7 **Will** +4 **HP** 20 --- **Speed** 20 feet, swim 20 feet **Melee** sucker +8 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** soul attach **Melee** tail +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [sanctified](/Traits.aspx?ID=519)), **Damage** 1d4+1 bludgeoning plus 1 spirit **Grind Soul** ([Sanctified](/Traits.aspx?ID=519), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) **Requirements** The spawning soulrider is attached to a creature's soul; **Effect** The soulrider grinds the creature's soul with its jagged inner mouth, dealing 2d8 spirit damage (DC 16 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). On a critical failure, the creature also takes 1d4 [persistent spirit damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). Regardless of the result, the spawning soulrider is no longer attached to the creature. **Propulsive Launch** The spawning soulrider [Leaps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) up to 40 feet, then makes a sucker [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167). If it's in the air and not attached to a creature after the [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306), it [falls](/Rules.aspx?ID=2352). **Soul Attach** ([Spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) When a spawning soulrider succeeds at a sucker [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) against a target with a soul capable of facing judgment, its sucker attaches it to that soul. While attached, both the spawning soulrider and the host creature are [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58), and the spawning soulrider moves with its host until the spawning soulrider dies or the host pulls it loose ([Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) DC 16). If the host dies while the spawning soulrider is attached, the spawning soulrider disappears immediately to follow the soul leaving the body. A creature returned to life before reaching its final destination generally returns with any attached spawning soulrider. **Tail Thrash** **Requirements** The spawning soulrider is attached to a creature's soul; **Effect** The spawning soulrider makes a tail Strike against the creature whose soul it's attached to, then one against another creature adjacent to the original target. These Strikes count towards the spawning soulrider's multiple attack penalty, but it doesn't increase until after the second attack.
name: Spawning Soulrider

markdown:
Of all Rovagug's spawn, the creature known as Volnagur is the one that has most distanced itself from the Dead Vault at Golarion's core, instead making its home among the skies. Volnagur possesses many wings, and they're mismatched, asymmetrical, and uncoordinated, yet the massive spawn is capable of powerful flight that carries it as far as Arcadia, Tian Xia, and even Sarusan. Though capable of wreaking great destruction with both its many razor-sharp tongues and powerful blasts of energy from its four eyes, Volnagur's most fearsome ability is the discordant song produced by the precise angling of its wings against the wind, which drives all who hear it to violence, setting neighbor against neighbor. Though not spotted for decades, astronomers have recently located a satellite in the upper bounds of Golarion's atmosphere, where Volnagur hangs motionless. As it drifts from continent to continent, its eyes seem to alternately turn to the moon, then back to Golarion, as if waiting for a signal. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 54 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 52 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 49 [Volnagur](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4559) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 299 **Perception** +39; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +42, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +45 **Str** +9 **Dex** +11 **Con** +11 **Int** -2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +7 **Slumbering Armageddon** Volnagur's slumber increases the rate of wildlife attacks, swarms of vermin and pests, and violent crime. --- **AC** 48 **Fort** +39 **Ref** +36 **Will** +33 **HP** 515 , regeneration absolute 25 **Immunities** [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697), [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) **Absolute Regeneration** Volnagur's regeneration can be deactivated if a choir of no less than 12 exquisitely skilled and inspired individuals sings a song of beginnings and hope over its corpse for 1 year and 1 day without pause or flaw.
**Alien Harmonics** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) 60 feet. Volnagur constantly emits a cacophony that drowns out sound and thought while reinforcing Volnagur's song. Creatures that enter the aura must attempt a DC 43 Fortitude save or Volnagur's song becomes all they can hear for as long as they remain within the aura, making creatures [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) against all sources other than Volnagur. On a critical failure, the effect is permanent, and the cacophony rings in the target's ears regardless of range. The aura also attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) any [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) effect, any effect that would provide resistance or immunity to auditory or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) effects, or any effect that would create silence (counteract rank 10, counteract modifier +33).
**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 300 feet, DC 42
**Reactive** Volnagur gains 3 reactions each round. It can still use only one reaction per trigger
**Intercepting Eyes** **Trigger** Volnagur is targeted by a ranged attack; **Requirements** Volnagur is aware of the attack and not [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to it; **Effect** One of Volnagur's eyes fixes on the attack, shooting it down with an eye beam. The attack becomes a critical failure.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 20 feet, fly 80 feet **Melee** jaws +41 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+24 piercing plus endsong and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** razor tongues +41 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [reach 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+24 slashing plus endsong and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Ranged** eye beam +41 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=765)), **Damage** 6d12 sonic plus endsong **Endsong** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=765)) Subtle vibrations laced under Volnagur's attacks confuse its targets and fill their minds with the desire to destroy. A creature damaged by one of Volnagur's Strikes must attempt a DC 45 Will save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and is temporarily immune for 24 hours or until it takes damage from Scream of the End.
**Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** The target is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1d4 rounds. It never attempts to attack Volnagur or another creature affected by endsong.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target is confused for 1 hour. While confused, its Strikes resonate with Volnagur's song, dealing an additional 1d6 sonic damage and forcing the target to save against endsong. **Gaze Upon** Volnagur makes an eye beam Strike against every creature in a 120-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). These attacks count toward Volnagur's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after Volnagur makes all its attacks. It can't use Gaze Upon again for 1d4 rounds, but until the start of Volnagur's next turn, it can use Intercepting Eyes as a free action. **Scream of the End** ([Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) **Requirements** Volnagur has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90); **Effect** Volnagur holds a creature close to its eyes before blasting the creature at point-blank range. Volnagur deals 23d6 sonic damage to the target (DC 46 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Regardless of the save's result, the target is no longer grabbed or restrained, is pushed 120 feet away from Volnagur, and falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
name: Volnagur
