markdown:
Sorvuth-ka is the youngest of Rovagug's spawn. It has never been seen eating; lacking the ravenous hunger observed in the other Spawn, it seems to destroy simply for the pleasure of it, approaching each of its attacks with novel, inventive cruelty. Sorvuth-ka might pause while destroying a town to give the populace just enough time to flee, only for them to realize in despair that their only route of escape is a mountain pass that Sorvuthka has already collapsed. However, the creature grows bored easily, swiftly dispatching prey with bladed limbs if its games stop going to plan or take too long to come to fruition. Curiously, despite Sorvuth-ka's regenerative abilities—powerful even for a Spawn and capable of rendering it resistant to attacks that have injured it— it has several wounds that refuse to heal, most notably a great gash across its face. Some scholars theorize that these wounds are some cruel design of Rovagug, the better to allow access to the amber blood that is Sorvuth-ka's primary instrument of destruction. Others take it as proof of the existence of a weapon capable of ending the threat of the Spawn of Rovagug once and for all. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 58 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 56 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 53 [Sorvuth-Ka](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4560) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 300 **Perception** +42; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +45, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +45, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +41, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +45 **Str** +12 **Dex** +9 **Con** +12 **Int** +10 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +5 **Slumbering Armageddon** Sorvuth-ka's slumber accelerates erosion and weathering, timed to always break at the point of maximum harm via rockslides, sinkholes, treefalls, and other collapses. --- **AC** 52 **Fort** +42 **Ref** +38 **Will** +36 **HP** 550 , regeneration absolute 25 **Immunities** adaptive defenses, [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) **Absolute Regeneration** Sorvuth-ka's regeneration can be deactivated by slaying it with a weapon made from the bones of Chemnosit, Kothogaz, Ulunat, [Volnagur](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4559), and [Xotani](/Monsters.aspx?ID=491).
**Adaptive Defenses** When injured, Sorvuth-ka's body adapts to ensure that the triggering insult can't harm it again. Immediately after it takes damage, it becomes immune to that type of damage. It can become immune to three different types of damage in this way, with newer immunities replacing older ones.
**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 300 feet, DC 45
**Reactive** Sorvuth-ka gains 3 reactions each round. It can still use only one reaction per trigger
**Bleed Destruction** **Trigger** Sorvuth-ka takes physical damage; **Effect** Amber blood spurts from Sorvuth-ka's wound, creating a blood pool in a square adjacent to Sorvuth-ka. The blood remains in the area until removed or it dries, which typically takes 1 day.
**[Reactive Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2256)** --- **Speed** 60 feet **Melee** claw +44 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+27 slashing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Ranged** crystallized blood +44 ([Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 5d6+21 slashing **Amber Strikes** **Requirements** Sorvuth-ka's previous action was a successful [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) against the target; **Effect** After landing a Strike, Sorvuth-ka commands it blood to continue the assault, choosing one of the three following options: Crystallize, Inject, or Splash. If the previous attack was a critical hit, Amber Strikes is a free action.
  • _Crystallize_ Sorvuth-ka's blood flows around the target's limbs before hardening. The creature must succeed at a DC 48 Reflex save or become [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) and [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296). If the creature was [flying](/Rules.aspx?ID=3374), it [falls](/Rules.aspx?ID=2352).
  • _Inject_ Sorvuth-ka's blood invades the target through its wounds. The target must succeed at a DC 48 Fortitude save or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 2 (sickened 3 on a critical failure). As long as the target is sickened, Sorvuth-ka's blood will attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) any effect that could restore Hit Points to the target (counteract rank 10, counteract modifier +38).
  • _Splash_ Sorvuth-ka's blood splashes violently, [dazzling](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) the target until the end of its next turn and creating a blood pool in a square adjacent to the target
**Detonate Blood** **Requirements** A pool of Sorvuth-ka's blood is within 500 feet; **Effect** Sorvuth-ka's blood detonates into crystalline amber flechettes. Every creature either in the required blood pool's square or in a 10- foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) of that pool other than Sorvuth-ka takes 20d8 piercing damage (DC 48 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). On a critical failure, any [resistances](/Rules.aspx?ID=2893) to physical damage the creature has are reduced by 10 for 1 minute. **Rough Rampage** **Requirements** Sorvuth-ka has at least one creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90); **Effect** Sorvuth-ka Strides, dragging any creatures it's grabbed or restrained along with it. Each grabbed or restrained creature takes 11d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 48 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). On a failure, the creature is also [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 2 (or clumsy 3 on a critical failure) until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296).
name: Sorvuth-Ka

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Dream Spider](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4561)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A dream spider's webs have an iridescent hue and are infused with the same hallucinogenic compound as the creature's toxin. Originally denizens of tropical jungles, dream spiders have adapted well to temperate environments, particularly thriving among the rooftops of cities where shady alchemists use their venom to produce addictive drugs.

**Dream Spider Venom**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 16 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 4 rounds; **Stage 1** [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** 1d6 poison damage plus [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 (1 round)

**Web Trap**   A creature hit by the dream spider's web attack is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) and stuck to the nearest surface until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 16).

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Venom Addicts</title>

Talented alchemists process dream spider venom into an addictive drug. Those who become addicted can be driven in desperation to seek out dream spiders and allow the creatures to bite their flesh to get their fix—an arrangement that backfires horribly as the spiders feed.
</aside>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These terrifying creatures grow as large as elephants. The placement of their eyes above their wide mandibles evokes the grimacing visage of an ogre's leer. Ogres find ogre spider faces simultaneously amusing and adorable, but in most cases, ogres' attempts to keep these spiders as pets result in dead ogres and well-fed spiders.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Spiral Centurion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4563)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These mechanical constructs were created to serve as guardians in an ancient and bygone era, although exactly who made them and the secrets of their construction have long since been lost to history. From the waist up, they resemble humanoids made of metal, but from the waist down, their bodies take the form of spinning metal tops ringed with blades that excel at cutting down nearby foes. Most spiral centurions can be directed to stand down with a password, but often these command phrases have been lost to the mists of time. In rare cases, a spiral centurion can also wield manufactured weapons or a shield in addition to its built-in weapons, giving it access to additional actions besides those listed below.

Most spiral centurions are hundreds or even thousands of years old, only staying functional because of the powerful magic used in their creation. Still, millennia of neglect have caused many spiral centurions to develop small glitches or malfunctions.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 28

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/SpiralCenturion.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 11">[Spiral Centurion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4563)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Construct" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=563" />
<trait label="Mindless" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=652" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 303

**Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +23, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +23

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +6

**Con** +5

**Int** -5

**Wis** +2

**Cha** -5
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 31 

**Fort** +22 

**Ref** +25 

**Will** +16 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 170

**Hardness**
10
</row>

**Immunities**
[bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [nonlethal attacks](/Rules.aspx?ID=2311), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)

**Top-Heavy** A spiral centurion's top-like design makes it susceptible to effects that would cause it to fall [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). The DC of any attempt to knock the spiral centurion prone is reduced by 5. If the spiral centurion attempts a check or saving throw to resist being knocked prone, it takes a –5 status penalty. A spiral centurion that has been knocked prone can't use any actions other than to attempt to [Stand](/Actions.aspx?ID=2303), but it must succeed at a DC 30 [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) check to do so.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
blade +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)),
**Damage** 2d12+12 slashing

**Hurl Blade** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The spiral centurion hurls one of its blades with an angled spin to ensure a swooping flight path. The blade deals 6d6 slashing damage to each creature in a 40-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). At the start of the spiral centurion's next turn, the blade swoops around and returns along the same flight path, again dealing 6d6 slashing damage (DC 30 basic Reflex save) to each creature along the same line.

**Rev Up** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The spiral centurion hasn't acted yet this turn; **Effect** The spiral centurion [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to its Speed. It then gains a +2 circumstance bonus to attack and damage rolls until the end of its turn.

**[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=81)** <actions string="Three Actions" />  Medium or smaller, blade, DC 30

**Whirling Death** <actions string="Three Actions" />  The spiral centurion spins furiously in place, its blades extended to slice through nearby creatures. It makes up to five melee blade Strikes. No single creature can be targeted by more than one blade Strike during one use of this ability. These attacks count toward the spiral centurion's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after all the attacks are made.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Spiral Centurion Glitches</title>

A spiral centurion might have one of the following glitches. 

**Dulled Blades** Its blade Strike deals only 2d10+5 bludgeoning damage. 

**Misaligned Gears** It loses Rev Up, and if it Strides more than once per round, it takes 1d10 damage. 

**Stuck in a Rut** It uses the same actions each round, regardless of the circumstances.
</aside>
name: Spiral Centurion

Spirit guides of cunning are often seen as the weakest of the spirit guides, but they're also the most numerous and proactive, guiding families to safe paths or leaving food and water for warriors. Cunning guides often take the form of foxes. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 15 **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Cunning Fox](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4564) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 304 **Perception** +9; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +6, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +8, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +5 **Str** +1 **Dex** +3 **Con** +0 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 16 **Fort** +5 **Ref** +8 **Will** +7 **HP** 15 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision **Resistances** all damage 2 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), or [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737); double resistance vs. non-magical) --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** horn +6 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 1d8+1 force **Melee** jaws +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 1d4+1 force **Primal Innate Spells** DC 17 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689) - **1st** [Cleanse Cuisine](/Spells.aspx?ID=1468), [Detect Poison](/Spells.aspx?ID=1486) - **3rd** [Wanderer’s Guide](/Spells.aspx?ID=2043) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Bond with Mortal** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The spirit guide forms a bond with a mortal creature. While the bond exists, the spirit guide increases their current and maximum Hit Points by 10, gains a +2 status bonus to their attack and damage rolls, and can communicate telepathically with the bonded mortal as long as the two beings are on the same plane. The spirit guide can only be bonded with one mortal at a time, and they can take this action again to end the bond or to form a new bond (which also ends the old bond). The bond also ends if the spirit guide or the mortal dies.
This bond strengthens the spirit guide's connection to [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26). While bonded, the spirit guide loses the incorporeal and spirit traits, loses their immunities and resistances, and changes their Strikes to deal the appropriate physical damage (typically piercing or slashing) instead of force damage. **Bonded Strike** **Requirements** The spirit guide is currently Bonded with a Mortal; **Effect** The spirit guide makes a jaws Strike. If this attack hits, the bonded mortal can spend their reaction to Strike the same target.
name: Cunning Fox

markdown:
Born from spirits possessing the qualities of strength, endurance, and ferocity, these spirit guides act as martial guardians for mortals under their care. Most have a burning hatred for demons, devils, and other fiends who prey on mortal weaknesses. However, they also display care and consideration around mortal children, who in turn seem to instinctively recognize the beings as protectors. Strength takes many forms, some evident in their power—like the feathered bear—and some unassuming. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 27 **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Feathered Bear](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4565) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 305 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +20, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +16 **Str** +7 **Dex** +2 **Con** +5 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +16 **Will** +18 **HP** 160 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision **Resistances** all damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), or [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737); double resistance vs. non-magical) **Guardian's Aegis** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) 30 feet. All allies within 30 feet of the feathered bear gain a +1 status bonus to saves against magical effects. The bonus increases to +2 if the effect originated from a [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600).
**Avenging Claws** **Trigger** A creature within 10 feet damages the feathered bear's ally with a melee attack; **Effect** The feathered bear immediately [Steps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) toward the triggering attacker and makes a claws Strike. --- **Speed** 35 feet **Melee** jaws +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d10+9 force **Melee** claw +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d6+9 force plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 27 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Know the Way](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582) - **3rd** [Environmental Endurance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1517), [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553), [Jump](/Spells.aspx?ID=1580), [Oaken Resilience](/Spells.aspx?ID=1617) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Bond with Mortal** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The spirit guide forms a bond with a mortal creature. While the bond exists, the spirit guide increases their current and maximum Hit Points by 20, gains a +2 status bonus to their attack and damage rolls, and can communicate telepathically with the bonded mortal as long as the two beings are on the same plane. The spirit guide can only be bonded with one mortal at a time, and they can take this action again to end the bond or to form a new bond (which also ends the old bond). The bond also ends if the spirit guide or the mortal dies.
This bond strengthens the spirit guide's connection to the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26). While bonded, the spirit guide loses the [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) and [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) traits, loses their immunities and resistances, and changes their Strikes to deal the appropriate physical damage (typically piercing or slashing) instead of force damage. **Bonded Strike** **Requirements** The spirit guide is currently Bonded with a Mortal; **Effect** The spirit guide makes a jaws Strike. If this attack hits, the bonded mortal can spend their reaction to Strike the same target. **Mauler** The feathered bear gains a +4 circumstance bonus to damage rolls against creatures they've [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77).
name: Feathered Bear

A swarm of sportlebores is a much more dangerous foe than a single insect. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Sportlebore Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4566) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 306 **Perception** +13; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17 **Str** +2 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** -4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +17 **Will** +13 **HP** 85 **Immunities** precision, [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=78) **Resistances** bludgeoning 3, piercing 7, slashing 7 **Weaknesses** area damage 7, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 7 **Pour Down Throat** **Trigger** A creature in the sportlebore swarm's area speaks, Casts a Spell, or opens its mouth; **Effect** A portion of the sportlebore swarm attempts to surge down the throat of the triggering creature, who must attempt a DC 25 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature gets a mouthful of sportlebores. They spit the insects out and avoid further damage, but they can't speak for 1 round, and if they were performing a spellcasting action, the spell fails and the caster wastes the action.
**Failure** The creature takes 4d6 piercing damage from sportlebore bites, can't speak for 1 round, and loses a spell as noted under success.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature is also exposed to sportlebore infestation. --- **Speed** 35 feet, fly 35 feet **Swarming Bites** Each creature in the sportlebore swarm's area takes 3d6 piercing damage (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). **Sportlebore Infestation** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) **Saving Throw** DC 22 Fortitude; **Stage 1** carrier with no ill effect (1 day); **Stage 2** [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 (1 hour); **Stage 3** enfeebled 2 (1 hour); **Stage 4** 4d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save) as the host painfully vomits out a sportlebore swarm and returns to stage 1
name: Sportlebore Swarm

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Stygira](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4569)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These scarred, eyeless creatures appear as withered hermits wrapped in tattered rags. They can command strange secrets of the earth and interpret the fateful energies of the subterranean depths. In some regions, stygiras are worshipped as seers or even gods, although they lack the ability to grant spells to clerics and often aren't aware of their worshippers at all. In other areas, they have strange ties to the ancient empires of the cyclopes, frequently dwelling in the oversized ruins those creatures left behind long ago. To many stygiras, gemstones harvested from ancient cyclopean mosaics have even greater magical properties than other crystals. 

Although technically blind, stygiras do have vestigial eyes hidden beneath the stony, scarred flesh of their faces. Capable of sensing bright lights even through their scars, stygiras are sickened and distracted by these flashing glimpses, so they keep to their caves during the day and only wander into the world above after nightfall. Far from benevolent, stygiras often waylay unwary travelers so they can reduce their victims down to the base chemicals and supernatural humors required to infuse their gemstones with the capacity to give them sight and magical power.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 25

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Stygira.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 7">[Stygira](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4569)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=585" />
<trait label="Fey" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=599" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 308

**Perception** +17; gemsight

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Cyclops](/Languages.aspx?ID=30), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=162), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +15, [Gem Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +17, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +17, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +17

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +4

**Con** +5

**Int** +4

**Wis** +6

**Cha** +2
</row>

**Gemsight** As long as the stygira holds a gemstone, they can see through the gem with [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) and the effects of [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727). A stygira is blind when they aren't holding a gem in a hand.

**Items**
[gemstone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2359) (worth 25 gp)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 25 

**Fort** +15 

**Ref** +13 

**Will** +19 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 80

</row>

**Immunities**
[paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)

**Resistances**
physical 10 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915))

**Light Sickness** A stygira in an area of [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 2d6+10 slashing plus stone curse

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 25
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Know the Way](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646)
- **2nd**
[Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445)
- **3rd**
[Clairaudience](/Spells.aspx?ID=1465), [Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1506)
- **4th**
[Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1466), [Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=1647), [Shape Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=1667)

**Gem Gaze** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The stygira holds aloft a gem and gazes into the mind of a creature within 30 feet, infusing the creature's thoughts with visions of its own dead body slowly petrifying. The creature must succeed at a DC 25 Will save or become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 (frightened 2 on a critical failure).

**Stone Curse**  ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) Wounds dealt by the stygira's claws leave the flesh bleached of color and turn the blood that runs from them dark gray. Each time a creature is damaged by the stygira's claw Strike, it must succeed at a DC 25 Fortitude save or become permanently [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 (slowed 2 on a critical failure) as its flesh stiffens like stone. If a creature is reduced to 0 Hit Points from the stygira's claw Strike and fails the saving throw against stone curse, it's [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87). A creature that spends 8 hours in direct sunlight can attempt a new saving throw to remove the effects of stone curse, even if it has been petrified.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Stygira Gems</title>

The gem a stygira carries doesn't need to be particularly valuable, but some stygiras prefer to use more expensive gems as an affectation. More powerful stygiras have developed methods of using particularly expensive gems to enhance their Gem Gaze ability, allowing some to charm those they gaze upon, light their enemies on fire, or even afflict creatures with debilitating poison.
</aside>
name: Stygira

markdown:
Sumbreivas are the Void's unstoppable hunters, tracking down and destroying other creatures on their plane for sport and practice. Occasionally, they pass through a rift or are brought to the Universe via a binding circle, where they collect living souls to display as trophies. Sumbreivas gather at Night Lodges, where they train and display their soul trophies, which appear as floating wisps of blue energy. The more formidable the soul, the more intense the blue glow that emanates from it. Sumbreivas in lodges periodically raid [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) on a Night Hunt and compete to see who can bring back the most brilliant soul trophies. The winner of the Night Hunt leads the lodge until the next hunt. Night Lodges are ranked against each other by the accomplishments of the hunters within. All sumbreivas desire to capture a soul powerful enough to earn them placement in the Twilight Lodge, reserved for the truly elite souls and hunters. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 35 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 [Sumbreiva](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4570) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 309 **Perception** +29; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +28, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +30, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +35, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +29 **Str** +8 **Dex** +9 **Con** +3 **Int** +6 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +33 **Will** +27 **HP** 290 ([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) **Hunter's Triumph** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** The sumbreiva kills a creature; **Effect** The sumbreiva lets out a triumphant, bone-chilling howl. Every enemy in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must succeed at a DC 36 Will save or become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 3 (and [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) as long as it's frightened on a critical failure).
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 50 feet **Melee** sumbreiva huntblade +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 3d8+16 piercing plus huntblade brutality **Melee** shadow whip +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)), **Damage** 3d4+16 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Ranged** sumbreiva huntblade +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 3d8+16 piercing plus huntblade brutality **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 36 - **4th** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480), [Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1506) **Claim Trophy** The sumbreiva claims the soul of a creature they killed within the last minute. This works like seize soul, except that no black sapphire is required, and the soul is turned into a glowing blue light called a soul trophy. Anyone who kills the sumbreiva can then free the soul from any soul trophy by touching it and speaking the word for “freedom” in any language. **Huntblade Brutality** The sumbreiva's huntblade deals an additional 2d8 precision damage to [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76), or [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures. **Whip Drain** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571)) **Requirements** The sumbreiva has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) with their shadow whip; **Effect** The grabbed creature must succeed at a DC 38 Fortitude save or become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 2 (drained 3 on a critical failure). If the creature is already drained, this increases its drained value instead, to a maximum of drained 4.
Superior Sumbreivas As sumbreiva hunters attain souls, they grow in power and physically transform. They might increase in stature, grow more limbs or great leathery wings, or form advanced armaments suited to their personalities and hunting methods. These outward manifestations make it easy for other sumbreivas to distinguish superior warriors from the less masterful hunters. The greatest sumbreivas are said to be inexorable giants suited for hunting only [kaiju](/Traits.aspx?ID=383), demigods, and the [spawn of Rovagug](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=621).
name: Sumbreiva

markdown:
When a warrior's last thoughts are of bloodlust, their body can become so suffused with rage that it infects whatever eats their corpse. This singular emotion binds together the resultant swarm, combining the insects' will to survive with a berserker's deathless rage, creating an indomitable combatant. Often made of roaches or similarly tenacious insects, skittering slayers rarely develop long-term plans, instead scuttling about in search of dark refuges and passionate fights. These swarm striders tend to avoid transforming into their swarm shape, preferring to face their foes directly whenever possible. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Skittering Slayer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4571) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 311 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +16 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 **Items** [Chain Mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=46), _[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [ flail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=374)_, [Light Hammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=384) (3) --- **AC** 26 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) **Fort** +18 **Ref** +16 **Will** +13 **HP** 130 **Immunities** precision, [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=78) **Resistances** physical 5, poison 5 **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 10 **Agitated by Light** When exposed to bright light, the skittering slayer must attempt a DC 25 Will save. On a failure, they become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 (frightened 2 on a critical failure). The skittering slayer then becomes immune to being agitated by light for 10 minutes.
**Discorporate** When the skittering slayer is reduced to 0 HP, their constituent creatures collapse, scattering on the ground under their space and each adjacent square. If even one of the creatures gets away, the skittering slayer can eventually re-form over 1d10 days (potentially longer in areas where there are few invertebrates).
The scattered invertebrates must be destroyed within 1 round to destroy the skittering slayer permanently. The invertebrates have a collective pool of HP, typically equal to 32 HP, and the same AC, saves, immunities, resistances, and weaknesses as the skittering slayer. The invertebrates can't take actions but they escape automatically once the round elapses. At the GM's discretion, clever means of trapping or eliminating the creatures might be sufficient to destroy the skittering slayer.
**Scatter** **Trigger** The skittering slayer is targeted by a splash attack or would attempt a Reflex save against an area effect; **Effect** The skittering slayer scatters in place, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to AC and Reflex saves against the triggering effect. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** _flail_ +20 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708), [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)), **Damage** 2d6+11 bludgeoning plus clinging remnants **Melee** light hammer +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d6+11 bludgeoning plus clinging remnants **Melee** fist +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+11 bludgeoning plus clinging remnants **Ranged** light hammer +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 1d6+11 bludgeoning plus clinging remnants **Clinging Remnants** A skittering slayer's melee Strikes and ranged Strikes made against targets within their weapon's first range increment deposit biting vermin on the target, dealing 2d4 [persistent piercing damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). **Draw Bugs** ([Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623)) The skittering slayer draws more arthropods from the environment around them to reconstitute some of their damaged body. They regain 10 HP. At the GM's discretion, the skittering slayer doesn't recover HP in areas where there aren't enough arthropods to call to themselves. **Swarm Shape** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) The skittering slayer collapses into a shapeless swarm of their constituent creatures. They drops all items in their possession. In this form, the skittering slayer can't use attack actions and can't cast spells, but they can move through areas small enough for their constituent creatures to fit without having to [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372). They can use the same action to coalesce from their swarm shape back into their normal form.
name: Skittering Slayer

markdown:
Even though they believe they'll dwell with their deity in the afterlife, when nearing death, some priests desperately call out to any gods willing to listen. The worms that consume such an ex-priest's flesh absorb these prayers, transforming into a worm prophet, a pious swarm strider that might worship hundreds of gods without calling any one its patron, perhaps collecting dozens of religious symbols or haphazardly tearing pages out of various religious texts and binding them into a contradictory new scripture. These creatures often lose whatever edicts and anathema bound them in life and instead hungrily seek to convert others to their hodgepodge faith—or “indoctrinate” victims by consuming those who won't join willingly. Worm prophets don't typically have reservations with entering their swarm form whenever they see fit. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Worm Prophet](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4572) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 312 **Perception** +23; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +20, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +24, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +22, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +25, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +22 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +6 **Items** [silver religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2745) (10), _[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [warhammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=403)_ --- **AC** 32 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) **Fort** +20 **Ref** +20 **Will** +25 **HP** 160 **Immunities** precision, [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=78), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Resistances** physical 10, poison 10, [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) 10 **Weaknesses** area damage 10, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 10 **Discorporate** When the worm prophet is reduced to 0 HP, their constituent creatures collapse, scattering on the ground under their space and each adjacent square. If even one of the creatures gets away, the worm prophet can eventually re-form over 1d10 days (potentially longer in areas where there are few invertebrates).
The scattered invertebrates must be destroyed within 1 round to destroy the worm prophet permanently. The invertebrates have a collective pool of HP, typically equal to 40 HP, and the same AC, saves, immunities, resistances, and weaknesses as the worm prophet. The invertebrates can't take actions but they escape automatically once the round elapses. At the GM's discretion, clever means of trapping or eliminating the creatures might be sufficient to destroy the worm prophet. --- **Speed** 25 feet, burrow 15 feet **Melee** _warhammer_ +24 ([Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)), **Damage** 2d8+11 bludgeoning plus clinging remnants **Melee** fist +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+1 bludgeoning plus clinging remnants **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 33, attack +25 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671) - **4th** [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=1499), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534), [Talking Corpse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712) - **5th** [Crisis of Faith](/Spells.aspx?ID=1478), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Spiritual Guardian](/Spells.aspx?ID=1688) - **6th** [Blessed Boundary](/Spells.aspx?ID=1452), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Vampiric Exsanguination](/Spells.aspx?ID=1735) **A Thousand Mouths in Prayer** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623)) The worm prophet's constituent creatures whisper countless paeans to their gods. The worm prophet attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) (counteract modifier +24, counteract rank 6) an effect on a creature within 30 feet that's imposing one of the following conditions: [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59), [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65), [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76), [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94). Once the worm prophet successfully counteracts an effect in this way, it can't do so again for 1d4 rounds. **Clinging Remnants** A worm prophet's melee Strikes and ranged Strikes made against targets within their weapon's first range increment deposit biting vermin on the target, dealing 3d4 [persistent piercing damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). **Draw Bugs** ([Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623)) The worm prophet draws more arthropods from the environment around them to reconstitute some of their damaged body. They regain 15 HP. At the GM's discretion, the skittering slayer doesn't recover HP in areas where there aren't enough arthropods to call to themselves. **Squirming Embrace** The worm prophet [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305). If they end their movement sharing a space with a creature, they deal 4d6 piercing damage to the creature, with a DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The worm prophet can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310) instead of Striding. **Swarm Shape** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) The worm prophet collapses into a shapeless swarm of their constituent creatures. They drops all items in their possession. In this form, the worm prophet can't use attack actions and can't cast spells, but they can move through areas small enough for their constituent creatures to fit without having to [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372). They can use the same action to coalesce from their swarm shape back into their normal form.
name: Worm Prophet

Good help is hard to find. For a master thief unable to find competent accomplices, sometimes the only way to execute the perfect heist involves becoming an entire guild of tiny creatures. Those who purposefully transform in this way favor scuttling creatures such as centipedes or spiders. On occasion, a thousand thieves instead forms when a community of pack rats discovers a dead scoundrel's body and fights over his ill-gotten gains. These swarm striders make use of their swarm form to enter homes, vaults, and other targets with ease. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Thousand Thieves](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4573) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 313 **Perception** +29; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=166) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +30, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +28, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +26, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +28, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +32, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +32, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +30 **Str** +4 **Dex** +8 **Con** +7 **Int** +6 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 **Items** _[+2 greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [ dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358)_, [sterling thieves' toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2758) --- **AC** 40 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) **Fort** +25 **Ref** +31 **Will** +27 **HP** 220 **Immunities** precision, [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=78) **Resistances** physical 15, poison 15 **Weaknesses** area damage 15, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 15 **Discorporate** When the thousand thieves is reduced to 0 HP, their constituent creatures collapse, scattering on the ground under their space and each adjacent square. If even one of the creatures gets away, the thousand thieves can eventually re-form over 1d10 days (potentially longer in areas where there are few invertebrates).
The scattered invertebrates must be destroyed within 1 round to destroy the thousand thieves permanently. The invertebrates have a collective pool of HP, typically equal to 55 HP, and the same AC, saves, immunities, resistances, and weaknesses as the thousand thieves. The invertebrates can't take actions but they escape automatically once the round elapses. At the GM's discretion, clever means of trapping or eliminating the creatures might be sufficient to destroy the thousand thieves. --- **Speed** 35 feet, climb 30 feet **Melee** _dagger_ +32 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 3d4+10 piercing plus clinging remnants and liquid delirium **Melee** fist +32 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+10 bludgeoning plus clinging remnants and liquid delirium **Ranged** _dagger_ +32 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 3d4+10 piercing plus clinging remnants and liquid delirium **Ranged** vermin dart +30 ([range increment 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d8+10 piercing plus clinging remnants and liquid delirium **Occult Innate Spells** DC 37 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Know the Way](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715) - **3rd** [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) (at will) - **6th** [Detect Scrying](/Spells.aspx?ID=1487), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577), [Knock](/Spells.aspx?ID=1581) **Clinging Remnants** A thousand thieves's melee Strikes and ranged Strikes made against targets within their weapon's first range increment deposit biting vermin on the target, dealing 4d4 [persistent piercing damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). **Draw Bugs** ([Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623)) The thousand thieves draws more arthropods from the environment around them to reconstitute some of their damaged body. They regain 20 HP. At the GM's discretion, the thousand thieves doesn't recover HP in areas where there aren't enough arthropods to call to themselves. **Liquid Delirium** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 37 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 4d6 poison and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** 4d6 poison and stupefied 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** 4d6 poison, stupefied 2, and [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) by a random object (1 round); **Stage 4** [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) with no [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) check to wake up (1 round) **Scuttling Shift** The thousand thieves reverts to a swarm using Swarm Getaway, [Sneaks](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) up to their Speed, coalesces into their normal form, and [Hides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404). This movement doesn't trigger reactions. **Sneak Attack** The thousand thieves deals an additional 3d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures. **Squirming Injection** The thousand thieves [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305). If they end their movement sharing a space with a creature, they deal dealing 6d6 piercing damage (DC 37 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) and exposing the target to liquid delirium. The thousand thieves can [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) instead of Striding. **Swarm Getaway** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) The thousand thieves collapses into a shapeless swarm of their constituent creatures. They drops all items in their possession but up to 3 Bulk of held, worn, or carried objects. In this form, the thousand thieves can't use attack actions and can't cast spells, but they can move through areas small enough for their constituent creatures to fit without having to [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372). As the swarm moves, the thousand thieves carries their objects if they can fit through the spaces the swarm moves through. The thousand thieves automatically dons any of the objects they desire when they reform. If the thousand thieves is [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79), Swarm Getaway doesn't reveal their location. They can use the same action to coalesce from their swarm shape back into their normal form.
name: Thousand Thieves

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Swordkeeper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4574)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Collectors who want to guard their magical arsenals procure or build swordkeepers. These multi-armed constructs are equal parts display case and security system, each holding a single weapon within its body and projecting copies of the weapon it stores to deter would-be thieves.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 29

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Swordkeeper.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 10">[Swordkeeper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4574)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Construct" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=563" />
<trait label="Mindless" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=652" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 314

**Perception** +20

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +21, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +23

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +7

**Dex** +5

**Con** +5

**Int** -5

**Wis** +2

**Cha** -5
</row>

**Central Weapon** A swordkeeper's torso houses a single weapon of a level no higher than the swordkeeper. While the swordkeeper is operational, the chamber requires four successful DC 32 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) checks to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=2411) to open; on a critical failure, magical backlash deals 6d6 force damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) to the creature attempting the check. If the swordkeeper is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81), [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88), or [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93), both DCs are reduced by 2. If the weapon is removed, the swordkeeper's echoblades vanish.

**Items**
_[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [vitalizing ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2852) [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=386)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 29 (31 with guard raised)

**Fort** +21 

**Ref** +19 

**Will** +14 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 245

</row>

**Immunities**
[bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [nonlethal attacks](/Rules.aspx?ID=2311), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)

**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** <actions string="Reaction" /> 

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
echoblade +23 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 2d8+13 slashing plus 1d8 force

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d8+13 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
echoblade +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 2d8+13 slashing plus 1d8 force

**Echoblade Flurry** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The swordkeeper makes two melee echoblade Strikes. If both Strikes hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weakness. Apply the swordkeeper's multiple attack penalty normally.

**Project Echoblade** <actions string="Free Action" />  **Requirements** The swordkeeper has a central weapon; **Effect** The swordkeeper projects an echoblade—a force copy of its central weapon that deals an additional 1d8 force damage and gains thrown 30 feet. Echoblades inherit the weapon damage dice, weapon traits, and runes of the central weapon but no other abilities or activations. The swordkeeper gains access to their [critical specialization](/Rules.aspx?ID=2203) effects. The swordkeeper can have up to four echoblades at once; unattended echoblades vanish at the end of the swordkeeper's turn.

**Colossal Echo** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610)) **Requirements** The swordkeeper has a central weapon; **Effect** The swordkeeper projects a massive echoblade held in all four hands, dealing 9d8 force damage to all creatures in a 30-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). It can't use Colossal Echo again for 1d4 rounds.

**Raise Guard** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Effect** The swordkeeper raises an echoblade to protect itself, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of its next turn.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Treasure Guardians</title>

While the sample swordkeeper awards treasure based on its level, you can use a swordkeeper to provide PCs with a powerful or significant weapon— particularly an artifact or relic. To create a swordkeeper with a different weapon, replace the [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724) trait from its echoblade Strikes with those of the new weapon, change the weapon damage dice to match, and apply any runes. Unless you want to significantly change the swordkeeper's level, you should also adjust the Strike damage to make sure it isn't too high or too low.
</aside>
name: Swordkeeper

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Tanuki Village Hero](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4575)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A tanuki village hero is a firefighter, monster slayer, peacekeeper, and altruist. Village heroes are elected by community vote; while the village hero sometimes grumbles that it's a lot of responsibility for a single tanuki to bear, the community insists there's no higher honor than to be trusted with their well-being.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 17

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Tanuki_Hero.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Tanuki Village Hero](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4575)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
<trait label="Tanuki" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=830" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 315

**Perception** +4

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Tanuki](/Languages.aspx?ID=179)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +5, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +6, [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +3, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +6

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +3

**Con** +2

**Int** +0

**Wis** -1

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Items**
[Dart](/Weapons.aspx?ID=426) (10), [Kama](/Weapons.aspx?ID=410), [studded leather](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1774)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 16 

**Fort** +7 

**Ref** +8 

**Will** +2 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 21

</row>

**Tactical Retreat** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** The tanuki takes damage; **Effect** The tanuki runs to a better tactical position. The tanuki gains the [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) condition until the beginning of their next turn and [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
kama +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)),
**Damage** 1d6+2 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dart +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 1d4+2 piercing

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The tanuki takes on the form of a mundane raccoon dog. This makes them Tiny and gives them a +2 status bonus to their Stealth modifier, but they can't make Strikes.

**Tricky Throw** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The tanuki winds up and puts their everything into a throw. They make a dart Strike at one enemy within 40 feet. If the Strike is unsuccessful, the tanuki falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). If the Strike is successful, they really did put everything into the throw, having transformed into the dart the moment they threw it. The tanuki disappears from the space they threw from, appears in a space adjacent to the enemy and makes a kama Strike against said enemy, who's [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the attack.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-625" />
name: Tanuki Village Hero

Tatzlwyrms resemble human-sized snakes with two arms and a dragon's head. Distant relatives of dragons, tatzlwyrms possess only a meager level of intelligence. They can speak Draconic (with a thick, hissing accent), but their ability to reason is limited, and they can't use magic. They do possess a level of cunning, however, and some have been known to build rudimentary traps and even lairs. They aren't treasure hoarders, though, so adventurers shouldn't expect to find a tatzlwyrm sitting atop a bed of coins. Some tatzlwyrms have managed to forge working relationships with dragons, though it's a rare occurrence. From time to time, dragons have used them as messengers (because they can speak their language, however crudely), scouts (due to their smaller size), guides (when they're familiar with a particular mountain), and even muscle (harassing foes not worthy of a dragon's direct attention). Tatzlwyrms hibernate in cold weather, and when they feel winter approaching, they seek out underground lairs, mountain crevices, or even hay lofts. On one notable occasion, villagers reported being briefly overrun by tatzlwyrms after a conflict between spellcasters in the nearby hills caused a sudden blizzard. Local scholars still debate whether the cause of the influx stemmed from the sudden change in weather or if the creatures were drawn to one of the other magic users staying in town who were part of the same pilgrimage as those doing battle. When confronted, tatzlwyrms are more likely to attack than retreat. Indeed, they sometimes lie in wait on purpose, taking advantage of their scales' natural camouflage and awaiting the chance to unleash their signature move: belching poisonous vapor into an opponent's face. Since this breath attack doesn't have much range, tatzlwyrms usually need to grab their foes first to bring them close to their mouth. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 16 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 [Tatzlwyrm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4576) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 316 **Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +7, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +8, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +4, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +7 **Str** +4 **Dex** +1 **Con** +3 **Int** -3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Natural Camouflage** A tatzlwyrm's green, gray, and brown scales provide it natural camouflage. In areas of dense undergrowth, a tatzlwyrm can move at its full Speed when [Sneaking](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405), and it gains a +4 circumstance bonus to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404). --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +5 **Will** +8 **HP** 30 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 30 feet **Melee** jaws +10 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 1d8+6 piercing **Melee** claw +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 1d6+6 slashing **Poison Gasp** The tatzlwyrm belches a puff of poisonous vapor into the face of an adjacent creature, which must attempt a DC 15 Fortitude save; the creature takes a –2 circumstance penalty to this save if it's [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58). The tatzlwyrm can't use Poison Gasp again for 2 rounds.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1.
**Failure** The target takes 2d6 poison damage and is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The target takes 4d6 poison damage and is enfeebled 1 for 1 minute.
Other Tatzlwyrms People in some regions claim to have seen creatures that appear just like tatzlwyrms in most respects—a long body, two arms, and a head—except that they have traits not of reptilian origin. Travelers on a savanna should beware a furry, [lion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2866)-headed tatzlwyrm protecting their cubs, while those in the mountains might be attacked by a feathered (but wingless) variant with the head of an [eagle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2968).
name: Tatzlwyrm

Even other earth elementals and creatures of stone fear the formidable terra carver. Their four arms end with sharpened stone tools that can cleave through the toughest rocks—and anything that wanders into their territory. Despite having powerful forms, their bulky upper bodies appear comically large compared to their stout legs. They lumber slowly through their territory and spend most of their time cutting stone deep in the bedrock to create elaborate networks of tunnels. They prefer isolation, and when two terra carvers meet, it typically ends with one hewing the other. Instead of taking their enemy's territory, a terra carver collapses the tunnels, continuing elsewhere with their own designs. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Terra Carver](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4577) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 317 **Perception** +23; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) (imprecise) 100 feet **Languages** [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +22, [Mining Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +25 **Str** +8 **Dex** -1 **Con** +6 **Int** +4 **Wis** +0 **Cha** +2 **Earthbound** When not touching solid ground, the terra carver is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 and can't use reactions. --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +19 **Will** +23 **HP** 265 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** crashing fall **Crashing Fall** Due to their size, a terra carver falls a lot harder than most creatures. When a terra carver is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) or takes falling damage, they take an additional 15 bludgeoning damage in addition to any other effect.
**Territorial Retaliation** **Trigger** A creature within 15 feet uses a move action or leaves a square during a move action (move actions using only a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) don't trigger this reaction); **Effect** The terra carver attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) the triggering creature. Regardless of the result, the space of the triggering creature and all spaces on the ground adjacent to that creature become [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) for 1 round --- **Speed** 20 feet; burrow 10 feet **Melee** stone tool +26 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [versatile B and S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 3d10+16 piercing plus hew stone **Ranged** rock +24 ([Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d8+12 bludgeoning **Carve Projectile** The terra carver carves a deadly projectile from nearby materials and makes a rock ranged Strike that gains the [deadly d10 trait](/Traits.aspx?ID=570). On a successful hit, the target also falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). **Hew Stone** Melee attacks the terra carver makes with their stone tool ignore physical [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) and [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183). **Stone Tunnels** A terra carver can burrow through solid stone. When they do, they leave a tunnel. **Wedge** The terra carver attempts a stone tool Strike while wedging the blow further with another tool. If it hits, the target takes an additional 3d10 damage of the same type as the Strike and is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2 for 1 hour or until the creature is fully healed. This counts as two attacks for the terra carver's multiple attack penalty.
Voiceless Miners Terra carvers are talented miners, and their tunnels are some of the longest lasting on any plane. However, the reason for these tunnels is unknown. Scholars have theorized that their tunnels function as a form of written language for the otherwise voiceless elementals. Unfortunately, attempts to map abandoned tunnels often end where another terra carver collapsed an encroaching tunnel, and mapping active tunnels often results in the cartographer never making it back.
name: Terra Carver

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Tikbalang](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4580)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Tikbalangs are forest creatures that delight in leading travelers astray. They deceive and mislead with their formidable magic, leaping from tree to tree while laughing or neighing uncontrollably. While not malicious, neither are they empathetic to the plight of their victims. Tikbalangs would sooner forget about those whom they've tricked and leave these unfortunate souls to die than lead them back to their intended path through guilty altruism.

Tikbalangs' occult magic stems from the esoteric mystery of believing a lie. In the moment of crafting illusions or conjuring extradimensional spaces, tikbalangs themselves believe that what they're creating is real. This conviction makes their spells harder to resist, and this same principle also makes them very effective liars. But this readiness to believe also makes them susceptible to deceptions and illusions in turn, and they're particularly vulnerable to mind games. Lone travelers without magic of their own are advised to learn riddles, sleight of hand, or other tricks to defend against tikbalangs' oft-deadly entertainment. Other whispers suggest wearing one's shirt inside out to confuse the creatures, or passing by a tikbalang's forest quietly to avoid drawing their attention.

Unlike some illusionists, tikbalangs can also rely on their physical prowess should the need arise. They have unusually long legs that end in cloven hooves and stand as tall as ogres when upright. Skilled climbers and leapers, they're also well known as master wrestlers, where their long limbs put them at a distinct advantage over their foes. They have elongated faces that, when combined with their well-kept hair, suggest an equine appearance—although some tikbalangs have saurian or birdlike faces instead.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 28

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Tikbalang.png" />
</column>

</row>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />

<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=547" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 319

**Perception** +16; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +20, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +21, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +16

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +4

**Con** +4

**Int** -1

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +6
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 27 

**Fort** +19 

**Ref** +17 

**Will** +14 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 197

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) 10

**Believe the Lie** The tikbalang takes a –2 circumstance penalty to saves against illusion spells and to their Will DC against checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) to them.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 2d6+8 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hoof +20 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d10+8 bludgeoning

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 29
- **4th**
[Hypnotize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1564), [Mirage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1604)
- **8th**
[Quandary](/Spells.aspx?ID=1644) (once per week)

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The tikbalang takes on the appearance of any Medium or Large humanoid. This doesn't change the tikbalang's Speed or their attack and damage modifiers with their Strikes.

**Flailing Thrash** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The tikbalang makes two fist Strikes, with each Strike dealing an extra 1d6 damage against creatures grabbing or [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) by the tikbalang. The multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after both attacks.

**Unnatural Leap** <actions string="Single Action" />  The tikbalang jumps up to their Speed horizontally, or half that vertically.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Golden Strand</title>

Why do adventurers go through the trouble of wrestling a tikbalang? Hidden among the luxurious ebony mane of the creature is a single strand of golden hair. Anyone who successfully [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) the tikbalang can [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) (DC 29) the strand and [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=3259) to pluck it from their head. Made of actual gold, this strand (worth 150 gp) holds magic particularly well and is highly sought after for creating magic items. A tikbalang regrows their plucked golden strand in a year.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Elysian Titan](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4581)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Those titans who remained true to their creators, refusing to participate in their peers' rebellion, earned their freedom while the others were imprisoned. Many Elysian titans train aspiring champions of freedom, sacrifice, and selflessness.
