markdown:
Thanatotic titans served as assassins in the war against the deities. They learned to sever their targets' divine connections, murdering mortal priests and divine heralds alike. Thanatotic titans were locked away in the Outer Rifts, but some have freed themselves and crept out from the Outer Rifts so they might continue their sprees of murder and mayhem. They maintain a devotion to their purpose and a grudge, and to this day, they seek out the faithful for slaughter. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 49 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 47 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 44 [Thanatotic Titan](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4582) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 320 **Perception** +36; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); telepathy 100 feet (page 362) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +45, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +41, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +36, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +38, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +38, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +36 **Str** +10 **Dex** +4 **Con** +9 **Int** +8 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +8 **Items** _[+2 greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50)_, _[+3 greater striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [halberd](/Weapons.aspx?ID=381)_ --- **AC** 46 **Fort** +37 **Ref** +34 **Will** +35 +4 status to all saves vs. [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) or [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) **HP** 540 **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) **Impossible Stature** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 100 feet. Titans warp perception and distance around them to seem even larger and more imposing. A creature that enters or begins its turn within the emanation must succeed at a DC 45 Will save or its movement toward the titan is movement over [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) (greater difficult terrain on a critical failure) for 1 round.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** The titan can use their Reactive Strike when a creature within their reach uses a concentrate action, in additional to its normal trigger. They disrupt actions on any hit, not just a critical hit—including triggering [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) actions. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** _halberd_ +42 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 4d10+25 piercing **Melee** foot +39 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+20 bludgeoning **Ranged** void chunk +39 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 200 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)), **Damage** 3d12+10 bludgeoning plus 2d10 void and void explosion **Divine Innate Spells** DC 45 - **5th** [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665) - **6th** [Scrying](/Spells.aspx?ID=1662) (×3) - **7th** [Spell Riposte](/Spells.aspx?ID=2027) - **8th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (at will), [Spiritual Epidemic](/Spells.aspx?ID=2030) (at will), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) (at will) - **10th** [Falling Stars](/Spells.aspx?ID=1521), [Massacre](/Spells.aspx?ID=1596) - **Constant (10th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Divine Rituals** DC 45 - **5th** [Planar Servitor](/Rituals.aspx?ID=120), [Resurrect](/Rituals.aspx?ID=123) **Godslayer** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Trigger** The titan damages a creature capable of using divine spells or abilities; **Effect** The creature must attempt a DC 45 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature can't use divine spells or abilities for 1 round and is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2. Only powerful non-divine magic, such as [_manifestation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1592), can undo this effect.
**Failure** As success, but the duration is 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As success, but the duration is unlimited. **Titanic Charge** The titan Strides twice and makes a melee Strike. If the Strike hits, the titan can cast [_earthquake_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1507) centered on the target as a free action. **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=81)** Huge or smaller, foot, DC 45 **Void Explosion** If the titan's void chunk Strike isn't a critical failure, the chunk explodes, dealing 10d6 void damage to all creatures in a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) (DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). **Wide Cleave** The titan makes a melee weapon Strike against each foe within their reach. This counts as three attacks for the titan's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until all attacks have been made.

markdown:
Danava titans once regulated the foundational forces that shaped reality. Rebuked by the gods for being harsh and inflexible overseers, these spurned titans joined their siblings in the failed war against their creators. Defeated, the danavas were cast into the paralyzing depths of the cosmos's seas. The few danava titans who have escaped now wield the cold, darkness, and crushing pressure of their prisons in pursuit of their ancient visions of reality. Danavas split mountains, wake primordial beasts, or level whole civilizations in accordance with grand designs they forged at the dawn of time. Others hunt and harvest the balance-enforcing aeons, whom they see as usurpers of their divine responsibility. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 51 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 49 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 46 [Danava Titan](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4583) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 321 **Perception** +41; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727), [wavesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=84) (imprecise) 100 feet **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +39, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +43, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +46, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +43, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +41, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +43, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +41, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +43 **Str** +11 **Dex** +8 **Con** +10 **Int** +10 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +6 **Items** _[+3 major striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [greatclub](/Weapons.aspx?ID=377)_ --- **AC** 49 **Fort** +41 **Ref** +37 **Will** +37 +4 status to all saves vs. [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) or [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) **HP** 470 **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) **Hadalic Presence** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) Creatures that fail their Will save against the titan's impossible stature aura also experience the crushing depths and darkness of the ocean floor. Such creatures see as if in an area of [_darkness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) (10th rank), and the titan can use their wavesense to detect such creatures as a precise sense, even if neither are in water. On a critical failure, the creature is also [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81).
**Impossible Stature** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 100 feet. Titans warp perception and distance around them to seem even larger and more imposing. A creature that enters or begins its turn within the [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must succeed at a DC 46 Will save or its movement toward the titan is movement over [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) (greater difficult terrain on a critical failure) for 1 round.
**Relentless** The titan is as ever-moving as ocean waves. They're permanently [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) 1, and the extra action can be used only to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167), or [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) a Spell, or as one of the actions necessary to cast [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493).
**Roiling Rebuke** **Trigger** A creature within 200 feet targets the titan with or includes the titan in the area of an attack, spell, or other effect; **Effect** The titan makes a benthic wave Strike against the triggering creature. If the Strike hits, the titan [disrupts](/Rules.aspx?ID=2342) the triggering action. --- **Speed** 50 feet, fly 50 feet, swim 40 feet; [_water walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1756) **Melee** _greatclub_ +43 ([Backswing](/Traits.aspx?ID=545), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)), **Damage** 4d10+20 bludgeoning plus 2d12 cold **Melee** foot +40 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+20 bludgeoning plus 2d12 cold **Ranged** benthic wave +40 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range 200 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)), **Damage** 4d6+20 bludgeoning plus 2d12 cold **Divine Innate Spells** DC 46, attack +38 - **9th** [Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1473) (at will), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (at will), [Eclipse Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=1508) (×3), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) (×3), [Hydraulic Push](/Spells.aspx?ID=1561) (×3), [Hydraulic Torrent](/Spells.aspx?ID=1562) (×3) - **10th** [Implosion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1572) - **Constant (10th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727), [Water Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=1756) **Rituals** DC 46 - **5th** [Resurrect](/Rituals.aspx?ID=20) (doesn't require secondary casters) - **6th** [Binding Circle](/Rituals.aspx?ID=110) - **8th** [Control Weather](/Rituals.aspx?ID=116) **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=81)** Huge or smaller, foot, DC 46 **Wide Cleave** The titan makes a melee weapon Strike against each foe within their reach. This counts as three attacks for the titan's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until all attacks have been made.
Danava Pillars Some danavas, known as danava pillars, are custodians of a fundamental concept like life or knowledge—each, a crux of the universe. Destroying a danava pillar forcibly shreds their bonds and risks unraveling a portion of reality, with potentially disastrous effects.
name: Danava Titan

The first three hekatonkheires were meant to guard the gates to the [Outer Rifts](/Planes.aspx?ID=45), but they proved too terrifying and rebellious— and so, in disgust, the gods cast them into the gulfs between the planes. Hekatonkheires titans are incomplete, monstrous progeny of the original three, from whom these titans calved like icebergs. Hekatonkheires wield interstellar darkness as a weapon and spurn the limits of physical reality, literally reaching through space with their countless arms. Filled with a drive to either discover their lost identity or create a new one, they metaphysically disembowel ancient beings and cosmic magics, using the entrails to find clues regarding their own nature and parentage or to serve as raw materials for fueling some alien apotheosis. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 53 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 51 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 48 [Hekatonkheires Titan](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4584) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 322 **Perception** +43; darkvision, [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +42, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +48, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +45, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +41, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +39 **Str** +12 **Dex** +10 **Con** +12 **Int** +7 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +9 --- **AC** 52 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) **Fort** +44 **Ref** +40 **Will** +37 +4 status to all saves vs. [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) or [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) **HP** 500 **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) **Impossible Stature** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 120 feet. Titans warp perception and distance around them to seem even larger and more imposing. A creature that enters or begins its turn within the emanation must succeed at a DC 44 Will save or its movement toward the titan is movement over [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) (greater difficult terrain on a critical failure) for 1 round.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** The hekatonkheires gains 99 extra reactions on their turn that they can use only to make Reactive Strikes. --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 60 feet; [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Melee** _empty weapon_ +45 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 50 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [versatile P and S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 4d12+18 bludgeoning plus 2d12 force **Ranged** _empty weapon_ +43 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 200 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile P and S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 4d12+18 bludgeoning plus 2d12 force **Occult Innate Spells** DC 48 - **9th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576), [Phantasmagoria](/Spells.aspx?ID=1629), [Seize Soul](/Spells.aspx?ID=1664), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) - **Constant (10th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727), [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Demolish Veil** **Frequency** once per month; **Trigger** The titan casts [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576); **Effect** The titan arrives in a storm of shattered planar barriers. This has the effects of a 10th-rank [_wrathful storm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1759). **Hundred-Dimension Grasp** The titan reaches between realities to drag foes closer. They attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check and compare the result to the Fortitude DCs of all foes within 120 feet. On a success, a foe is teleported to any square the titan chooses within 120 feet; on a critical success, it's also [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) for 1 round. The titan can [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) any foe brought within 30 feet as a free action. **Hundred-Handed Whirlwind** The titan overwhelms opponents with blows both conventional and interplanar. They make one empty weapon Strike against each foe within reach. Even on a failed attack (but not a critical failure), the titan deals 24 force damage to the target. This counts as three attacks for the titan's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until all attacks have been made. **Send Beyond** **Requirements** The titan has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90); **Effect** The titan thrusts the creature into a nightmare realm full of lightless hands and eyes. This has the effects of [_quandary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1644) (DC 48). The titan can't use Send Beyond for 1d4 rounds. **Shape Emptiness** The titan molds a weapon from interstellar darkness. This empty weapon is a _[+3 major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) weapon_ in any form. The titan can't be disarmed of this weapon and it deals an additional 2d12 force damage. If Released, an empty weapon vanishes.
Hekatonkheires Anatomy Artisans with exceptional skill can harvest a hekatonkheires's black bones before they dissolve upon the titan's death. These bones can be forged into shapeshifting weapons or refined into planar keys that allow travel to esoteric planes.
name: Hekatonkheires Titan

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Totenmaske](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4585)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Spawned by the same unnatural and self-destructive obsessions that drove them when they still lived, totenmaskes are the undead remnants of the most self-indulgent and sinful among mortals. Their need for indulgence proved stronger than even the grasp of death, raising them from their graves. Though unable to sate their depraved desires, these foul undead can drain the very flesh from their victims so as to wrap themselves in a perverse mockery of life that allows them to pursue their base wants. Totenmaskes' specific longings vary—one might be obsessed with food or drink, while another might be vain and desirous of an attractive form to marvel at in a mirror, while yet another could simply long for the scent of blood. 

Whatever sensation the totenmaske seeks, it's always a vice taken to the extreme, for this sin helped condemn them to undeath in the first place. A totenmaske obsessed with food, for example, might find themself assaulting bakeries or breweries, while a vain totenmaske obsessed with glamor could quickly grow bored of each new look and switch their victims out daily, or even hourly.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 23

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Totenmaske.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 7">[Totenmaske](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4585)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=722" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 324

**Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +15, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +15

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +6

**Con** +2

**Int** +1

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +3
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 24 

**Fort** +15 

**Ref** +17 

**Will** +13 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 130
([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83))

</row>

**Immunities**
[bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +18 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d6+7 piercing plus 2d6 void

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d8+7 slashing

**Drink Flesh** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Requirements** The totenmaske hit the same creature with two claw Strikes this turn and is still adjacent to it; **Effect** The totenmaske drains flesh from the creature's body. The creature becomes [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 2 and [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 unless it succeeds at a DC 25 Fortitude save (sickened 2 and drained 2 on a critical failure).

**Living Form** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The totenmaske takes the appearance of a Medium or smaller humanoid creature. This is either their form from before they became undead or the form of the last creature they successfully drained with Drink Flesh. This doesn't change the totenmaske's Speed or the attack and damage bonuses for their Strikes but might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning).

**Shape Flesh**  ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) After spending 1 minute in contact with a [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), or willing creature, a totenmaske can reshape the target's face, causing flesh to cover vital features. The target can attempt a DC 25 Fortitude save to resist; a critical success grants temporary immunity to Shape Flesh for 24 hours. Each time the totenmaske Shapes Flesh, they choose one feature: ears (target becomes [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66)), eyes (target becomes [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59)), mouth (target can't speak or eat), or nose (target can't smell). A creature with both its nose and mouth sealed can't breathe and begins to [_suffocate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1269). Changes are permanent until reversed by removing this curse, but the sealed flesh can be surgically opened with a successful DC 25 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) check that takes 1d4 rounds and deals 1d6 slashing damage per round.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Flesh Sculptors</title>

Some totenmaskes craft macabre “art” by shaping the flesh of their victims, spending hours, days, or even weeks molding a victim's skin or even fusing multiple creatures together into one piece. The horrifying results of this process can take the form of furniture made from flesh that still lives and breathes, “sculptures” that in no way resemble the human form, and even more twisted and depraved things.
</aside>
name: Totenmaske

Before mortals, fey, or even genies, totum fonts were the first creatures born of six elements undivided and harmonious. From the fonts sprung new elemental children, and they aided the gods in the many acts of creation that would follow. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Totum Font](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4586) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 325 **Perception** +30; [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49), [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111), [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113); truespeech **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +25, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +30, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +29, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +30, [Elemental Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +33, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +31 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +8 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 **Items** _[moderate](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2811) [sturdy shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2828)_ (Hardness 13^ HP 104^ BT 52) --- **AC** 35 (37 with shield raised) **Fort** +26 **Ref** +23 **Will** +29 **HP** 325 **Elemental Attunement** A totum font is always attuned to a single element ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732), or [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508)), represented by which of their faces points forward. They can change this attunement to the element of their choice as a single action, or as a free action when they roll initiative.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** **Requirements** The font is attuned to [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), or [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732).
**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=75)** **Requirements** The font is attuned to [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505), or [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508). --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** tendril +30 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+16 bludgeoning **Primal Innate Spells** DC 33 - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Briar's Hold** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508)) **Requirements** The font is attuned to [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508); **Effect** Each creature within 20 feet must succeed at a DC 36 Fortitude save or become [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 minute. When a creature already slowed by Briar's Hold fails its Fortitude save, it becomes petrified for 1 minute but is turned to wood instead of stone. **Brilliance of Flame** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Requirements** The font is attuned to fire; **Effect** The font flies 30 feet and explodes in a fiery Elemental Eruption. **Elemental Eruption** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The font explodes in a cacophony of color and energy. Each creature in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) takes 9d6 damage (DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The explosion deals bludgeoning damage unless the font is attuned to [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527) (electricity damage), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) (fire damage), [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505) (slashing damage), or [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508) (piercing damage). Elemental Eruption gains the trait matching the element the font is attuned to. **Entomb** ([Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585)) **Requirements** The font is attuned to [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), and its last action was a successful tendril Strike; **Effect** The font attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) the target of the Strike. **Overflowing Tide** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) **Requirements** The font is attuned to [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732); **Effect** Elemental waves surge around the font, creating a torrential Elemental Eruption and pushing creatures in the area 20 feet (or 10 feet on a successful saving throw). **Serrated Flurry** ([Metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505)) **Frequency** once per round; **Requirements** The font is attuned to metal; **Effect** The font lashes out with two tendril Strikes, each targeting a different creature within their reach. These Strikes deal slashing damage. **Tempest Charge** ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527)) **Requirements** The font is attuned to [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527); **Effect** The font [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) 60 feet and makes a tendril Strike against a creature it hasn't attacked this turn.
Wellsprings Of One Without access to the balance of all six elemental planes, a totum font becomes fractured and inundated in a single element. Most were healed when the [Planes of Metal](/Planes.aspx?ID=24) and [Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25) returned, but some of these so-called “wellsprings of one” still wander the universe, agitated and confused.
name: Totum Font

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Trilobite](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4587)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Trilobites have segmented, chitinous bodies that provide armor but also enough flexibility for them to roll up and protect their vulnerable undersides from attack. Instead of jaws, trilobites have specialized structures called gnathobases at the base of some of their legs. These serrated and jagged structures grind and masticate any prey the trilobite latches onto, enabling the creatures to feed on anything from hard-shelled clams to soft-bodied creatures. While not particularly fast on their own, they can employ scuttling bursts of speed to dart into ocean silt or seaweed beds to escape predators. 

Oceanic [wizards](/Classes.aspx?ID=39) of many types employ trilobites as familiars. In addition to trilobites' durability, their ubiquity means they make capable spies for their masters as they travel unnoticed throughout the depths.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 13

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Trilobite.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature -1">[Trilobite](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4587)</title>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Trilobite Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4588)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Trilobites typically live in close proximity to others of their kind. On rare occasions, particularly when the most common prey around is larger than what a single trilobite can take down, they form swarms that seem to actively cooperate with each other. These swarms latch onto their prey, and the combined efforts of so many trilobites at once make escape difficult.

<column gap="tiny">

([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 18

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Ice Troll](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4591)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Ice trolls are nightmarish frozen monsters of rime-stained claws and hoarfrost teeth who haunt glaciers, mountains, and tundras. Their unceasing hunger drives them to pursue elk herds, seal colonies, and human villages. Ones that regenerate while sealed within ice floes or permafrost can be birthed from the ice during a thaw after being trapped for months, years, or even decades of unceasing hunger before they can finally break free.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Troll_Ice_Remastered.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 4">[Ice Troll](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4591)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=555" />
<trait label="Giant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=616" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
<trait label="Troll" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=777" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 328

**Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=162)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +10, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +10, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +10

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +2

**Con** +5

**Int** -2

**Wis** +2

**Cha** -2
</row>

**Easily Misled** The ice troll takes a –4 circumstance penalty to their Perception DC against [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks.

**Items**
[Hatchet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=382)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 19 

**Fort** +13 

**Ref** +10 

**Will** +8 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 90

, regeneration 15 (deactivated by [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697))

</row>

**Immunities**
[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555)

**Weaknesses**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10, [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) 10

**Furious Carve** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The ice troll takes fire or sonic damage; **Effect** The troll makes a hatchet or claw Strike against a random creature within reach. If the ice troll has [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), they attempt a DC 15 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to remove it.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet; ice passage

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +13 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d8+5 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hatchet +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)),
**Damage** 2d6+5 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d4+5 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hatchet +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 2d6+5 slashing

**Brutal Sweep** <actions string="Three Actions" />  **Requirements** The ice troll is wielding a hatchet; **Effect** The troll sweeps their hatchet in a large arc, dealing 3d6 slashing damage to all creatures in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 18 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).

**Ice Passage**   An ice troll isn't impeded by [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) caused by snow or ice, nor do they need to attempt [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks to keep from falling on slippery ice.

**[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=73)** <actions string="Single Action" />  claw

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Mixing Variations</title>

[Cavern trolls](/Monsters.aspx?ID=832) and [ice trolls](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4591) spawn their own [jotund trolls](/Monsters.aspx?ID=834), [two-headed trolls](/Monsters.aspx?ID=833), and [warleaders](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3220). In these cases, change their immunity, regeneration, and weaknesses to match their origin. You can also update their reaction's trigger and its [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) removal to match those weaknesses.
</aside>

markdown:
Insatiable scavengers, cavern trolls stalk the eternal gloom of the Darklands, consuming all in their path—even rocks and minerals. These rocky goliaths can tunnel through stone with their ironsharp claws. Despite a debilitating vulnerability to sunlight that petrifies them on sustained contact, hungry cavern trolls are often drawn to the comparatively lush surface world. Even when petrified, the immobile trolls are often mistaken for boulders or other natural phenomenon, leaving them free to hunt once night falls again. As cavern trolls age, their flinty skin becomes studded with small crystals and stones of various composition. Paragons of their kind have toothy maws replete with large crystalline fangs. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Cavern Troll](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4592) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 328 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=162), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +16, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +14 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +6 **Int** -2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -2 **Easily Misled** The cavern troll takes a –4 circumstance penalty to their Perception DC against [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks. --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +13 **Will** +9 **HP** 135 , regeneration 20 (deactivated by [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) **Weaknesses** acid 10, sonic 10 **Sunlight Petrification** If exposed to direct sunlight, a cavern troll immediately becomes [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 and can't use reactions. The slowed value increases by 1 each time the cavern troll ends its turn in sunlight. If the cavern troll's actions are reduced to 0 in this way, they become [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87) until they spends at least 1 minute in [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2404). Spells like [_sunburst_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1707) that create magical sunlight can't petrify a cavern troll, but the troll is slowed 1 for 1d4 rounds after being exposed to such an effect.
**Furious Throw** **Trigger** The cavern troll takes acid or sonic damage; **Effect** The troll uses Throw Rock targeting a random enemy within their first range increment. If the cavern troll has [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), they attempt a DC 15 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to remove it. --- **Speed** 25 feet, burrow 20 feet, climb 20 feet **Melee** jaws +16 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+8 piercing **Melee** claw +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d6+8 slashing **Ranged** rock +16 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d12+8 bludgeoning **[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=73)** claw **Rock Tunneler** A cavern troll can [burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310) through solid stone at a Speed of 10 feet. They can leave a tunnel if they desire. **[Throw Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=80)**
Mixing Variations [Cavern trolls](/Monsters.aspx?ID=832) and [ice trolls](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4591) spawn their own [jotund trolls](/Monsters.aspx?ID=834), [two-headed trolls](/Monsters.aspx?ID=833), and [warleaders](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3220). In these cases, change their immunity, regeneration, and weaknesses to match their origin. You can also update their reaction's trigger and its [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) removal to match those weaknesses.
name: Cavern Troll

<traits>

markdown:
After a particularly severe head injury, a troll might regrow two heads, with each taking on fragments of the old personality and its responsibilities over the body. As separated parts of a whole, the heads find it difficult to agree on any but the most basic tasks. These tasks usually involve gathering food to satisfy two mouths, even if they share a single cavernous stomach. Dread tales of two-headed trolls' ravenous appetites are whispered of in homesteads throughout the lands of the Inner Sea. It's a custom for parents to invoke the two-headed troll as a warning to misbehaving children. “Finish your chores,” a parent might say to a stubborn child, “or a two-headed troll will snatch you away at night and swallow you whole!” It's unclear why such a morbid tradition gained traction, but it's an undeniable fact that two-headed trolls have an appetite for “nibbles”— creatures small enough to devour with one bite. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Two-Headed Troll](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4593) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 329 **Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=162) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +17 **Str** +6 **Dex** +1 **Con** +6 **Int** -2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 **Easily Misled** The two-headed troll takes a –4 circumstance penalty to their Perception DC against [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks.
**Independent Brains** Each of a two-headed troll’s heads rolls their own initiative and has their own turn. Neither head can [Delay](/Actions.aspx?ID=2294). At the start of a head’s turn, that head gets 2 actions and 1 reaction. Each brain controls one arm, but both can move the legs. Any ability or item that would sever a two-headed troll’s head (such as a [_vorpal_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2853) weapon) doesn’t cause the two-headed troll to die if they still have their other head, but does cause them to lose the turns, actions, and reactions of the severed head. [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effects that target a single creature affect only one of the troll’s heads. **Items** [Club](/Weapons.aspx?ID=357) --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +15 **Will** +14 **HP** 190 , regeneration 20 (deactivated by [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) or [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) **Weaknesses** electricity 10, fire 10 **Head Regrowth** A two-headed troll's regeneration can regrow a severed head. After regaining Hit Points from regeneration, the two-headed troll attempts a DC 10 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333). On a success, a missing head is fully restored. If a two-headed troll loses their last remaining head, they die immediately.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** jaws +18 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+8 piercing **Melee** claw +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+8 slashing **Melee** club +18 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d6+8 bludgeoning **Ranged** club +13 ([thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 2d6+8 bludgeoning **Collaborative Chomp** The troll makes a claw Strike and Grabs a single target. If both are successful, the other head can use their reaction to make a jaws Strike against that creature.
Severed Spiral Although most two-headed trolls have difficulty seeing past their confusion and rage, some have founded a nuanced faith emulating the dualistic gods [Gozreh](/Deities.aspx?ID=284), [Nethys](/Deities.aspx?ID=288), and [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=290). The obscure philosophy seeks enlightenment at menhirs of black and white stones, most notably the Severed Spiral in Mendev.
name: Two-Headed Troll

Jotund trolls are gigantic, nine-headed horrors who prowl frigid moors, marshes, and wastelands, often alone and always ravenous. While each of the jotund troll's nine heads possess their own brains and senses, they bicker with each other much less than the heads of a two-headed troll. Yet the heads still argue, particularly over which of them gets to eat. The fact that all nine maws lead to the same shared stomach makes little difference in such culinary disagreements. Many scholars echo a story that jotund trolls resemble the first trolls, titans cast out of Elysium and cursed for their crimes against the gods. The jotund trolls themselves make no claims to such a heritage, however, and those concerned with such matters claim the father of all trolls arose in the Outer Rifts. Individual jotund trolls either begin their life as a common troll or, far more rarely, are born to a jotund troll parent. To the jotund trolls, their nine heads are a crown of rule that marks them as above the natural order and free from its laws. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 39 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 [Jotund Troll](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4594) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 330 **Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=162) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +29, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +27 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +8 **Int** -1 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 35 ([all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) (page 360)) **Fort** +31 **Ref** +24 **Will** +23 **HP** 360 , regeneration 40 (deactivated by [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) or [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) **Weaknesses** electricity 15, fire 15 **Head Regrowth** A jotund troll's regeneration can regrow severed heads. After regaining Hit Points from regeneration, the jotund troll attempts a DC 8 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333). On a success, one missing head is fully restored; on a critical success, two missing heads are fully restored. If a jotund troll loses their last remaining head, they die immediately.
**Reactive Heads** A jotund troll gains an extra reaction per round for each of their heads beyond the first, which they can use only to make [Reactive Strikes](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48) with their jaws or to Fast Swallow. They can't use more than 1 reaction for the same triggering action, even if a creature leaves several squares within their reach, and must use a different head for each Reactive Strike. Whenever one of the jotund troll's heads is severed, the troll loses 1 of their extra reactions per round.
**Furious Roar** **Trigger** The jotund troll takes [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) or [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) damage; **Effect** The jotund troll uses their Cacophonous Roar and, if they're aware of the damage's source, can [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) toward it. If the jotund troll has [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), they attempt a DC 15 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to remove it.
**[Reactive Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2256)** --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 20 feet **Melee** jaws +29 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+14 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** claw +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+14 slashing **Cacophonous Roar** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The jotund troll roars from all their heads mystically distorting the listener's mind. Each nontroll creature in a 100-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 34 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is stupefied 2 for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round. **Fast Swallow** **Trigger** The jotund troll [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) a creature with their jaws; **Effect** The troll uses [Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77). **Ravenous Jaws** The jotund troll makes a number of jaws Strikes up to their number of heads, each against a different target. These attacks count toward the troll's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after the jotund troll makes all of these attacks. **[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=73)** claw **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77)** (attack) Medium, 3d12+8 bludgeoning, Rupture 36
Ravenous Mutants Jotund trolls arise with distressing regularity, particularly in areas like the magic-warped Mana Wastes and the radiation-wracked badlands of Numeria. Although they generally consume other trolls along with everything else, voracious jotund troll families haunt particularly desolate lands. The presence of a single jotund troll can lead an area to blight and ruin, beyond even the damage caused by its terrifying hunger.
name: Jotund Troll

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Trollhound](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4595)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Trollhounds are squat, slavering beasts akin to [trolls](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=478) in canine form. With a similar nigh-insatiable hunger stoked by their regenerative metabolisms, packs of wild trollhounds prowl the foothills of regions where trolls dwell to hunt for vast quantities of meat. In some regions, trolls breed trollhounds as pets, utilizing the trollhounds' keen sense of smell to aid in the hunt. 

Covered in fetid, weeping sores, trollhounds are carriers of a debilitating contagion known as bloodfire fever. Creatures that contract the disease through the bite of a trollhound experience deep internal pain, as if their blood were on fire. Additional symptoms include loss of muscle coordination, pus-filled blisters, and overall lethargy and fatigue. Other than living with the pain from skin irritation, both trolls and trollhounds are immune to the major effects of the disease.

Trollhounds are fearless on the hunt and in combat, relying on their ability to regenerate to carry them through. Not even the threat of fire is enough to repel them, as the beasts don't recognize the danger it represents. Nevertheless, fire is one of the most effective tools in combating trollhounds; canny hunters know to burn every last remnant of a supposedly slain trollhound, for their regenerative powers are potent indeed.

While trolls have had great success in domesticating, training, and even befriending trollhounds, the same can't be said for other would-be masters. Whether impeded by constant exposure to trollhounds' diseased slobber, their ravenous hunger that never seems to be fully sated, or simply their foul personality and quick-to-bite temperament, most attempts to use trollhounds in place of more reliable guardians end in pain, misery, and a pack of feral trollhounds escaping into the hinterlands.

Left to their own devices, trollhounds will breed relatively quickly. It can take less than a year for a small pack to multiply into such a size that they pose a significant threat to the countryside. Best to leave the trollhounds to the trolls, as they say!

**Pack Attack**   The trollhound's Strikes deals 1d6 extra damage to creatures within reach of at least two of the trollhound's allies.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Trollhound Genesis</title>

The first trollhound was the result of a clash between a starving [warg](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3230) and an enraged [forest troll](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3219). Though the warg was no match for the troll, it landed several bites before retreating into the nearby woods. The regenerative chunks of troll flesh within the warg's bleeding maw infected the warg's body, slowly and painfully transforming the beast into a proto-trollhound. Stripped of cunning and intelligence, the altered warg attacked an approaching pack of wargs, spreading the unusual infection. Soon, trollhounds became their own species, and successive generations of the beasts have been discovered hunting alongside trolls, sharing an instinctual bond with the lumbering giants. Many modern-day wargs hold a deep-seated animosity toward trollhounds because of this past. This antagonism is exacerbated by the fact that, in rare cases, a warg who reaches stage 5 of bloodfire fever might transform into a trollhound.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Tupilaq](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4596)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A tupilaq is an artistically crafted construct carved from animal bones (typically whale or walrus) and imbued with the express purpose of eviscerating its creator's enemies. A tupilaq manifests from hateful magic— such a thing can only be created by someone who believes they were grievously wronged. When a terrible, unforgivable crime is committed against someone with great skill at carving and who has knowledge of the proper ritual, the aggrieved can channel their grief and hate through whispered incantations to bring a tupilaq to life.

Sadly, the same emotions used to create a tupilaq often lead to even greater tragedies. Functionally immortal but built for vengeance, a tupilaq lacks the reason or discernment to do anything other than pursue the goal imbued by its creator. A wish to utterly destroy an enemy can lead a tupilaq to slaughter an entire settlement, killing until it has slain everyone even remotely related to the original offender. Many stories describe how a tupilaq ends up causing more tragedy for its creator than the crime that precipitated its creation. The most common tales feature the tupilaq eventually murdering its creator's spouse or family members due to a distant relationship to the original target that no one knew about.

A tupilaq's animating energies aren't tied to its original functions, and the creature typically long outlasts its creator, its victims, and often any who recall the reason for its creation. It might fall into a sort of hibernation once it has achieved immediate vengeance, but in many cases, the constructs reawaken to continue their rampage against unsuspecting targets ignorant of their involvement.

Spellcasters might occasionally summon these constructs. A summoned tupilaq, hauled unceremoniously from its vengeance, becomes a nearfrenzied combatant, unleashing every offensive ability in its arsenal to break free. These reactions aren't strategic or considered, but an instinctual, almost programmed need to return to its true purpose. Conjurers should be wary about a tupilaq employing spells that might be turned against its summoner, such as a [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530) “accidentally” placed such that the spell incinerates the spellcaster and returns the creatures from whence it came.

**Carver's Curse**   When a tupilaq is created, the curse imparted by its creator manifests in the form of a single 3rd-rank [primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) spell the tupilaq can cast three times per day. The particular spell is a reflection of the creator's wish for vengeance. By default, and for a found or summoned tupilaq, this spell is [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Relics Of The Past</title>

A tupilaq can last indefinitely once created, and it isn't uncommon for a tupilaq to be unearthed years, decades, or even centuries after its creator has passed away. Archaeologists working at northern dig sites might accidentally stumble across one of these fierce constructs, inadvertently awakening its vengeful curse.
</aside>

markdown:
Due to their affinity for darkness and apocalyptic terror, tzitzimitls are widely feared as harbingers of death and destruction. A solar eclipse marks their arrival, and they draw strange powers of darkness and electricity from these phenomena. Some sages believe tzitzimitls to be instruments of the gods, called down to destroy worlds whose times have come, while others claim they're the undead remains of stranded exiles from a far-off civilization of spacefaring giants. Such legends are ancient and fragmented, but some tell of tzitzimitls being defeated by great heroes and sealed away—though these tales imply that the enormous undead now lie buried and waiting, soon to rampage again should their tombs be breached. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Tzitzimitl](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4597) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 334, [Pathfinder #150: Broken Promises](/Sources.aspx?ID=15) pg. 82 **Perception** +32; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +33, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +37, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +33, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +37, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +37, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +40 **Str** +10 **Dex** +8 **Con** +6 **Int** +5 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +8 --- **AC** 43 **Fort** +29 **Ref** +32 **Will** +35 **HP** 390 ([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 15, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83) (page 362)) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 15, physical 15 (except bludgeoning) **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 15 **Light to Dark** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) **Trigger** A creature uses an ability or spell with the [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) trait within 120 feet of the tzitzimitl; **Effect** The tzitzimitl inverts the energy used in the triggering ability or spell, causing it to lose the vitality trait and gain the void trait, and changing all instances of vitality energy or healing in the ability's description to void energy. --- **Speed** 50 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** claw +34 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+10 slashing plus 3d8 electricity and drain life **Ranged** eye beam +34 ([range 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d12 electricity plus 10d6 force **Occult Innate Spells** DC 38 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) - **3rd** [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553) (×3) - **4th** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) (×3) - **7th** [Eclipse Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=1508), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1720) (×3) - **9th** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) (×3), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1720), [Wails of the Damned](/Spells.aspx?ID=1747) - **Constant (6th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Rituals** DC 38 - **2nd** [Create Undead](/Rituals.aspx?ID=10) (9th rank) **Drain Life** When a tzitzimitl's claw Strike deals damage to a living creature, the tzitzimitl gains 20 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321), and the target must succeed at a DC 41 Fortitude save or become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 2. Further damage dealt by a tzitzimitl's claw Strike increases the value of the drained condition by 2 on a failed save, to a maximum of drained 4. **Eclipse** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Effect** The tzitzimitl casts [_darkness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) and drains the heat and warmth from the darkness spell's area. Each creature within the spell's area must attempt a DC 41 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The creature takes 4d8 cold damage.
**Success** The creature takes 8d8 cold damage and is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature takes 16d8 cold damage and is slowed 1 for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 16d8 cold damage, is slowed 2 for 1 minute, and is [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1.
Harbingers Of The End A tzitzimitl's arrival in a region often precedes a deadly disaster or apocalyptic event. It isn't known whether the tzitzimitl accompanies an event already fated to occur or if the undead causes such events through their own strange magic.
name: Tzitzimitl

markdown:
An unrisen is a mangled conglomeration of splintered bones, decaying organs, and rotting flesh, created when a ritual such as resurrect goes catastrophically wrong. Immense care must be taken, for if such a ritual fails utterly, an unrisen can be the result—as many a ritualist has learned to their horror. Unrisen are barely intelligent, aware only of the agony constantly inflicted by their flawed creation and their resentment for the living. They tend to attack the casters involved in the botched ritual first before lashing out at everyone else around them. Though an unrisen's twisted form is unrecognizable as the intended target of the resurrection, its wordless howls are often disturbingly similar to the deceased's voice. If an unrisen is destroyed before it can rise again, it's reduced to a handful of metallic blue-green salts referred to as essential salts. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Unrisen](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4598) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 335 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +24, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +19 **Str** +7 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** -2 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +17 **Will** +21 **HP** 220 (meant to live, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Weaknesses** resurrection vulnerability **Meant to Live** ([occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) Whenever an unrisen would take damage from [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) energy, it instead heals half that number of Hit Points.
**Resurrection Vulnerability** A creature with a prepared or spontaneous spell that can restore the dead to life (such as [_breath of life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1456) or [_raise dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1645)) can expend an appropriate spell slot as a 2-action activity to destroy an unrisen within 30 feet. The attempt fails if the unrisen succeeds at a Will save against the creature's spell DC.
**Rise Again** ([occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) If the unrisen is reduced to 0 Hit Points by means other than fire damage, disintegration, or its resurrection vulnerability, it returns to unlife at the start of its next turn. It has 100 Hit Points and is [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) in the space in which it was destroyed. The unrisen can't be returned by this ability again for 1 hour. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** jaws +24 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d8+13 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** claw +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+13 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Agonized Howl** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The unrisen howls in pain at its cursed existence. Creatures within a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) take 9d8 mental damage with a DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save. The unrisen can't use Agonized Howl again for 1d4 rounds. **Awful Approach** **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Effect** The unrisen reshapes its grotesque form to move swiftly. It Strides twice. Any living creature that can see the unrisen during this movement must succeed at a DC 28 Fortitude save or be [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (or sickened 2 on a critical failure); this is a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) and [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) effect. **Death Grip** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Requirements** The unrisen has a living creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90); **Effect** The unrisen attempts to destroy its victim's life force so it shares in the unrisen's fate. The creature must succeed at a DC 30 Fortitude save or become [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1. While the curse lasts, the creature regains only half as many HP from effects with both the [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623) and [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) traits; if it dies, any attempt to raise it from the dead causes it to return as an unrisen. The curse ends automatically if the creature's doomed value is reduced to 0.
Unrisen Salts An unrisen's essential salts, formed from its remaining distilled life essence, can be used for spells and rituals such as create undead or resurrect, replacing 600 gp worth of gemstones. The existence of these essential salts doesn't damage the soul of the unrisen's source creature, but devout [Pharasmins](/Deities.aspx?ID=290) still frown on their use.
name: Unrisen

From the moment they're born, urdefhans are prepared for war. Urdefhan warriors are among the least powerful urdefhans one might encounter outside of their eerie underground cities. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Urdefhan Warrior](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4599) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 336 **Perception** +9; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +10, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +9, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +7, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +7 **Str** +3 **Dex** +1 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +2 **Items** [Composite Longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=434) (20 arrows), [Rhoka Sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=158), [studded leather](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1774) --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +8 **Will** +9 **HP** 55 (void healing) **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), fear **Weaknesses** [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) 5 **Necrotic Decay** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) When an urdefhan dies, their translucent flesh quickly rots away and sublimates into a foul-smelling gas that fills a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) around the body. This gas deals 3d6 void damage to creatures in this area as their flesh curdles and rots (DC 17 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save).
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** rhoka sword +12 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [two-hand d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)), **Damage** 1d8+6 slashing **Melee** jaws +12, **Damage** 1d6+6 piercing plus Wicked Bite **Ranged** composite longbow +10 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=730)), **Damage** 1d8+4 piercing **Divine Innate Spells** DC 17 - **1st** [Enfeeble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1513), [Gentle Landing](/Spells.aspx?ID=1542) (at will; self only) - **2nd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) **Ravenous Attack** The urdefhan makes one rhoka sword Strike and one jaws Strike against a single creature. Their multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after both attacks. **Wicked Bite** **Requirements** The urdefhan damaged a creature with a jaws Strike on their last action; **Effect** The urdefhan maintains contact, turning the creature's flesh translucent around the site of the injury. The target must succeed at a DC 20 Fortitude save or be affected by drain blood or drain vitality (the urdefhan's choice). If the jaws Strike was a critical hit, the creature is affected by both effects, using the same save result for both.
  • **Drain Blood** The urdefhan drinks some of the creature's blood. On a failed save, the creature is [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 and the urdefhan regains 5 HP (or, on a critical failure, it's drained 2 and the urdefhan regains 10 HP).
  • **Drain Vitality** The urdefhan draws out some of the creature's vital essence. The creature becomes [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 1 hour on a failed save (or enfeebled 2 for 1 hour on a critical failure).
name: Urdefhan Warrior

With their unholy gifts, these urdefhan tormentors summon daemons into battle and bolster their allies with profane magic, preferring to stick to the sidelines rather than enter the fray directly. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Urdefhan Tormentor](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4600) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 337 **Perception** +13; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +8, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +9, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +11, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +12, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +13 **Str** +3 **Dex** +1 **Con** +3 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 **Items** [Warhammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=403) --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +10 **Will** +15 **HP** 75 ([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) **Weaknesses** [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) 5 Necrotic Decay (divine, void) When an urdefhan dies, their translucent flesh quickly rots away and sublimates into a foul-smelling gas that fills a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) around the body. This gas deals 5d6 void damage to creatures in this area as their flesh curdles and rots (DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** warhammer +12 ([Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)), **Damage** 1d8+5 bludgeoning **Melee** jaws +14, **Damage** 2d6+5 piercing plus Wicked Bite **Divine Innate Spells** DC 23 - **1st** [Enfeeble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1513), [Gentle Landing](/Spells.aspx?ID=1542) (self only), [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=1548), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) - **2nd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480), [False Vitality](/Spells.aspx?ID=1523), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552), [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) - **3rd** [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622) **Rituals** DC 23 - **1st** [Daemonic Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=25) **Stoke the Fervent** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The urdefhan lets out a battle cry, sending themself and their allies into a fanatical frenzy. The tormentor and each ally that hears the call gains a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, damage rolls, and saving throws, and takes a –1 status penalty to AC. Affected creatures must use at least one of their actions to Strike each round, if they're able (even if it means attacking an ally, object, or thin air). This lasts for 2d4 rounds. **Wicked Bite** **Requirements** The urdefhan damaged a creature with a jaws Strike on their last action; **Effect** The urdefhan maintains contact, turning the creature's flesh translucent around the site of the injury. The target must succeed at a DC 22 Fortitude save or be affected by drain blood or drain vitality (the urdefhan's choice). If the jaws Strike was a critical hit, the creature is affected by both effects, using the same save result for both.
  • **Drain Blood** The urdefhan drinks some of the creature's blood. On a failed save, the creature is [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 and the urdefhan regains 5 HP (or, on a critical failure, it's drained 2 and the urdefhan regains 10 HP).
  • **Drain Vitality** The urdefhan draws out some of the creature's vital essence. The creature becomes [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 1 hour on a failed save (or enfeebled 2 for 1 hour on a critical failure).

Nosferatu thralls are mortals bound to a nosferatu's will. While thralls aren't [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722), they stay alive by feeding on the blood of their masters. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Nosferatu Thrall](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4601) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 340 **Perception** +16 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +14, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +15, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +14 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 **Items** _[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [greatclub](/Weapons.aspx?ID=377)_ --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +17 **Will** +14 **HP** 135 ([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 5) **Weaknesses** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) 10 **Mindbound** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A nosferatu master exerts a fierce hold over their thrall's mind. If any creature other than the thrall's master targets them with an effect that would give them the [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64) condition, the thrall's master rolls a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) check against it using their Dominate DC – 10 as the counteract check modifier.
**Mortal Shield** **Trigger** The thrall's master would take damage from a Strike or spell attack and is in an adjacent square; **Effect** The thrall throws themself in front of their master, taking half the damage of the attack (before applying any weaknesses or resistances). The thrall's master takes the remaining damage, applying any weaknesses or resistances as normal.
**Rally** **Trigger** The thrall ends their turn more than 30 feet away from their master; **Effect** The thrall [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to their Speed toward their master. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _greatclub_ +19 ([Backswing](/Traits.aspx?ID=545), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)), **Damage** 2d10+10 bludgeoning **Melee** fist +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661)), **Damage** 2d6+10 bludgeoning **Swing Back** **Frequency** once per round; **Requirements** The nosferatu thrall's last action was a greatclub Strike that missed; **Effect** The nosferatu thrall makes another greatclub Strike against the same target, using the previous Strike's multiple attack penalty.
name: Nosferatu Thrall

Nosferatu malefactors spread plague in their wake and yearn for mortal blood. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Nosferatu Malefactor](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4602) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 341 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 60 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +21, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +21, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +19, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +17, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +19, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +23 **Str** +5 **Dex** +7 **Con** +3 **Int** +7 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +21 **Will** +19 **HP** 135 ([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 10, plagued coffin restoration, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** physical 10 (except magical wood) **Nosferatu Vulnerabilities**
  • **Revulsion** A nosferatu can't voluntarily come within 10 feet of brandished garlic or a brandished religious symbol of a deity with a holy sanctification option. To brandish garlic or a religious symbol, a creature must Interact to do so for 1 round (similar to Raising a Shield). If the nosferatu involuntarily comes within 10 feet of an object of their revulsion, they gain the [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) condition, running from the object of their revulsion until they end an action beyond 10 feet. After 1 round of being exposed to the subject of their revulsion, a nosferatu can attempt a DC 25 Will save as a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait. On a success, they overcome their revulsions for 1d6 rounds (or 1 hour on a critical success).
  • **Stake** A magical wooden stake (such as one affected by a weapon potency rune, runic weapon, or similar magic) driven through the nosferatu's heart drops the nosferatu to 0 HP and prevents them from healing above 0 HP, even in their coffin. Staking a nosferatu requires 3 actions and works only if the nosferatu is [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95). If the stake is removed, the nosferatu can heal above 0 HP again, and if they're in their coffin, the 1-hour rest period begins once the stake is removed. If the nosferatu's head is severed and anointed with holy water while the stake is in place, the nosferatu is destroyed.
  • **Sunlight** If exposed to direct sunlight, a nosferatu immediately becomes [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1. The slowed value increases by 1 each time the nosferatu ends their turn in sunlight, and the condition ends when they're no longer in sunlight. If the nosferatu loses all their actions in this way, they're destroyed.
 --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 25 feet **Melee** claw +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 2d10+11 piercing plus plague of ancients **Melee** fangs +23 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 2d12+11 piercing plus Drink Blood **Divine Innate Spells** DC 29 - **5th** [Telekinetic Haul](/Spells.aspx?ID=1716) (×3) **Change Shape** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The nosferatu transforms into a swarm of pale-gray rats. They gain a land Speed of 30 feet and a [climb Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2349) of 10 feet, and they become Large. In this swarm form, the nosferatu can take an action to deal each enemy in the swarm's space 2d10 piercing damage with a DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. A creature that fails its save is also exposed to plague of ancients (see below). **Command Thrall** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Requirements** One of the nosferatu's thralls is present and can hear the nosferatu; **Effect** The nosferatu gives a single command to one of their thralls, which the thrall follows to the best of its ability during its next turn. **Dominate** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) DC 29. The nosferatu can cast [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501) at will as a [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) [innate spell](/Rules.aspx?ID=2232). Casting it requires staring into the target's eyes, giving the spell the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) trait. A creature that succeeds is temporarily immune to that nosferatu's Dominate for 24 hours. Fully destroying the nosferatu ends the domination, but merely reducing the nosferatu to 0 HP is insufficient to break the spell. **Drink Blood** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Requirements** The nosferatu's last action was a successful fangs Strike; **Effect** The nosferatu sinks their fangs into the targeted creature to drink its blood. This requires an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check against the creature's Fortitude DC. On a success, the creature becomes [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1, and the nosferatu regains 13 HP, gaining any excess HP as [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321). Drinking Blood from a creature that's already drained doesn't restore any HP to the nosferatu but increases the creature's drained condition value by 1, killing the victim when it reaches drained 5. A nosferatu can also consume blood that's been emptied into a vessel for sustenance, but they gain no HP from doing so.
The target creature's drained condition value decreases by 1 per week. A blood transfusion, which requires a successful DC 20 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) check and sufficient blood or a blood donor, reduces the drained value by 1 after 10 minutes. **Plague of Ancients** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [virulent](/Traits.aspx?ID=726)) **Saving Throw** DC 29 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 day; **Stage 1** [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (1 day); **Stage 2** drained 2 and [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2 (1 day); **Stage 3** [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1, drained 3, and enfeebled 3 (1 day); **Stage 4** doomed 2, drained 3, and enfeebled 3 (1 day); **Stage 5** [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) (1 day); **Stage 6** death
name: Nosferatu Malefactor
