<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Vanara Disciple](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4604)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Many young vanaras travel, both to see the world and to search for a path that might lead to enlightenment.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 17

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Vanara.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Vanara Disciple](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4604)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
<trait label="Vanara" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=889" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 342

**Perception** +6; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Vanara](/Languages.aspx?ID=72)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +7, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +4, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +4, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +7

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +4

**Con** +0

**Int** +1

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Prehensile Tail** The vanara can use their long, flexible tail to perform [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions requiring a free hand, even if both hands are otherwise occupied. Their tail can't perform actions that require fingers or significant manual dexterity, including any action that would require a check to accomplish, and they can't use it to hold items.

**Items**
[Bo Staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=372), [javelins](/Weapons.aspx?ID=429) (3)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 19 

**Fort** +5 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +8 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 16

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, climb 15 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d6+1 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
bo staff +4 ([Parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=667), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)),
**Damage** 1d8+1 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
javelin +7 ([thrown 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),
**Damage** 1d6+1 piercing

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 13
- **1st**
[Pest Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1626) (monkey only)

**Flurry of Blows** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The vanara disciple makes two fist Strikes. If both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purpose of resistances and weaknesses.

**Spring Up** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** The vanara disciple is [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88); **Effect** The vanara [Stands](/Actions.aspx?ID=2303), then can immediately [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) twice. The Stand action doesn't trigger reactions.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Vaspercham](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4605)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Vasperchams are aquatic horrors who delight in violence, lurking in the shallows near shorelines. Once a vaspercham settles on a home, they stubbornly stay there, regardless of any communities dwelling nearby. A vaspercham's physical might and magic-warping abilities make them incredibly hard to dislodge once entrenched. Vasperchams respond only to strength, so one must best them in combat to gain their begrudging cooperation. But once a vaspercham recovers their strength, they inevitably betray any temporary alliance.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 36

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Vaspercham.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 17">[Vaspercham](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4605)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Huge" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=523" />
<trait label="Aquatic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=533" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 343

**Perception** +30; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663)

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164)

**Skills**
[Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +33, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +33, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +31, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +29, [Sea Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +33

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +8

**Dex** +4

**Con** +6

**Int** +8

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +6
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 41 

**Fort** +31 

**Ref** +25 

**Will** +32 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 335

</row>

**Resistances**
[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 10, [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) 10

**Weaknesses**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 15

**Magic-Warping Aura** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542)) 30 feet. A vaspercham's shell distorts nearby magic. Any creature in the aura who Casts a Spell must attempt a DC 37 Will save. **Critical Success** The spell is unaffected, and the caster is temporarily immune to the magic-warping aura for 1 minute. **Success** The spell is unaffected, but if the spell allows a saving throw, the vaspercham gains a +1 circumstance bonus to save against it. **Failure** If the spell has a target and there are one or more viable targets within its range, the spell's target changes, determined randomly by the GM. If there's no other possible target within range or the spell has no target, the spell is [disrupted](/Rules.aspx?ID=2236). **Critical Failure** The caster instead Casts another Spell, choosing randomly from their spell repertoire, prepared spells, or available focus spells (as appropriate) and selecting any targets at random.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, swim 50 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tentacle +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 3d10+16 bludgeoning plus hallucinatory brine

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
water blast +33 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)),
**Damage** 2d8+16 bludgeoning plus hallucinatory brine

**Arcane Innate Spells** DC 41
- **5th**
[Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1473) (at will)
- **6th**
[Spellwrack](/Spells.aspx?ID=1683) (×3)
- **7th**
[Regenerate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1648)
- **8th**
[Lightning Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=1586)
- **9th**
[Dispelling Globe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1494), [Howling Blizzard](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559)
- **Constant (7th)**
[See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663)

**Hallucinatory Brine**  ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) A creature hit by the vaspercham's Strikes or Mindwarping Tide must attempt a DC 38 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature is overwhelmed with phantasmal visions, becoming [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round (1 minute on a critical failure).

**Mindwarping Tide** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) The vaspercham releases an effusion of noxious water from its shell. Creatures within a 15-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must save against the vaspercham's hallucinatory brine.

**Whipping Tentacles** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The vaspercham makes four tentacle Strikes, each against a different target. These attacks count toward the vaspercham's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after the vaspercham makes all of their attacks.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Forbidden Armor</title>

After a devastating battle with a vaspercham, many legendary heroes have tried to forge armor or weapons from the sea beast’s magical shell, but all have failed thanks to the powerful curse that suffuses the opaline material. If one were able to dispel the curse of a vaspercham’s shell—or somehow twist the curse to their own benefit—they would be able to craft an incredible suit of _[+2](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804)  [ antimagic](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2787) [ greater resilient ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) plate mail_.
</aside>
name: Vaspercham

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Augur](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4606)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These spherical knots of sinewy muscle, serrated blades, and bloody metal are the most common velstracs on [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36). Each augur has only a single eye, from which they can witness the horrors inflicted by other velstracs, who train the augur to expect and appreciate pain. Augurs are 1 foot in diameter and weigh 30 pounds.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Velstrac_Augur.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Augur](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4606)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Tiny" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=600" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
<trait label="Velstrac" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=890" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 344

**Perception** +8; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), painsight

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=166); (can't speak any language)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +8, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +6, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +7, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +4, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +8, [Torture Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +7

<row gap="medium">
**Str** -1

**Dex** +3

**Con** +1

**Int** +2

**Wis** +1

**Cha** -1
</row>

**Painsight** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A velstrac automatically knows whether a creature it sees has any of the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69), and [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) conditions as well as the value of those conditions.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 17 

**Fort** +4 

**Ref** +10 

**Will** +7 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 15

, regeneration 2 (deactivated by [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920))

</row>

**Immunities**
[cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555)

**Weaknesses**
holy 5, silver 5

**Feel the Blades** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it feels the sharp barbs of the augur's blades on its skin. The creature must succeed at a DC 17 Will save or become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 (frightened 2 on a critical failure).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, fly 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
blade +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4–1 slashing plus 1d4 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86)

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 17
- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715)
- **1st**
[Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) (×3)
- **2nd**
[Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445) (×2)
- **4th**
[Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=1647) (once per week)

**Focus Gaze** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The augur stares at a creature they can see within 30 feet. The target must immediately attempt a Will save against feel the blades. After attempting this save, the creature is then temporarily immune to Focus Gaze until the start of the augur's next turn.

**Whirling Slice** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The augur [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) or [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), whirling as they move. The augur deals the damage of their blade Strike to each creature whose space they enter (DC 16 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Each creature is affected only once, even if the augur moves through its space multiple times.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-565" />
name: Augur

Ostiariuses, as emissaries of the velstracs, tend to the portals between [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36) and [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26). They not only escort other velstracs into the world of mortals but also work to entice mortals into the realms of the velstracs—from which most mortals never leave. Among the most pleasant and persuasive of the velstracs, ostiariuses are prepared to converse for hours upon any topic, and they're skilled at returning, again and again, to the subject of the delights found in their perverse philosophies. Ostiariuses stand over 6 feet tall, and individuals range from skeletally thin to hugely bulky. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Ostiarius](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4607) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 345 **Perception** +15; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), painsight, sense portal **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=166); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +12, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +16, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +11, [Torture Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +11 **Str** +0 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 **Painsight** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A velstrac automatically knows whether a creature it sees has any of the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69), and [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) conditions as well as the value of those conditions.
**Sense Portal** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The ostiarius always knows the direction and distance to the closest portal between [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36) and [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26). This sense functions only on these two planes.
 --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +15 **Will** +13 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 65 , regeneration 5 (deactivated by [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920)) **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) **Weaknesses** holy 5, silver 5 **Whispering Wounds** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it hears the wounds on the ostiarius's body whisper obscene truths. The creature must succeed at a DC 21 Will save or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** claw +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d6+2 slashing plus 2d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 24 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671) - **2nd** [Calm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458) (at will), [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480), [Silence](/Spells.aspx?ID=1674) - **3rd** [Enthrall](/Spells.aspx?ID=1516), [Safe Passage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1659) **Rituals** DC 22 - **2nd** [Inveigle](/Rituals.aspx?ID=150) **Compel Courage** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The ostiarius inspires their willing allies and themself by whispering words of courage from their wounds. The ostiarius and their allies in a 50-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls, damage rolls, and saves against fear effects. The ostiarius can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2631) Compel Courage. Non-velstracs who accept this compelled courage find bleeding wounds opening on their own bodies to whisper in thanks. They take 1 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) and can't attempt a [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to end this damage as long as they're compelled. **Focus Gaze** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The ostiarius stares at a creature they can see within 30 feet. The creature must immediately attempt a Will save against whispering wounds. In addition, if the creature was already [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) and fails its save, the creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) by the ostiarius and can't use [hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251). This fascination lasts for 1 round or until the ostiarius takes any hostile action against the creature or the creature's allies. Whether the creature succeeds at or fails the save, it's temporarily immune to Focus Gaze for 1 hour.
name: Ostiarius

Evangelists, the velstracs' unofficial ambassadors, roam the farthest reaches of the planes to spread the word of their kind's abhorrent belief in perfection through pain. They're therefore the most frequently encountered velstracs in [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26), leading covens of hedonistic mortal flesh-sculptors or serving as wardens of horrific dungeons. In regions ruled by infernal powers, evangelists might serve as lieutenants or advisors, whispering secret paths to power in exchange for mortals' souls or choice mortal flesh. Evangelists are the same size as humans, although with their flesh transformed into something resembling a mockery of aristocratic raiments, and they often weigh 350 pounds or more. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Evangelist](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4608) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 346 **Perception** +13; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), painsight **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=166) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +10, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +15, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +11, [Torture Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +12 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +1 **Painsight** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A velstrac automatically knows whether a creature it sees has any of the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69), and [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) conditions as well as the value of those conditions. --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +14 **Will** +11 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 90 , regeneration 10 (deactivated by [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920)) **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) **Weaknesses** holy 5, silver 5 **Unnerving Gaze** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it sees the face of a departed loved one in place of the evangelist's face. The creature must succeed at a DC 21 Will save or become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2 (frightened 3 on a critical failure).
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _morningstar_ +17 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d6+7 bludgeoning plus 1d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) and grievous wound **Focus Gaze** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The evangelist stares at a creature they can see within 30 feet. The target must immediately attempt a Will save against unnerving gaze. In addition, if the creature was already frightened, on a failed save, the evangelist is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) from the creature for as long as the creature remains [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76). After attempting this save, the creature is then temporarily immune to Focus Gaze until the start of the evangelist's next turn. **Grievous Wound** When the evangelist critically hits with a morningstar Strike, the target's wound is particularly gruesome and disorienting. The creature becomes [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1, and the DC to recover from its [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) is 17 (DC 12 when receiving especially appropriate assistance). The clumsy condition doesn't end until the creature recovers from its persistent bleed. **Unleash Weapon** The evangelist releases their morningstar and commands the augur trapped within to attack. The weapon flies off and the evangelist makes up to two morningstar Strikes, each against a different target within 20 feet. These attacks count against the evangelist's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after all the attacks. The morningstar then returns to the evangelist's hand.
name: Evangelist

Sacristans are failures among the velstracs, creatures whose bodies and minds have been utterly broken by the velstracs' torments. These unfortunates are assembled from scrap metal, nerveless flesh, and bits of darkness into loyal agents who take ecstatic pleasure in serving other velstracs. Sacristans are empowered by a miniature gateway to [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36) deep in their mouths. By distending their jaws, they can howl with the shrieks and windstorms of that plane. Sacristans vary in appearance but are the size of average, if maimed, humans. Their features are redundant or absent, and barbed and rusted chains wrap around them. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Sacristan](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4609) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 347 **Perception** +19; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), painsight **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=166) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +19, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +21, [Torture Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +16 **Str** +6 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 **Painsight** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A velstrac automatically knows whether a creature it sees has any of the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69), and [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) conditions as well as the value of those conditions. --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +19 **Will** +17 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 175 , regeneration 10 (deactivated by [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920)) **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) **Weaknesses** holy 10, silver 10 **Staggering Servitude** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it sees a vision of the sacristan groveling in pitiable servitude. The creature must succeed at a DC 27 Will save or become [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** barbed chain +22 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d8+9 piercing plus 1d6 spirit and 2d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 29 - **3rd** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524) - **5th** [Chilling Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1464) **Focus Gaze** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The sacristan eerily stares at a creature they can see within 30 feet. The creature must immediately attempt a Will save against staggering servitude. In addition, if the creature was already [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93), on a failed save, its revulsion toward the sacristan's presence causes it to be [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 2 for 1 minute. After attempting this save, the creature is then temporarily immune to Focus Gaze until the start of the sacristan's next turn. **Shadow Scream** ([Aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=569), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=765)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** The sacristan opens their mouth to unloose the wailing howls and mind-twisting darkness of [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36). This creates a 30- foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) of darkness and wailing sounds around the sacristan. Creatures with darkvision can't see through this darkness. The sacristan can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2631) Shadow Scream for up to 1 minute. Non-velstrac creatures in the area when the ability is used, as well as those who enter or start their turn in the area, must attempt a DC 28 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is then temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) and deafened for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 20 mental damage, and is confused and deafened for 1 round.
name: Sacristan

markdown:
Interlocutors are the most talented surgeonsculptors of the velstracs, carving away flesh and replacing it with new body parts of muscle, sinew, and metal. Each interlocutor structures their individual appearance carefully, but all are towering, multilimbed amalgamations of the strongest limbs, densest bones, and sharpest metals they can find. They continually search for new material to graft to their forms, and their slain foes are rarely found intact, as little is more valuable to interlocutors than a powerful opponent's legs, eyes, or even brain. Due to their unique properties, deposits of skymetals sometimes draw interlocutors to the mortal [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26). Interlocutors average 9 feet in height and weigh approximately 800 pounds. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Interlocutor](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4610) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 347 **Perception** +24; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), painsight **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=166) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +25, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +25, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +26, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +19, [Torture Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +20 **Str** +7 **Dex** +3 **Con** +5 **Int** +2 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +5 **Painsight** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A velstrac automatically knows whether a creature it sees has any of the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69), and [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) conditions as well as the value of those conditions. --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +21 **Will** +26 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 215 , regeneration 20 (deactivated by [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920)) **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) **Weaknesses** holy 15, silver 15 **Glimpse of Stolen Flesh** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it sees pieces of its own body amid the interlocutor's form. The creature must succeed at a DC 29 Will save or become [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)**
**Shadow Siphon** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=693)) **Trigger** The interlocutor would take damage from a spell or magical effect; **Effect** The interlocutor takes half the triggering damage instead. --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** claw +25 ([deadly 2d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d10+13 slashing plus 2d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 33 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689) - **4th** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) (×2), [Sound Body](/Spells.aspx?ID=1679) (×2) - **5th** [Breath of Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=1456) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (self only; to [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36) or [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) only) **Focus Gaze** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The interlocutor stares at a creature they can see within 30 feet. The creature must immediately attempt a Will save against glimpse of stolen flesh. In addition, if the creature was already [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93), on a failed save, it feels its internal organs twist and writhe, and is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 2 for 1 minute. After attempting this save, the creature is then temporarily immune to Focus Gaze until the start of the interlocutor's next turn. **Surgical Rend** This functions as the [Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=73) ability, dealing claw Strike damage. In addition, if the target is a living creature with organs and muscle, the interlocutor opens a precise wound. Until the creature is restored to its maximum Hit Points, thus closing the wound, Strikes against the creature deal an additional 1d6 precision damage.
name: Interlocutor

Eremites roam the planes to seek out ideal portions of other creatures, such as a hero's sword arm or an angel's pinions. Eremites capture these specimens, clinically test their true limits, then harvest the choicest pieces and add them to their own bodies. An eremite might attach tongues to their hand as extra fingers or a fist to the back of their neck in a horrid “improvement.” Eremites typically stand about 7 feet tall and weigh approximately 200 pounds. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Eremite](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4611) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 348 **Perception** +34; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), painsight, [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=166); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +33, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +38, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +36, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +40, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +36, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +34, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +36, [Torture Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +36 **Str** +9 **Dex** +6 **Con** +7 **Int** +6 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +10 **Painsight** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A velstrac automatically knows whether a creature it sees has any of the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69), and [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) conditions as well as the value of those conditions. --- **AC** 45 **Fort** +37 **Ref** +32 **Will** +34 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 375 , regeneration 25 (deactivated by [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920)) **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [nonlethal](/Rules.aspx?ID=2315) **Weaknesses** holy 20, silver 20 **Ignore Pain** An eremite's actions can't be [disrupted](/Rules.aspx?ID=2236) due to damage or Strikes (such as [Reactive Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2256)).
**Paralytic Perfection** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it feels compelled to offer pieces of its own flesh to the eremite. The creature must succeed at a DC 40 Will save or become [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) for 1 round. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 50 feet **Melee** jaws +39 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 4d8+19 piercing plus 2d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) and exquisite pain **Melee** claw +39 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d6+19 slashing plus 2d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), exquisite pain, and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 42 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (to [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36) or [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) only), [Planar Seal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1635), [Shadow Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1666), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will), [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1754) - **9th** [Blessed Boundary](/Spells.aspx?ID=1452), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) (×2), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) (×2), [Seize Soul](/Spells.aspx?ID=1664), [Shadow Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1666) - **Constant (9th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Evisceration** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) **Requirements** The eremite has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90); **Effect** The eremite excises flesh or bone from a creature they've grabbed or restrained. The target takes 6d10 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). **Exquisite Pain** An eremite's knowledge of pressure points and pain centers is unsurpassed. A creature hit by an eremite's melee Strikes must succeed at a DC 40 Fortitude save or be [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 2 (stunned 4 on a critical failure). A creature that critically succeeds is temporarily immune for 24 hours. **Focus Gaze** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The eremite stares at a creature they can see within 30 feet. The creature must immediately attempt a Will save against paralytic perfection. In addition, if the creature was already [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), on a failed save, its unnatural longing causes it to become [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1. After attempting this save, the creature is then temporarily immune to Focus Gaze until the start of the eremite's next turn. **Graft Flesh** **Requirements** The eremite holds a piece of flesh they collected via Evisceration; **Effect** The eremite attaches the stolen flesh to themself. They either regain 100 Hit Points; reduce the value of their [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) condition by 3; or reduce the stage of any affliction affecting them by 3. **Shadow Traveler** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) When an eremite uses [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576), they arrive at exactly their intended destination.
name: Eremite

markdown:
A voracious, flesh-eating carnivore, the viper vine has a single enormous blossom arising from a thick, leafy tangle of snakelike vines. When the plant senses the approach of suitable prey through its sensitive, shallow-buried root system, it rises up like an agitated snake and unfurls its brightly colored bloom, an act that releases a cloud of mind-numbing pollen. Since viper vines gain nourishment by consuming creatures rather than through photosynthesis and absorbing nutrients from the soil, they have developed rudimentary locomotion and can drag themselves along the ground with tentaclelike roots. They even have a form of rudimentary sentience, allowing them to both discern differences in prey and make limited tactical decisions, while also avoiding creatures that are particularly large or dangerous looking. The area around viper vine hunting grounds is often strewn with the partially devoured remains of victims. It's not unusual to find the rotting corpses of wild animals, ill-fated adventurers, and even giants in the plant's immediate vicinity, along with a scattering of incidental treasure left behind on the corpses. A viper vine rarely returns to the carcass of a creature it killed earlier, preferring to hunt fresh meat. **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Viper Vine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4612) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 350 **Perception** +22; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +24 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +7 **Int** -4 **Wis** +5 **Cha** -3 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +24 **Will** +22 **HP** 270 **Resistances** [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 15 **Cold Vulnerability** When exposed to a cold effect, the viper vine is overwhelmed by lethargy, becoming [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1d4 rounds. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** jaws +27 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d6+11 piercing plus 3d6 poison **Melee** vine +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+11 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Captivating Pollen** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) The viper vine releases a 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) of invisible pollen that stays in the air for 5 rounds unless dispersed by a moderate or stronger wind. Each creature that enters or starts its turn in the area must succeed at a DC 33 Will save or be captivated. The viper vine can't use Captivating Pollen for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Captivating Pollen for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1.
**Failure** The creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72), and it must spend each of its actions to move closer to the viper vine as expediently as possible while avoiding obvious dangers. If a captivated creature is adjacent to the viper vine, it stays still and doesn't act. It ceases to be fascinated if it's no longer in the pollen aura at the end of its turn.
**Critical Failure** As failure, plus the creature is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 2 for 24 hours. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** 3d8+8 bludgeoning, DC 33
Viper Vine Pollen While viper vine pollen degrades quickly after it’s harvested carefully from the plant, a character who has a set of alchemical tools can gather and preserve 1d6 doses of pollen with a successful DC 33 [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) or [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) check and 10 minutes of work. A single dose of viper vine pollen is worth 300 gp as raw materials for crafting any alchemical or magical item that creates an [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) effect.
name: Viper Vine

markdown:
These legendary creatures lurking in remote lakes always seem to find their way into the tavern tales of lakeside communities. To some travelers, every lake of respectable size seems to be surrounded by towns full of fishers claiming to have spotted a water orm. These elusive creatures inhabit lakes mainly in cool and gloomy regions. Some claim that water orms are an offshoot of sea serpents and linnorms, but no credible link between these creatures has been found. Water orms have many features that sea serpents don't, such as the ability to understand the rudiments of language. Their natural inclination to avoid contact and remain hidden often remains at odds with their equally compelling curiosity about those they might spy upon the shores of their lakes. Water orm sightings usually occur when they can't help but to rise up to the surface to take a peek at someone (or something) particularly unusual on the beach or floating on the water's surface. These creatures are extremely long lived and can go for decades, or even centuries, with very little to eat. This allows water orms to subsist in lakes without surfacing for many years, even in bodies of fresh water without ample food sources. Water orms might lie in a silty lake bed for years, their elusiveness only contributing to their mythical reputation. When a pet or child goes missing near a lake, rumors might hold that the local water orm is responsible, leading to folktales that caution residents against venturing out alone near the water. While most water orms are described as serpentine or long-necked reptiles, others look similar to bizarrely elongated [seals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=501) or whales, impossibly large sea horses, or long-necked creatures with paddles resembling those of elasmosauruses. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Water Orm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4614) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 352 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +19, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +23 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +5 **Int** -3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +0 **Slow Metabolism** A water orm can go for 10 years without feeding. Beyond this limit, the water orm's hunger causes it to become [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 but doesn't otherwise impact its lifespan. A water orm that's slowed as a result of starvation can remove this condition by using [Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77) to gulp down a meal.
**Undetectable** ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) A water orm automatically tries to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) any [detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=574), [revelation](/Traits.aspx?ID=686), or [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=689) ability attempted against it, using its [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) modifier for the counteract check. --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +19 **Will** +17 **HP** 170 **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 10, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10 --- **Speed** 20 feet, swim 50 feet **Melee** jaws +24 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+11 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** tail +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d6+11 bludgeoning **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77)** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) Large, 2d8+8 bludgeoning, Rupture 22 **Water Travel** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) A water orm can dissolve into water, appearing only as a long, dark, serpentine stretch of liquid. While in this form, a water orm's swim Speed increases to 600 feet, it automatically succeeds at [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381), and it gains a +4 circumstance bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks in water. A water orm can remain in this form for 8 hours, but it can't enter salt water when using this ability. A water orm can return to its normal form by [Dismissing](/Rules.aspx?ID=2245) this action.
Local Orms People who live by the lakes inhabited by legendary water orms have a tendency to give local lake monsters names that sound somewhat homey or even adorable. As a result, such creatures are often regarded as local mascots or good luck charms— particularly in lakeside settlements that depend on fishing as a significant income source.
name: Water Orm

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Wemmuth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4616)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Fertilized by large quantities of spilled blood, such as that found on bloodstained battlefields or in the war-torn regions surrounding besieged cities, wemmuths are vile swaths of vines that draw sustenance from mortal suffering, lapping up blood like water. They possess a rudimentary intelligence and an unquenchable thirst for blood, lying in wait for most of their lives and growing to incredible size in the bloody soil of their grisly homes. A wemmuth's body comprises vines that are scarcely thicker than rope, and a single adult wemmuth system consists of six tons of vines, enough to stretch for 6,000 feet if laid out from end to end in a single straight line. Wemmuths never orient themselves this way, however, instead preferring to wrap themselves into massive mounds approximately 15 feet across and equally thick. The creature condenses its entire mass into a sphere of sharp thorns and lashing vines, resembling a hateful tumbleweed the size of an elephant. Wemmuths commonly dig up massive boulders or entire trees from the ground and incorporate them into their rolling mass, using these objects to bolster their defense against many forms of attack or to hurl at faraway foes with terrifying precision. 

Some speculate that wemmuths are a form of diabolical corruption let loose upon Golarion by House Thrune of Cheliax, perhaps as a scorched earth tactic against their rivals. Influential nobles from Nirmathas and Molthune both point fingers at one another for the wemmuth's creation, Nirmathas citing Molthune's close ties to infernal Cheliax and Molthune blaming the primal magic commonly employed by Nirmathas's many [druids](/Classes.aspx?ID=34) and [rangers](/Classes.aspx?ID=36). Several Varisian tales describe creatures closely matching the wemmuth's description attempting to apprehend a famous folk trickster, while crusaders from Mendev adhere to the belief that the wemmuths were a blight unleashed upon Golarion by Deskari, former demon lord of locusts, before his defeat at the hands of mortal heroes.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Witchwyrd](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4617)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Witchwyrds conceal most of their faces, leaving only their eyes unmasked. Their inscrutability is a boon to one of their most significant interests—mercantilism—and many haughty witchwyrds openly revel in the befuddlement inspired by their mysterious guises and mannerisms. Witchwyrds have a keen eye for new opportunities and markets, and they almost always know when someone tries to pull one over on them.

These four-armed humanoid creatures have hairless blue-gray skin, are typically 6-1/2 feet tall, and weigh 300 pounds. Their hands have three evenly sized and spaced digits in a tripod-like arrangement. When not trying to blend in with the local community, witchwyrds favor outlandish, loose-fitting clothes in bright reds or yellows and a signature conical hat. They tend to prefer the driest, warmest regions of the lands they visit—perhaps an indicator of their mysterious home world. Witchwyrds are notoriously closemouthed about details of this distant place, and with good reason: most witchwyrds on Golarion have never visited their ancestral home. To these witchwyrds, the notion of a home planet is a constant thorn in their side, and when asked, many choose to ignore the question altogether. Others are so agitated by these queries that they respond with impatience or even violence. Some scholars have theorized a connection between witchwyrds and several other fourarmed creatures, but as with questions of their place of origin, witchwyrds have little to say about the topic.

<column gap="tiny">

**Absorb Force** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610)) **Frequency** once per round; **Trigger** A [_force barrage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536) or Force Dart (see below) is fired at the witchwyrd, and the witchwyrd is aware of it and has a free hand; **Effect** The witchwyrd “catches” one force projectile, absorbing it, preventing the damage, and causing that hand to glow while it holds this energy. A hand that's holding energy can't be used for any other purpose except to use Force Dart. The energy lasts for 1 minute or until it's released.

</column>

**Force Dart** <actions string="Single Action to Three Actions" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=666)) The witchwyrd fires one dart of force per action spent (dealing 1d4+1 force damage each). They can't spend more actions on this ability than they have free hands. If they use a hand that has Absorbed Force, that hand hurls two darts instead of one, expending the held energy.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Alien Allies</title>

High-ranking or wealthy witchwyrds rarely travel the planes alone, employing bodyguards to accompany them and leading entourages composed of various strange beings they’ve met during their travels.
</aside>

markdown:
These menacing spiritual remnants of wicked warlords or bloodthirsty generals are towering specters of shadow and death. Like other wraiths, war wraiths haunt the shadowy places of the world but are more likely to travel greater distances to sow terror or amass influence, often sticking to dark glades or sinister ruins when journeying across sun-dappled lands. War wraiths tend to be arrogant and rarely form a pack with others of their kind, preferring instead to dominate groups of ordinary wraiths. A particularly powerful necromancer might compel packs of war wraiths into service. However, a particularly malevolent goal—such as eradicating a bastion of light and life—might draw several war wraiths together in a common purpose. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [War Wraith](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4618) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 356 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +19, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +19 **Str** -5 **Dex** +6 **Con** +3 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +19 **Will** +21 +1 status to all saves vs. [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) **HP** 130 ([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Resistances** all damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), or [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509); double resistance vs. non-magical **Draining Presence** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) 10 feet. A living creature that enters the aura must succeed at a DC 26 Fortitude save or become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1. It recovers after it has been out of the aura for 1 minute. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune to draining presence for 24 hours.
**Sunlight Powerlessness** While in sunlight, a war wraith is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 2 and [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 2. --- **Speed** fly 60 feet **Melee** wraith touch +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)), **Damage** 2d12+6 void **Absorb Wraith** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The war wraith extends their hand toward another wraith creature within 100 feet. The target wraith dissolves and streaks toward the war wraith in a straight [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388), dealing 3d10 void damage to each creature along the line with a DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save. The war wraith absorbs the essence of the target wraith, becoming [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) for 1 minute. They can use their extra action only to [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) or [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167). An unwilling target can resist being absorbed if it succeeds at a DC 28 Will save. **Grip of Fear** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661)) The wraith reaches into an adjacent creature's chest, gripping its heart. The target takes 9d6 mental damage with a DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save. On a critical failure, the creature is also [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) until the start of the wraith's next turn. **Robes of Welcome** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The wraith wraps their robes around an adjacent living creature, exposing it to void's embrace. If any creature is cursed by the wraith's void's embrace, the wraith can't impose void's embrace on another creature. **Void's Embrace** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) If the victim succeeds at a saving throw against this curse while in sunlight, the curse ends. While a creature has this curse, it bypasses the resistance of the wraith that cursed it; **Saving Throw** DC 28 Will; **Stage 1** the victim is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) in any light (1 hour); **Stage 2** the victim gains [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 30 feet but is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) in any light (1 hour); **Stage 3** as stage 2, but the creature also has [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83) (1 hour); **Stage 4** the victim becomes [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) and can't awaken (1 day); **Stage 5** the creature dies and becomes a wraith under the command of the war wraith, its body crumbling to ash
War Wraith Origins Not every war wraith was once an individual. Many of them coalesce over time, merging from multiple wraiths where lost souls accumulate and have their spirits and consciousnesses shorn apart or undermined in some way. These war wraiths tend to think and communicate not as one potent being, but as a riot of voices—often conflicting but truly bone-chilling when they agree to work in unison. In locations where void energy focuses, such as in the Void or on the Isle of Terror, war wraiths form in this way frequently.
name: War Wraith

Wyrwood sneaks specialize in moving unseen behind enemy lines, performing reconnaissance and quietly eliminating threats. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Wyrwood Sneak](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4619) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 357 **Perception** +6; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155); plus one regional language **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +7, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +5, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +7, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +5, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +7 **Str** +0 **Dex** +4 **Con** +0 **Int** +2 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +2 **Items** [Buckler](/Shields.aspx?ID=17) (Hardness 3, HP 6, BT 3), [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=398) --- **AC** 16 (17 with buckler raised) **Fort** +3 **Ref** +9 **Will** +8 **HP** 15 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) **Living Machine** Though their body is an organic construct, a wyrwood is a living creature. They're not immediately destroyed when reduced to 0 HP, but rather fall [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) and eventually die. They don't need to eat or drink. They can be targeted by effects that target living creatures or that target constructs.
**No Breath** A wyrwood doesn't breathe and is immune to effects that require breathing (such as an inhaled poison). --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** shortsword +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d6 piercing **Sneak Attack** The wyrwood's Strikes deal an additional 1d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Zombie Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4622)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The rotted husk of a once great dragon, this abomination has lost all its former splendor, but none of the ferocity. Its patchy, rotted wings don't generate enough lift to keep it aloft, but the foul necromantic energies animating it still allow it to fly, albeit slowly.

<column gap="tiny">

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21

**Viscera Breath** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) The zombie dragon breathes a wave of fetid viscera that deals 5d6 bludgeoning damage and 5d6 poison damage (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature that critically fails is also [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 2. The zombie dragon can't use Viscera Breath again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Hoarding Instincts</title>

Though zombies have no use for wealth—indeed, most don't understand the concept in the first place—zombie dragons retain a hint of their innate tendency to hoard. A fresher corpse might guard the hoard it gathered in life (or what remains of it), while a zombie dragon further from life might instead hoard bones, rocks, corpses, or other unusual objects. The monetary value of these hoards varies widely.
</aside>

name: Zombie Dragon

markdown:
Lean and athirst, the hounds of Tindalos are drawn to those who tamper with the flow of time, travel through time, or use magic or rare alchemical drugs to send their thoughts or perception backward or forward in time. Powerful spellcasters can draw them from the [Dimension of Time](/Planes.aspx?ID=46) via rare rituals, but doing so attracts the hounds' ire, so few who traffic in such rituals live long enough to spread their knowledge. While the hounds possess great cunning and cruel intellect, they rarely interact with other creatures—other than to hunt and destroy those who've attracted their unblinking attention. Once a hound catches the scent of a mortal to hunt, it calls others of its ilk. The pack then pursues its victim through all space and time until it catches, slays, and devours them. Those pursued can escape only by avoiding all angles, as hounds of Tindalos could step through them from nothingness at any time. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 28 **[Recall Knowledge - Time](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Hound Of Tindalos](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4623) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 183 **Perception** +17; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +17, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +13 **Str** +4 **Dex** +6 **Con** +2 **Int** +6 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +17 **Will** +15 **HP** 90 **Immunities** [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) **Resistances** mental 10, physical 10, [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 10 **Otherworldly Mind** ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) Whenever a creature targets the hound with a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect, that creature takes 4d6 mental damage (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). On a critical failure, it also becomes [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1d4 rounds.
**Ripping Gaze** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 30 feet. The hound of Tindalos's eyes glow balefully, causing painful but bloodless wounds to rip open in the body of a creature that meets its awful gaze. When a creature ends its turn in the aura's emanation, it takes 4d6 slashing damage (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). A creature that critically succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Vulnerable to Curved Space** When a hound of Tindalos is not adjacent to a structural angle of 90° (or more acute), its resistance to physical damage is suppressed and it becomes [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1. It can't recover from this sickened condition, but the condition ends automatically once the hound is again adjacent to a suitable angle. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** jaws +17, **Damage** 2d10+7 piercing **Melee** claw +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d8+7 slashing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 21 - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (self only) - **3rd** [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677) - **4th** [Planar Tether](/Spells.aspx?ID=1636) - **8th** [Pinpoint](/Spells.aspx?ID=1633) **Angled Entry** The hound of Tindalos casts a 4th-rank [_translocate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) spell, but it must transport itself into a space adjacent to an angle of 90° (or more acute) in the structure or environment around it. For example, it could teleport to a space adjacent to a wall (using the angle between the wall and floor) or a corner in a room, or adjacent to a sizable tree growing straight up out of the ground, but not to a flat plain or a room with only curved corners and edges.
Once per day, the hound can use this ability to interplanar teleport to or from the [Dimension of Time](/Planes.aspx?ID=46), with the same restrictions on what angles it can appear next to.
Tindalos Ancient texts refer to these relentless temporal hunters as the hounds of Tindalos, yet they never seem to explore what Tindalos actually is. In some references, the implication is that Tindalos is one of the [Great Old Ones or Outer Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=13), but if this is the case, they're among the most obscure of these entities. Other tomes refer to Tindalos as a location, perhaps even a city or nation that once existed before time began and that can be reached only by methods impossible for any creature bound by the laws of time. In all likelihood, both and neither are right—what Tindalos is, is simply impossible for mortal minds to comprehend.
name: Hound Of Tindalos

markdown:
Some mistake these spiderlike spirits as scavengers looking to loot abandoned shrines, but they're actually the opposite: self-appointed guardians of forgotten places of worship, defending the sanctity of discarded faiths and tending sites that have no caretakers. Sometimes, an algriever's duties are tinged with sadness and grief, as they mourn the loss of those who once attended their chosen shrine. Others are filled with frustration and rage, viewing any who stumble upon their shrine as lapsed faithful at best or blasphemers at worse—sinners who deserve punishment. Algrievers have little interest in living creatures until they wander into their territory. Once an algriever's shrine has been breached, they block passage until the interlopers pay their respects. Aggressive algrievers steal from travelers or take hostages until their demands for an offering are satisfied. When algrievers suspect someone of committing blasphemy, no gifts can slake their thirst for vengeance. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 28 **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Algriever](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4624) **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 324 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155) **Skills** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +17, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +19, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +19 **Str** +4 **Dex** +4 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +17 **Will** +21 **HP** 155 **Shrine Bond** The algriever bonds with a shrine after they spend 24 hours at the site. While bonded, the algriever gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks and skill checks.
**Acolyte's Defense** ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540), [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) **Trigger** A creature within sight of the algriever damages, desecrates, or defiles their bonded shrine; **Requirements** The algriever is bonded with a shrine; **Effect** The algriever Strides toward the creature; this movement doesn't trigger reactions. The algriever marks the creature as their enemy and gains a +2 status bonus to attack rolls against that enemy until they select a new enemy with Acolyte's Defense. --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 30 feet **Melee** fangs +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d12+7 piercing plus algriever venom **Melee** web +19 ([range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** web trap **Divine Innate Spells** DC 28, attack +20 - **4th** [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=1499) (×3) - **5th** [Crisis of Faith](/Spells.aspx?ID=1478), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) **Algriever Venom** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 28 Will; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d6 mental damage; **Stage 2** 2d6 mental damage and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1; **Stage 3** 2d6 mental damage and stupefied 2 **Pilgrim's Ward** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Frequency** once per month; **Effect** The algriever touches a willing creature and fills it with reverence for their bonded shrine. As long as the target creature obeys the edicts and anathemas of the algriever's bonded shrine, or until that algriever grants Pilgrim's Ward to a different creature, that creature gains a +1 status bonus to all [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) checks and Will saves. **Web Trap** A creature hit by an algriever's web Strike is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) and stuck to the nearest surface until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 28).
Reactive Punishment An algriever follows the edicts and anathemas of the deity or faith of the shrine to which they're bonded, although they remain uncommitted in most other topics. Regardless of how large the shrine is, an algriever chooses three Medium or smaller objects in the shrine as their own focus for defense and can use Acolyte's Defense against any creature that touches or disturbs those objects.
name: Algriever

markdown:
A bakeneko comes into existence when the life of a cat is extended past its normal lifespan, whether through incredible fortune, magic, or other means. With each passing year, the feline becomes more and more monstrous—growing in size, becoming more intelligent, and gaining an affinity for magic. Eventually, they become able to walk upright on their rear legs, pursue new hobbies, and learn languages. Despite this expanded intellect, many bakenekos prefer to enjoy the first decades of their ascension continuing their lives as usual as a (particularly oversized) house cat. For some, they do so because they want to acquire knowledge of the world that they couldn't comprehend as cats, but for others, it's just a desire to live out their lives with their current families and loved ones before moving on to what comes next. Bakenekos enjoy dancing above all other pleasures and can't resist nodding their heads, tapping their paws, or swishing their tails in time to music. They often animate corpses simply to watch them dance—a pastime that often spells trouble for nearby settlements should the undead get free from the bakeneko's control. Bakenekos typically have a playful and mischievous demeanor, and while they aren't inherently evil creatures, they're still feared for their ability to animate corpses and command fire. Villagers are often frightened half to death by the sight of a flaming cat strolling alongside a walking corpse, and their association with undead and the occult has caused some villages in Tian Xia to vilify all cats. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Bakeneko](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4625) **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 325 **Perception** +9; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407)) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +10, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +8, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +10, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +10 **Str** +3 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +7 **Will** +4 **HP** 35 **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 5 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 5 **Cat at Heart** When a bakeneko is killed, they immediately reincarnate into a normal house cat who can never again become a bakeneko.
**Feel the Beat** Bakenekos love to dance. If a bakeneko begins their turn within 30 feet of a source of music, they become [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 until the start of their next turn as they follow their compulsion to move to the rhythm of the tune by swishing their tail in time, stomping from one hind leg to the other, or clapping their front paws.
**Flammable** Thanks to their high-fat diets, bakenekos secrete flammable oils from their skin that coats their fur. If a bakeneko takes any fire damage, their entire body lights up in flame, turning them into a furry fireball until the end of their next turn. While burning in this way, a bakeneko's Strikes inflict an additional 1d6 fire damage. --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** claw +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 1d8+5 slashing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 20 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715) - **2nd** [Summon Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=1706) (zombie only) **Rituals** DC 20 - **2nd** [Create Undead](/Rituals.aspx?ID=10) (zombie only) **Skilled Ritualist** Bakenekos can only create zombies with create undead but can perform the ritual without secondary casters and lower the casting time to 4 hours
Nekomatas Bakenekos mostly see their animated but mindless zombies as amusements, though they also use them as guardians or even as agents for revenge. Still, they do their best to ensure that undead they animate aren't left to wander free. Most tales of “evil bakenekos” in fact refer to a similar and much more dangerous creature, the nekomata—a level 6 Medium-sized variant of the bakeneko whose bite causes a debilitating disease of the mind.
name: Bakeneko

markdown:
A dalgyal gwishin is a faceless, undead spirit that wears a stone mask. This forlorn undead forms from the soul of a mortal who died a lonely death with no one to remember them and is similarly unable to remember what it looked like in life. A dalgyal gwishin wanders the land of the living in search of any trace of who they were when they were alive. Full of anger, they remain envious of well-loved or famous individuals who are surrounded by close friends and admirers—the type of people who would never share the dalgyal gwishin's fate. While a dalgyal gwishin has little more than a ghostly body made of mist, the featureless stone mask it wears on its head is solid. Even the stolen faces it claims are transitory, cracking off after a day to form what looks akin to a fragile porcelain death mask. Some of these lonely spirits keep these masks and add them to a collection in a remote and forlorn lair, hoping to find companionship in an ever-growing display of silent faces. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Dalgyal Gwishin](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4626) **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 326 **Perception** +12; lifesense (precise) 120 feet, no vision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165); (can't speak any language), [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 120 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +13 **Str** -5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 **Lifesense** The dalgyal gwishin senses the vital essence of living and undead creatures within the listed range --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +15 **Will** +12 **HP** 58 ([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Resistances** all 5 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), or [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509); double resistance vs. non-magical) **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 30 feet, DC 19
**Stone-Faced** While the dalgyal gwishin has no physical body, their stone face is solid. They can't pass through solid objects. --- **Speed** fly 40 feet **Melee** head +12 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d6+2 bludgeoning plus 2d6 mental **Jealous Frenzy** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** The dalgyal gwishin Strikes a creature that has a higher Charisma modifier than themself; **Effect** The dalgyal gwishin enters an envious frenzy as they sense strong ties of friendship and personality within the creature they struck. The dalgyal gwishin becomes [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) 1 but can use their quickened action only to [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) the triggering creature. If the dalgyal gwishin fails to Strike the triggering creature at least once during their turn, Jealous Frenzy ends. It also ends after 1 minute. After Jealous Frenzy ends, the dalgyal gwishin is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 minute. **Peel Face** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Trigger** The dalgyal gwishin critically hits a creature or reduces a creature to 0 Hit Points while in a Jealous Frenzy; **Effect** The dalgyal gwishin presses their stony face to the creature's face, causing a ghostly imprint of that face to peel away and duplicate onto their head. The triggering creature must succeed at a DC 22 Will save or become [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 (stupefied 2 on a critical failure). The dalgyal gwishin's Jealous Frenzy ends, and they don't become [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92).
Faceless Horror The dalgyal gwishin's most significant physical trait—the lack of a face—is shared with the living aberrations known as [noppera-bos](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=677). Despite this shared feature and the ability to steal faces, the two creatures don't cooperate and are more often bitter enemies. Anyone slain by a dalgyal gwishin leaves behind an unnerving faceless corpse.
name: Dalgyal Gwishin

<title level="1" pfs="">[Imprecasia](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4627)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

An imprecasia, commonly known as a curse eater, is a solitary fey drawn to the energy emanating from curses. They appear as an emaciated humanoid creature with shadowy wings, long thin hands with sharp claws, and an insectile head with feathery antennae.

An imprecasia's appearance in a region signifies the land is troubled, but they're not unpopular creatures. On the contrary, an imprecasia's whereabouts are coveted information, as they have the ability to fully consume curses on objects and creatures. Many carrying the weight of a curse seek out curse eaters, most of whom will gladly accept doomed objects or charitably destroy an individual's curse.

An imprecasia will never accept the idea of healing a cursed land or blighted region. One might even seek to strengthen the afflictions on a region simply to ensure a regular supply of food. A bolder imprecasia tracks cursed families over generations, feeding off their misfortune until the curse ends or the bloodline dies off. Any who attempts to destroy such curses might become the enemy of an imprecasia defending their source of sustenance.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 30

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25
</column>

</column>

</column>

</row>

**Skills**
