<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +6

**Con** +5

**Int** +3

**Wis** +6

**Cha** +5
</row>

**Scent Curse** The imprecasia can smell the presence of curses of a level equal to or lower than their own level within 30 feet; this is an imprecise sense.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 31 

**Fort** +18 

**Ref** +23 

**Will** +21 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 180

</row>

**Immunities**
[cursed](/Traits.aspx?ID=567)

**Resistances**
[void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 10

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 10

**Drink Death** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet of the imprecasia is [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69); **Effect** The imprecasia inhales, drawing a thin blue streak of vapor out of the dying creature's body into their mouth. The dying creature's dying condition is reduced by 1, and the imprecasia regains 2d10+9 Hit Points.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claws +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d12+9 slashing plus homeland's hostility

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 30, attack +22
- **Cantrips (5th)**
[Tangle Vine](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713)
- **3rd**
[Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1506)
- **5th**
[Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1453), [Cleanse Affliction](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467) (curses only), [Deafness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1483), [Mirage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1604)

**Devour Curse** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The imprecasia casts [_cleanse affliction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467) against a curse afflicting a creature. If they succeed in removing the curse, they also breathe in vitality from the creature (appearing to inhale blue streaks of vapor from the target's body), causing the creature to become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 unless it succeeds at a DC 30 Fortitude save (drained 2 on a critical failure). If the curse is removed, the imprecasia doesn't expend that use of _cleanse affliction_ as a [primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) [innate spell](/Rules.aspx?ID=2901).

**Homeland's Hostility**  ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) A creature that takes damage from the imprecasia's claws must attempt a DC 30 Will save. On a failure, it treats all terrain within 60 feet of an imprecasia as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2586).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Targeting Items</title>

An imprecasia can cast [_cleanse affliction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467) on a cursed item rather than on a creature, but they can't use Devour Curse on it. They can only do so on an item that's of a level equal to or less than their own level; on a success, the item is uncursed for 24 hours if it's of a level equal to or higher than the imprecasia's level – 4, or uncursed permanently if it's lower level than that.
</aside>
name: Imprecasia

<title level="1" pfs="">[Meokdan (Hungry Nindoru)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4628)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

As the least of their kind, meokdans suffer a constant and painful hunger, for they lack the ability of more powerful nindorus to derive nourishment from a reincarnated soul. This hunger drives meokdans to constantly seek food and often compels them to eat dirt, stones, and refuse that offer little (if any) nutritional value. Regardless of how much they eat, meokdans always remains painfully thin in stature. Regardless of how hungry they get, they never starve to death.

A meokdan's emaciated frame appears human in shape, with thick, tangled hair that grows directly from their face as well as the rest of their head, leaving only the meokdan's gaping mouth visible. Their arms appear to be broken and unnaturally long, with equally long fingers tipped with ragged nails. Small, open sores pockmark their skin.

Meokdans epitomize the cycle of food, from consumption to the creatures themselves being consumed in the end, be it by predators or the environment growing from their remains.

bite +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 1d4+6 piercing

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 18, attack +10
- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718)
- **1st**
[Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568), [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=1632)

**Manifest Bone Club** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Requirements** The meokdan isn't carrying a bone club; **Effect** The meokdan reaches down their throat into their stomach and pulls out a human femur that they can wield as a bone club. After the meokdan Strikes a creature, the bone club shatters, but its sharp fragments remain in roughly the same shape; Strikes with a shattered bone club inflict slashing and [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). After the meokdan Strikes with a shattered bone club, or if the meokdan drops the bone club, the club is destroyed, and the nindoru can Manifest a new bone club.

**Quick Snack** <actions string="Reaction" />  **Trigger** The meokdan critically hits a living creature with their bite; **Effect** The meokdan Snacks on the creature

**Snack** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) The meokdan attempts a bite Strike against an adjacent creature or chews on a handheld object the creature is carrying. If the creature is living or the object is food, the meokdan gurgles in delight and regains 1d8+8 Hit Points. If the creature is undead or a construct, or the object is inedible, the meokdan shrieks in frustration and gains a +1 status bonus to attack rolls and saving throws until the end of their next turn. The meokdan can't Snack again for 1 round.

markdown:
A sojiruh comes into existence when a creature who has dedicated multiple lives to achieving enlightenment turns their back on these goals and deliberately tears themself away from the cycle of reincarnation before their death. These monstrous creatures are the most common nindoru and are also the most hated by followers of Sangpotshi since so many of these fiends rise from fallen members of that very philosophy. This type of nindoru relishes the flavor of an oft-reincarnated soul and finds this life force tastes best when consumed while the physical body still lives and screams. To sojiruhs, the sound of a perfect scream from the body they feed upon is akin to the perfect wine pairing with dinner. A sojiruh's mouth is stitched shut with lengths of their own hair. Despite this, they can speak freely, their hollowsounding voices issuing clearly from sealed lips. They only stretch these stitches when they choose to shriek and scream. Their arms are long and broken-looking, while their chests are little more than rib cages filled with red wet butterflies that crawl through and cling to the creature's ribs and organs. When a sojiruh screams, the wings of the butterflies in their chest flutter frantically. Sojiruhs symbolize the cycle of reincarnation after it has come undone from its purity and has become hopelessly mired in decay and corruption— both spiritually and physically. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 21 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 [Sojiruh](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4629) **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 329 **Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +11, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +10, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +9 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 **Items** [broken shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=398) --- **AC** 17 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +11 **Will** +13 **HP** 58 **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331) **Resistances** [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) 5, [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 5 **Weaknesses** [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) 5 **Soul Shred** Creatures killed by a meokdan are difficult to bring back to life or animate as undead. The meokdan automatically attempts to counteract any attempt to resurrect or animate any creature that they've killed (counteract modifier +11, counteract rank 2). If the sojiruh who slew the creature is slain, the creature can be brought back from death or become undead normally. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _broken shortsword_ +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d6+7 slashing plus 1d6 mental **Occult Innate Spells** DC 21, attack +13 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718) - **1st** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=1740) - **2nd** [Item Facade](/Spells.aspx?ID=1579), [Noise Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616), [Shatter](/Spells.aspx?ID=1670) **Manifest Armor** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The sojiruh manifests a layer of armor on their body that appears to be made of interlocking bones, clinging red butterflies, and wicker weaves, increasing their AC to 21. The armor is permanent but collapses and vanishes if the sojiruh takes a critical hit from a Strike that inflicts bludgeoning, force, or slashing damage, reducing their AC back to 17. **Ruin Wielder** When a sojiruh wields a broken nonmagical weapon, they don't take any of the disadvantages of the weapon being broken. While the sojiruh wields it, the weapon becomes a _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2662) weapon_ that deals an additional 1d6 mental damage on a successful hit. **Stolen Scream** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The sojiruh targets a creature within 30 feet that they can see and then inhales, creating a sound identical to that of the target's scream. The targeted creature must attempt a DC 21 Fortitude save, after which it's temporarily immune to that sojiruh's Stolen Scream for 24 hours. If the creature becomes frightened as a result of Stolen Scream and has had a past life, the sojiruh regains 2d8+8 Hit Points.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 2d6 mental damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 4d6 mental damage and is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 8d6 mental damage and is frightened 3.
name: Sojiruh

markdown:
A guhdggi forms from the corrupted souls of those who have trod the same pilgrimage route through multiple lives. This type of nindoru looks like a bloated frog with long, slender human arms instead of front legs. Their eyes appear bulbous and blind yet are capable of sight. A guhdggi carries a large wicker cage strapped to their back, while dozens of cords and straps affix a trove of broken weapons, tools, and items to their belly and flanks. When they walk, they do so with the aid of two long spears clutched in their forearms, stumbling and hopping in an ungainly gait. The most unsettling feature of the guhdggi is their second face, revealed when the frog-like mouth opens to reveal a human face peering from their throat amid a tangled nest of butterflies. This face has long, dark, animated hair capable of inflicting painful lashes, the lengths of which hang from the corner of the frog's mouth when their lips are closed. While the guhdggi is no longer capable of movement as swift as most humanoids walk, they still enjoy traveling while harboring a ferocious jealousy of those who can do so with greater ease. One would do well not to underestimate the stumbling stagger of a guhdggi, though! The fiend particularly delights in lying in wait along isolated sections of pilgrimage routes to ambush travelers, trapping victims in their cages and feeding upon their captive's mind and memories of previously visited places. Guhdggis embody the cycles of repeated journeys and the concept that every journey taken ends at the start of the next. Central to this concept is the notion that no journey ever truly begins or ends—they just emerge from the previous one. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 25 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 [Guhdggi](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4630) **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 330 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +11, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +11, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +11, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +14 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +2 **Items** [broken spears](/Weapons.aspx?ID=365) (2), [wicker cage](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1698) --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +9 **Will** +14 **HP** 80 **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) **Resistances** [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 5 **Weaknesses** [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) 5 **Soul Shred** Creatures killed by a guhdggi are difficult to bring back to life or animate as undead. The guhdggi automatically attempts to counteract any attempt to resurrect or animate any creature that they've killed (counteract modifier +13, counteract rank 3). If the guhdggi who slew the creature is slain, the creature can be brought back from death or become undead normally. --- **Speed** 15 feet **Melee** broken spear +14 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d6+6 piercing plus 1d6 mental **Melee** hair lash +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d6+6 plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 22, attack +14 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Know the Way](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582) - **1st** [Enfeeble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1513) - **2nd** [Item Facade](/Spells.aspx?ID=1579), [Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=1717) - **3rd** [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677), [Wanderer’s Guide](/Spells.aspx?ID=2043) **Belch Butterflies** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The guhdggi opens both of their mouths and, from their innermost humanoid mouth, belches forth a blast of red butterflies in a 15-foot-[cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). All creatures in the area must attempt a DC 22 Reflex save as the hideous insects' razor sharp wings and tiny claws slash and cut away at exposed flesh. The guhdggi can't Belch Butterflies again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature takes no damage from the butterflies.
**Success** The creature takes 2d6 slashing damage from the butterflies.
**Failure** The creature takes 4d6 slashing damage, 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), and is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 8d6 slashing damage, 2d6 persistent bleed damage, and is sickened 2. **Cage Prey** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) **Requirements** The guhdggi has grappled a Medium or smaller creature with their hair lash and doesn't have a creature caged; **Effect** The guhdggi attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check against the grabbed creature's Reflex DC. If they succeed, they place the creature into the cage they carry on their back. The guhdggi's hair lash no longer grapples the creature, and they can't attack a creature in their cage.
The caged creature is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77). It takes 2d4+6 mental damage when first caged and at the end of each of its turns while it's caged. A caged victim can attempt to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375) or [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) the cage with a DC 22 check, or it can attack the cage's wicker bars, but only with unarmed attacks or with weapons of light Bulk or less. Other creatures can attack the wicker bars as well but can't attempt to Force Open the cage or aid a caged creature's Escape if the guhdggi can resist. Attacks against the cage resolve against the guhdggi's AC, and if the cage takes at least 16 bludgeoning or slashing damage from a single attack or spell, the caged creature gets free.
If the guhdggi dies, their cage crumbles to fragments. **Lurching Skewer** The guhdggi [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), then makes two spear [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167), one with each of the two spears they wield. Both Strikes must have the same target. Apply the guhdggi's multiple attack penalty to each Strike normally. **Manifest Tool** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** The guhdggi manifests a tool from their thoughts. If their next action is to attempt a skill check that could be affected by the tool they chose, they gain a +2 item bonus to the skill check. The tool is then destroyed. **Ruin Wielder** When a guhdggi wields a broken nonmagical weapon (typically two spears), they don't take any of the disadvantages of the weapon being broken. While the sojiruh wields it, the weapon becomes a _[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) weapon_ that deals an additional 1d6 mental damage on a successful hit.
name: Guhdggi

markdown:
Ijdas are creatures of forgetfulness. Born from souls who were once enlightened but then renounced it and their role in the cycle of reincarnation, they curse the futility of their existence and find pleasure in leading others to the same fate. Ijdas appear as towering humanoids, their bodies constantly bursting with nindoru butterflies. Their arms are overly long and so broken as to writhe more like tentacles, affording them an unsettling combat style with greater reach than most large creatures enjoy. They wear clothing and capes made of moldy straw and wield broken swords whose shattered blades still float roughly in place. Their most unsettling features are their complete lack of heads; instead, where a head should be sits a wicker cage filled with a writhing cloud of butterflies. Yet still, they see, hear, and speak without issue, as if their heads remained in place. Formed without heads or even brains, an ijda's mind is shared among the flocks of butterflies in their head cages or bursting from their bodies, and as each of these insects escapes and fades away, these forgetful fiends lose another potential thought. Unable to learn new things, any actions they take stem from memories etched into their souls or directives they blindly follow from other nindorus or creatures they've come to accept as superiors. Their movements, words, and reactions are all conducted via a type of muscle memory based on how their souls believe they should respond to their external stimuli. As a result, most now serve as brutal guardians since their forgetfulness also prevents them from being able to sleep. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Ijda](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4631) **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 332 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +16 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +5 **Int** -2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Items** [broken katana](/Weapons.aspx?ID=411) --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +13 **Will** +12 **HP** 100 **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [emotion effects](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fatigue](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) 5, [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 5 **Weaknesses** [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) 5 **Aura of Forgetfulness** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) 30 feet. A creature that begins its turn in the aura finds it difficult to recall memories or to focus on what it's currently attempting to accomplish, and must attempt a DC 21 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to aura of forgetfulness for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 until the end of its next turn, as it has trouble remembering what it intended to do and spends extra time on its turn trying to recall what it had planned to do.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but slowed 2.
**Soul Shred** Creatures killed by a ijda are difficult to bring back to life or animate as undead. The ijda automatically attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) any attempt to resurrect or animate any creature that they've killed (counteract modifier +14, counteract rank 3). If the ijda who slew the creature is slain, the creature can be brought back from death or become undead normally. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** _broken katana_ +17 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [two-hand d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=718), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d6+8 slashing plus 1d6 mental **Occult Innate Spells** DC 24, attack +16 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482) - **1st** [Sure Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709) - **2nd** [Item Facade](/Spells.aspx?ID=1579), [Stupefy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1691) - **3rd** [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=1632), [Shatter](/Spells.aspx?ID=1670) **Manifest False Memories** ([Aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The ijda releases a cloud of red nindoru butterflies from their body, each of which carries a false memory that infects those they brush against. All non-nindoru creatures within 10 feet of the ijda must attempt a DC 21 Will save. The ijda can't Manifest False Memories for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 by the deluge of false memories until the end of its next turn.
**Failure** The creature is stupefied 1 and [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) until the end of its next turn.
**Critical Failure** The creature is stupefied 1 and confused for 1 minute. It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns to end the effects. **Ruin Wielder** When a Ijda wields a broken nonmagical weapon (typically a katana), they don't take any of the disadvantages of the weapon being broken. While the ijda wields it, the weapon becomes a _[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) weapon_ that deals an additional 1d6 mental damage on a successful hit.
name: Ijda

<title level="1" pfs="">[Kagekuma (Lurking Nindoru)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4632)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A kagekuma exists in the periphery of society, lurking in the very shadows of their favored prey—the working class and downtrodden. Known in some regions as “shadefiends,” this nindoru's ability to appear as uninteresting figures in the crowd allows them to feed even in populated areas. A kagekuma appears as a hunched over humanoid with a long neck and a fanged mouth, but when wearing a bundle, the creature's head and neck tuck in tight between their shoulders to become entirely obscured. The kagekuma personifies the cycle of trust being built and broken over and over again through the course of a complex relationship.

**Soul Shred** Creatures killed by a kagekuma are difficult to bring back to life or animate as undead. The kagekuma automatically attempts to counteract any attempt to resurrect or animate any creature that they've killed (counteract modifier +16, counteract rank 4). If the kagekuma who slew the creature is slain, the creature can be brought back from death or become undead normally.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet (or 15 feet with bundle)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_kusarigama_ +19 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [monk](/Traits.aspx?ID=656), [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716), [versatile B](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 2d8+7 slashing plus 1d6 mental

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
bite +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d6+7 piercing plus 1d6 mental

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 26, attack +18
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718)
- **2nd**
[Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455), [Item Facade](/Spells.aspx?ID=1579)
- **3rd**
[Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Veil of Privacy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1739)
- **4th**
[Rewrite Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=1655), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724)

**Manifest Bundle** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** The kagekuma manifests a large bundle of straw, reeds, rope, yarn, hair, or the like, all lashed together with cords and strapped to their back. The kagekuma's AC increases to 27 as long as their bundle remains manifested, but their Speed is reduced to 15 feet.

**Ruin Wielder**   When a kagekuma wields a broken nonmagical weapon (typically a kusarigama), they don't take any of the disadvantages of the weapon being broken. While the kagekuma wields it, the weapon becomes a _[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) weapon_ that deals an additional 1d6 mental damage on a successful hit.

**Shadow Bite** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Requirements** The kagekuma is casting a shadow; **Effect** The kagekuma targets an adjacent creature that's casting a shadow, then their shadow feeds on that creature's shadow. If the kagekuma has a manifested bundle, observers (including the creature whose shadow is being bitten) can notice the kagekuma's Shadow Bite only if they succeed at a DC 38 [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=3303) check. The target must succeed at a DC 26 Fortitude save or become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (drained 2 on a critical failure).

**Shed Bundle** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** The kagekuma has a manifested bundle; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The kagekuma causes its bundle to vanish, [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), and then attempts a kusarigama Strike and a bite Strike. The target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against both of these Strikes.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Kagekuma Bundles</title>

A kagekuma's bundle is more than just a disguise and armor—it's a physical indication of how many lives they've cut short and how many souls they've supped upon. Whether the bundle is composed of straw, ropes, hair, or other materials, the individual lengths symbolize a sort of “reaper's harvest,” with each strand in the bundle representing a metaphorical lifeline that the nindoru has severed and collected—to an extent that the multitude of lives taken overwhelms the nindoru's own presence in the world.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-676" />
name: Kagekuma

<title level="1" pfs="">[Akashti (Sobbing Nindoru)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4633)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Akashtis are manipulative fiends who prey on those in mourning; they assume the form of a recently deceased loved one so they can infiltrate homes, then murder and consume the souls of grieving families. Sometimes called a “copycorpse,” an akashti in their true form appears as a skincovered skeleton with sharp talons on their fingers and tears of blood dripping from their lidless eyes. Their skull appears open like a bowl, and inside nest handfuls of crimson butterflies. When an akashti hides within their manifested body, close investigation can sometimes reveal the truth when the scalp of the supposed beloved one returned to life seems to subtly ripple and bulge as the butterflies within writhe. 

Akashtis embody the complex cycle of grief. While they typically prefer to work on their own, it's not unheard of for a shisagishin to recruit the aid of a few akashtis when a region has suffered a great disaster, and there are a large number of bodies—and thus a large number of mourners—to take advantage of.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 32

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Akashti.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 10">[Akashti](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4633)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=600" />
<trait label="Nindoru" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=904" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 335

**Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165)

**Skills**
[Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +23, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +21, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +17, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +19, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +21

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +5

**Dex** +5

**Con** +3

**Int** +3

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +7
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 29 

**Fort** +17 

**Ref** +21 

**Will** +19 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 175

</row>

**Resistances**
[void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 10

**Weaknesses**
[silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) 10

**Soul Shred** Creatures killed by a akashti are difficult to bring back to life or animate as undead. The akashti automatically attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) any attempt to resurrect or animate any creature that they've killed (counteract modifier +19, counteract rank 5). If the akashti who slew the creature is slain, the creature can be brought back from death or become undead normally.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d6+11 slashing plus 1d6 mental

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 29, attack +21
- **Cantrips (5th)**
[Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598)
- **2nd**
[Item Facade](/Spells.aspx?ID=1579)
- **4th**
[Honeyed Words](/Spells.aspx?ID=1558), [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=1632), [Talking Corpse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712)
- **5th**
[Illusory Creature](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567), [Wave of Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757)

**Manifest Body**  ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The akashti manifests a hollow humanoid body around their slender skeletal frame over the course of 10 minutes of concentration. If this time is spent within 10 feet of a humanoid body that has been dead for less than a week, the akashti's manifested body looks almost identical to that of the dead humanoid when they lived and functions as a disguise of that creature. If the akashti can consume a portion of the body they're duplicating, they gain a +4 circumstance bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) the creature. While wearing a manifested body, the akashti loses their claw Strike, and their voice sounds like that of the dead creature; they don't possess any of the dead creature's memories or skills and must rely on deception to continue the charade in the face of scrutiny. An akashti's manifested body decays swiftly if they don't take 10 minutes once per day to maintain the manifestation of the false flesh. The body has 10 Hit Points and the same defenses as the akashti. When the body is destroyed, the akashti emerges unharmed, and the fleshy shell left behind scatters into a cloud of red butterflies that swiftly fade into red mist.

**Stunning Despair** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The akashti unleashes overwhelming sobs of despair. Creatures within 30 feet must attempt a DC 29 Will save or become [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1 (stunned 3 on a critical failure). The akashti can't use Stunning Despair again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Deathly Invaders</title>

Sometimes, akashtis ambush and murder targets not to bask in the grief of their loved ones, but instead to assume their form and infiltrate an organization from within to corrupt established bonds of camaraderie. Inevitably, though, these akashtis grow bored with deception and lead their new allies into a trap for slaughter.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-676" />
name: Akashti

markdown:
The shisagishin takes delight in infiltrating a community and then disguising themself as the horribly decayed or mutilated remains of a loved one or adored public figure, only to spring to life in their true form (a many-limbed humanoid with a hollow head that glows from within like a lantern) during funeral services. A shisagishin epitomizes the cycle of decay giving rise to new life. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 35 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 [Shisagishin](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4634) **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 336 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +25, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +24, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +22, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +25 **Str** +4 **Dex** +7 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +25 **Will** +20 **HP** 214 **Resistances** [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 10 **Weaknesses** [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) 10 **Burst From Death** ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) **Trigger** A creature touches the disguised shisagishin or a coffin or other container the shisagishin has been placed into; **Requirements** Initiative hasn't yet been rolled, and the shisagishin is disguised as a corpse with Manifest Corpse Shell; **Effect** The shisagishin violently unfolds into their true form, shredding their decaying corpse shell, and attempts a claw Strike on an adjacent target before they roll initiative.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)**
**Soul Shred** Creatures killed by a shisagishin are difficult to bring back to life or animate as undead. The shisagishin automatically attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) any attempt to resurrect or animate any creature that they've killed (counteract modifier +21, counteract rank 6). If the shisagishin who slew the creature is slain, the creature can be brought back from death or become undead normally. --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** claw +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [deadly d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d8+7 slashing plus 1d6 mental and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 32, attack +24 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598) - **2nd** [Blood Vendetta](/Spells.aspx?ID=1454) (at will), [Item Facade](/Spells.aspx?ID=1579) - **5th** [Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=1570), [Vision of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742) - **6th** [Vampiric Exsanguination](/Spells.aspx?ID=1735) **Manifest Corpse Shell** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The shisagishin tucks their arms and legs into their body, drops prone, and manifests a decaying and/or mutilated humanoid corpse around themself over the course of 10 minutes of concentration. The shisagishin can cause this corpse to appear to be the remains of a specific creature they've seen before by [Impersonating](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) that creature with [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38). Once their corpse shell has manifested, until the next time the shisagishin acts, they appear to be a corpse. They have an automatic result of 44 on Deception checks and DCs to pass as a corpse. **Spread Decay** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The shisagishin's lantern face glows, causing the flesh of any number of creatures grabbed or restrained by them to swiftly begin to decay. Each of those creatures must attempt a DC 32 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 2d8 void damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 4d8 void damage and is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 6d8 void damage and is enfeebled 2.
Handmade Habits A shisagishin prefers to roam areas already rife with conflict and potential accidents, but they'll happily create such a location if needed. They might poison wells, introduce disease to healthy livestock, or even collaborate with corrupt humans if it means the freedom to kill. Cursed towns or haunted roads are sometimes the playground of such a shisagishin.
name: Shisagishin

markdown:
Most nindorus are harmed by silver, but not the argyrzei. Instead, argyrzeis use silver as a method of spreading sickness and pain, and to help them manipulate the minds of those who unwittingly fall under the curse of their infected gifts. They delight in disguising themselves and then selling or giving infected silver to victims. Other nindorus particularly fear argyrzeis for their mastery over silver, knowing that they're capable of causing painful wounds with their silver blades. Argyrzeis appear humanoid, but their feet point backward, and they have no heads. Their glowing red neck stumps are stuffed with a wide assortment of knives, blades, and shards of metal. These nindorus represent the cycle of art being created, destroyed, and recycled into new art. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Argyrzei](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4635) **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 337 **Perception** +23; cursesense, [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +27, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +25, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +24, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +25, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +24 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** +6 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 **Cursesense** The argyrzei can see and hear through any silver object they cursed, as long as the object is within 1 mile. --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +24 **Will** +21 **HP** 240 **Resistances** [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) 10, [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 10 **Headless** An argyrzei is headless but can still hear, speak, see, and smell as if they had a head. The argyrzei is immune to decapitation effects, such as those caused by [_vorpal_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2853) weapons.
**Soul Shred** Creatures killed by an argyrzei are difficult to bring back to life or animate as undead. The argyrzei automatically attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) any attempt to resurrect or animate any creature that they've killed (counteract modifier +23, counteract rank 7). If the argyrzei who slew the creature is slain, the creature can be brought back from death or become undead normally. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** silver blade +28 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [two-hand d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)), **Damage** 2d8+11 slashing plus 1d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) and 1d6 mental **Occult Innate Spells** DC 33, +25 attack - **Cantrips (7th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718) - **3rd** [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) (at will), [Item Facade](/Spells.aspx?ID=1579) - **5th** [Subconscious Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1692) - **6th** [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501), [Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=1570) - **7th** [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=1632) **Infect Silver** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The argyrzei concentrates on an adjacent silver object that’s no larger than 2 Bulk. That object becomes cursed; if the object is being carried, the object’s carrier can resist the object becoming cursed with a DC 33 Will save. The argyrzei can maintain up to five curses simultaneously; if they infect a sixth silver item, the oldest cursed item becomes uncursed. See [infected silver](/Curses.aspx?ID=107). **Manifest Silver Blade** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Requirements** The argyrzei isn’t carrying a silver blade; **Effect** The argyrzei pulls one of the broken silver weapons from the stump of their neck, transforming it into a _[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [ wounding ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2854) [ silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) [ bastard sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=370)_. This weapon crumbles into sand if it leaves the argyrzei’s possession.
Sometimes, an argyrzei might manifest a different type of weapon than a blade—particularly if they’re infiltrating or preying upon an organization that utilizes a signature weapon. In this case, the appearance of the silver blade can change and might inflict bludgeoning or piercing damage, but its other effects are unchanged.
name: Argyrzei

<title level="1" pfs="">[Noppera-Bo Grunt](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4637)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Noppera-bo grunts lack the social graces necessary to fully disguise themselves with another person's face.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/NopperaBo_Grunt.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 0">[Noppera-Bo Grunt](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4637)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=523" />

They don't breathe and are immune to effects that require breathing. They gain sustenance by absorbing fluids through their skin—traditionally by immersing their hands or faceless head into the fluid to “drink” through osmosis; they can also benefit from consumables, such as potions or elixirs, in this way. **Items** [Staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=367) --- **AC** 17 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +9 **Will** +8 **HP** 30 **Immunities** [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=634), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) **Weaknesses** sensitive hearing **Sensitive Hearing** As long as a noppera-bo is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66), they're [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** staff +7 ([two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)), **Damage** 1d4+3 bludgeoning **Occult Innate Spells** DC 18, attack +9 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671) - **1st** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=1632), [Sleep](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675) **Remove Face** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Requirements** The noppera-bo occultist is [Impersonating](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) a creature with a face; **Effect** The noppera-bo occultist's facial features melt into nothingness, and they menace a creature within 30 feet. The creature must succeed at a DC 18 Will save or become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 (frightened 2 on a critical failure). **Impersonate Shape** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The noppera-bo occultist changes into a version of a Small or Medium humanoid they've touched during the last 24 hours. While transformed and their face is covered, the occultist gains a +2 circumstance bonus to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) that creature. As long as the noppera-bo occultist has a face, they lose their no face ability and their listed immunities. The noppera-bo occultist can change back by using this ability again. **Steal Face** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** The noppera-bo occultist is faceless; **Effect** The noppera-bo occultist targets one creature within 30 feet. The creature must attempt a DC 18 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) until the start of its next turn.
**Failure** The noppera-bo gains the target's face. The target's face vanishes until the end of its next turn. Until then, it is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59), can't speak or ingest, and can't smell. The target must also hold its breath or begin to suffocate.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the effect doesn't end automatically. The target can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each such attempt.

markdown:
Noppera-bos are faceless yokai who delight in tricking others. The noppera.bo impersonators present in this adventure are direct manifestations of Kugaptee's lingering presence in Governor Heh Shan-Bao's mindscape. When this latest mindscape cycle began, the duplicate PCs who dwelled in this mindscape were like the other townsfolk--phantasmal but harmless. As the PCs grew in power in the Willowshore mindscape, the sinister thought energy in Heh Shan-Bao's mindscape reacted and transformed the PCs' duplicates into dangerous monsters: noppera-bo impersonators. Note that you can adjust the statistics of the noppera-bo impersonator presented below, using the rules for [monster creation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2874) found in [Pathfinder GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) to challenge other PCs of different levels. Impersonator RolesA noppera-bo impersonator plays one of three roles; you'll need to decide what each impersonator's role is depending on the PC being impersonated. Martial Impersonator**Enhanced Weapon** Increase the noppera-bo impersonator's attack modifiers and damage of their Strikes by 2.
**Hearty Increase** the noppera-bo's Fortitude save modifier by 3, but decrease their Will save modifier by 3.
**Impersonated Abilities** The noppera-bo impersonator gains one of the following abilities of your choice.
  • **Armored** The noppera-bo appears to be heavily armored, and they increase their AC to 24.
  • **Reactive Strike**
  • **Shield Carrier** The noppera-bo carries a minor sturdy shield (that transforms into a useless prop if it's destroyed, dropped, or upon the noppera-bo's death), and they gain the [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212) general feat.
  • **Weapon Perk** The noppera-bo chooses one of these weapon traits to add to their false weapon: [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=667), [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694), [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708), or [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716).
Skilled Impersonator**Deft** Increase the noppera-bo's Reflex save modifier by 3, but decrease their Fortitude save modifier by 3.
**Enhanced Skills** Increase the noppera-bo impersonator's skill modifiers by 2.
**Impersonated Abilities** The noppera-bo impersonator gains one of the following abilities of your choice.
  • **Commander** The noppera-bo impersonator gains a minion that they can command. This creature takes the form of the impersonated character's [animal companion](/Rules.aspx?ID=2113), [eidolon](/Classes.aspx?ID=18), or other fighting companion creature; choose a 4th-level creature that approximates the impersonated companion creature's abilities. This creature gains the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=653) trait.
  • **Elemental Attack** ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)) Choose from [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732), or [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508) (this choice can't be changed once you select it for the impersonator). The noppera-bo impersonator targets a creature within 60 feet and creates a detonation of that element; that creature takes 4d6 slashing (air), bludgeoning (earth, water, or wood), piercing (metal), or fire (fire) damage (DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Elemental Attack gains the appropriate elemental trait.
  • **Sneak Attack** The noppera-bo impersonator deals an additional 1d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.
  • **Weapon Perk** The noppera-bo impersonator chooses one of the following weapon traits to add to their false weapon: agile, [backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=544), [disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), sweep, or trip.
Spellcasting Impersonator**Determined** Increase the noppera-bo's Will save modifier by 3, but decrease their Reflex save modifier by 3.
**Impersonated Spellcasting** The noppera-bo impersonator gains one of the following suites of innate spells, matching the spellcasting tradition utilized by the character being impersonated. You can adjust the spells to make a more perfect fit for the impersonation.
  • **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 24, attack +16; **3rd** [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530), [_slow_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677); **2nd** [_acid grip_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1436), [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577); **1st** [_enfeeble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1513), [_force barrage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536); **Cantrips (3rd)** [_electric arc_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1509), [_frostbite_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539)
  • **Divine Innate Spells** DC 24, attack +16; **3rd** [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [_vampiric feast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1736); **2nd** [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [_noise blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616); **1st** [_bane_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1447), [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470); **Cantrips (3rd)** [_divine lance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498), [_shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671)
  • **Occult Innate Spells** DC 24, attack +16; **3rd** [_paralyze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622), [_phantom pain_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1632); **2nd** [_illusory creature_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567), [_paranoia_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1623); **1st** [_dizzying colors_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1500), [_soothe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1678); **Cantrips (3rd)** [_daze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [_telekinetic projectile_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718)
  • **Primal Innate Spells** DC 24, attack +16; **3rd** [_lightning bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1586), [_wall of thorns_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1752); **2nd** [_floating flame_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1533), [_web_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2045); **1st** [_grease_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1547), [_spider sting_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1684); **Cantrips (3rd)** [_caustic blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1461), [_tangle vine_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713)
**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Noppera-Bo Impersonator](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4640) **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 340 **Perception** +14; no face **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 30 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +12, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +12, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +14, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +14 **Str** +4 **Dex** +4 **Con** +4 **Int** +4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +4 **No Face** Noppera-bos have no sense of vision, but their sense of hearing is precise. They don't breathe and are immune to effects that require breathing. They gain sustenance by absorbing fluids through their skin—traditionally by immersing their hands or faceless head into the fluid to “drink” through osmosis; they can also benefit from consumables, such as potions or elixirs, in this way. --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +14 **Will** +14 **HP** 93 **Immunities** [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=634), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) **Weaknesses** sensitive hearing **Sensitive Hearing** As long as a noppera-bo is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66), they're [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** false weapon +15, **Damage** 2d8+8 mental **Ranged** false weapon +15, **Damage** 2d4+8 mental **False Weapon** A noppera-bo impersonator carries a _[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) weapon_ that looks identical to the favored weapon of the character being impersonated. If the PC favors unarmed Strikes, it appears as [handwraps](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3086). If the impersonated character favors a ranged weapon, then the noppera-bo impersonator has no melee weapon and instead uses the ranged false weapon Strike above; otherwise, the noppera-bo impersonator has no ranged weapon. If the character favors no weapon, then the noppera-bo wields a [club](/Weapons.aspx?ID=357) as their false weapon. This false weapon is only partially real and inflicts only mental damage when it hits; it gains none of the original weapon's traits or magical enhancements. If a noppera-bo impersonator's false weapon is [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60), [disarmed](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383), or set down, it fades away. The noppera-bo impersonator can craft a replacement false weapon by spending 10 minutes to recreate one.
Impersonating Npcs The noppera-bo impersonators in this adventure focus on duplicating PCs, but they're just as adept at duplicating and replacing NPCs in the world. Sometimes, a noppera-bo impersonator's role as a disguised NPC in a position of power can last for years or even decades.
Impersonator Adjustments Since the exact mix of PCs in each _Season of Ghosts_ campaign will vary, the statistics here should be adjusted as needed to match each PC as closely as possible. Don't worry too much about exactly matching each character's rules choices since monsters follow different rules than PCs do—rather, look at ways you can present these creatures in ways that make them feel like monstrous duplicates. Each noppera-bo impersonator is faceless, but their gear and equipment should look identical to what the PCs have when they arrive in Heh Shan-Bao's mindscape. Of course, the bulk of this equipment is all part of the illusion—when a noppera-bo impersonator is slain, their gear shifts and transforms into ragged, broken props that only vaguely resemble the items carried and used by each PC.
name: Noppera-Bo Impersonator

**Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +24, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +22, [Pharasma Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +24, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +22

<row gap="medium">
**Str** -5

**Dex** +5

**Con** +0

**Int** +4

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +6
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 31 

**Fort** +18 

**Ref** +22 

**Will** +24 

+1 status to all saves vs. [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 160
([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83))

</row>

**Immunities**
[bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95)

**Resistances**
All damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), or [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509); double resistance to non-magical)

**Imprisoning Grave** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) 30 feet.The soul of a creature that dies within the path maiden's aura is trapped within its corpse until its body spends 1 minute outside the aura. While trapped, the soul can't move on to the afterlife but can still be brought back to life or transformed into an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creature.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** limited flight 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
apostate's shroud +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 3d10+8 mental

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 32, attack +24
- **Cantrips (6th)**
[Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Know the Way](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718)
- **4th**
[Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1620), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724)
- **5th**
[Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=1570), [Wave of Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757)
- **6th**
[Phantasmal Calamity](/Spells.aspx?ID=1630), [Spirit Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1685)

**Rituals** DC 32

- **2nd**
[Create Undead](/Rituals.aspx?ID=117)

**Limited Flight**   A path maiden has a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) of 25 feet but can't fly higher than 10 feet above the ground. If she travels above a nonsolid surface, she vanishes and rematerializes on the last space she occupied over solid ground and becomes stunned 1. Limited flight allows a path maiden to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) any incline that requires an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check of DC 25 or lower by treating the incline as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) but is blocked by climbs requiring higher DCs.

**Scorn Fate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The path maiden targets a creature she can see. The creature must attempt a DC 32 Will save. On a failure, the creature's next roll is cursed. If the result of the roll is a critical success, it becomes a regular success. A success becomes a failure, and a failure becomes a critical failure. That creature is then temporarily immune to Scorn Fate for 24 hours.

**Vengeful Frenzy** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The path maiden targets a creature within 60 feet that she can see. The target must attempt a DC 32 Will save. On a failure, the target is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) until the end of its next turn, but it can't attack path maidens or itself as the result of this confusion.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Gossamer Sash</title>

The jorogumo of Shenmen sometimes grant these white and violet [sashes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4110) to those who ask them for aid in contacting path maidens for advice.
</aside>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Path Nomads</title>

Path maidens almost always manifest in feminine forms. Those who manifest in masculine forms are instead known as path nomads. These undead have the same abilities but tend to travel more widely; they represent turning one's back on [Pharasma's](/Deities.aspx?ID=290) role as a prophet and manipulator of destiny rather than of her roles over birth and death.
</aside>
name: Path Maiden

markdown:
Shimmerthieves are blind, lizard-like cave dwellers with an insatiable yearning to consume the rare delicacy of light. This hunger compels them to travel beyond their subterranean lairs to the surface to feed, leaving pockets of darkness behind as they go. Shimmerthieves are far from unintelligent yet prefer to avoid other sapient creatures, aware that many find them off-putting. They become independent at a young age but are protective of their kind, and they travel in small groups when able. Prudent shimmerthieves try to avoid feeding on light in busy areas, but such places are also the most tempting, as bustling populations mean more light sources. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 25 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 [Shimmerthief](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4642) **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 344 **Perception** +15; echolocation 120 feet, shimmervision **Languages** [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156) **Skills** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +17 **Str** +4 **Dex** +6 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +0 **Echolocation** A shimmerthief can use their hearing as a precise sense at the listed range.
**Shimmervision** A shimmerthief's sight allows them to sense sources of light from the same distance as creatures with standard vision but doesn't allow them to make out details. They can't detect anything other than light sources using this vision. --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +17 **Will** +15 **HP** 119 **Immunities** [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59), [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) **Gleam Theft** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=569), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) 10 feet. A shimmerthief constantly absorbs ambient light. [Bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) in their aura becomes [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403), and dim light becomes [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2404). A shimmerthief can suppress or activate this aura as a single action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** jaws + 17, **Damage** 2d8+10 piercing **Melee** claw +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d6+10 slashing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 25, attack +17 - **2nd** [Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455), [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) (at will) - **4th** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) **Drain Light** ([Darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=569), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The shimmerthief targets a light source within 30 feet, then attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) it with a spell attack roll (counteract rank 4). If they succeed, the shimmerthief regains 2d8+10 Hit Points (4d8+20 Hit Points on a critical success). **Lasting Darkness** **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** The shimmerthief casts [_darkness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) as a 2nd-rank spell; **Effect** The duration of the shimmerthief's darkness increases to 24 hours. **Unnerving Shriek** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The shimmerthief releases an unearthly wail, forcing creatures within 60 feet to attempt a DC 24 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is frightened 2 and [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) for as long as it remains frightened.
Alternate Diets Some shimmerthieves develop a taste for and the capacity to consume energy forms far more insidious than light, allowing them to instead feed on life energy or thought energy. Such behavior is considered monstrous by most shimmerthieves and is a reason for exile. Shimmerthieves with these proclivities are dangerous predators who maintain the ability to feed on light and purposely create pockets of darkness to trap and feed on victims. A shimmerthief who feeds on life energy deals void damage with their jaws, while one that feeds on thought energy deals mental damage with their jaws.
name: Shimmerthief

<title level="1" pfs="">[Shinigami](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4643)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Even immortals such as kami aren't immune to death. When a kami or equally powerful entity refuses the call of the Boneyard, a shinigami is summoned to bring these stubborn souls to heel. Exact and swift, shinigami are known to dispense the judgment of death efficiently and dispassionately.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 38

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Shinigami.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 17">[Shinigami](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4643)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Monitor" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=655" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 345

**Perception** +31; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34), [Utopian](/Languages.aspx?ID=36); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +31, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +30, [Boneyard Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +32, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +32, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +34, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +30, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +33, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +30, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +31

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +8

**Dex** +6

**Con** +7

**Int** +5

**Wis** +6

**Cha** +9
</row>

**Items**
_[+2 greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [ astral](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2832) [ naginata](/Weapons.aspx?ID=297)_, pouch of four gems

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 40 

**Fort** +32 

**Ref** +29 

**Will** +31 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 260
([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 40)

</row>

**Immunities**
[death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)

**Resistances**
all physical 15 (except silver), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 15, [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 15

**Weaknesses**
[silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) 15

**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 60 feet, DC 35

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, fly 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_naginata_ +33 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 3d8+16 slashing plus 1d6 spirit and spirit touch

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 38
- **7th**
[Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576), [Spirit Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1685) (×3)
- **8th**
[Execute](/Spells.aspx?ID=1519) (×3), [Scrying](/Spells.aspx?ID=1662)
- **9th**
[Seize Soul](/Spells.aspx?ID=1664) (×3)
- **Constant (9th)**
[Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727), [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The shinigami takes on the appearance of a specific Small or Medium humanoid creature. This doesn't change their statistics, other than to reduce the reach of their naginata Strike to 10 feet. The shinigami's humanoid shape is unique to the shinigami and can't be used to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) a creature.

**Pouch of Four Gems**   Each shinigami carries a pouch containing four black sapphires for use with [_seize soul_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1664). Each gem is capable of holding a soul of a maximum level equal to that of the shinigami. If the shinigami dies, the gems are destroyed as well (whether they contain a soul at the time or otherwise), and any souls they contain are released harmlessly to travel to the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39).

**Release Soul** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate Requirements One of the black sapphires in the shinigami's pouch contains a soul from a casting of seize soul](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) Effect The shinigami crushes the gem in their hand, sending the soul on to the Boneyard while releasing a wave of powerful energy. All creatures within a 30-foot emanation take 18d6 damage (DC 38 basic Fortitude save); the shinigami chooses whether this damage is vitality damage or void damage. A creature that takes damage from this effect and fails the saving throw is also sickened 2 and drained 1 (or sickened 4 and drained 2 on a critical failure).

**Release Soul** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** One of the black sapphires in the shinigami's pouch contains a soul from a casting of seize soul; **Effect** The shinigami crushes the gem in their hand, sending the soul on to the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39) while releasing a wave of powerful energy. All creatures within a 30-foot emanation take 18d6 damage (DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save); the shinigami chooses whether this damage is vitality damage or void damage. A creature that takes damage from this effect and fails the saving throw is also [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 2 and [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (or sickened 4 and drained 2 on a critical failure).

**Spirit Touch**   A shinigami's Strikes can affect [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) creatures with the effect of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Guardians Of The Cycles Of Life</title>

Shinigami are responsible for investigating and dealing with widespread phenomena that disrupt the natural flow of life. They might be tasked with solving the issue or be told to wait, observe, and provide minimal assistance. While most shinigami can retain their impartiality during such long vigils, some are far from emotionless and end up wavering toward mercy or cruelty.
</aside>
name: Shinigami

markdown:
When a home is abandoned after it has been lived in for generations, sometimes the building itself mourns the loss of its family so greatly that it attracts the pity of a kami who has yet to claim a ward. Similar to how a [tsukumogami](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=272) manifests, such a conjoining of spiritual power can result in the formation of a silsyche—a spirit of an abandoned home who craves companionship. A silsyche never aims to outright harm those who enter the homes they form from. They are merely desperate for connection and lack the understanding that most others don't take kindly to captivity. The silsyche's true form is a slender, childlike shimmering figure with a headpiece resembling the structure they initially manifested from. These representations of the silsyche's first home reflect the warmth and welcome they yearn for, potentially even displaying mementos of the house's inhabitants that have long gone. **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Silsyche](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4644) **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 346 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +15 **Str** -5 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +17 **Will** +12 **HP** 68 (rejuvenation) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Resistances** all damage 5 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), or [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510); double resistance vs. non-magical) **Inhabit Home** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The silsyche inhabits a modest building and controls it. While the building is possessed, its decor shifts to reflect the silsyche's mood, such as being filled with bright light (when the silsyche is happy) or appearing old and filthy (if they're depressed). A silsyche can't willingly leave their home; if forced out of their home, the silsyche takes 3d10 force damage (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save) at the start of each of their turns.
**Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) When a silsyche is slain, they re-form after 2d4 days within the abandoned home that serves as their domain. A silsyche can be permanently killed only if their home is completely destroyed. --- **Speed** fly 30 feet **Melee** touch +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+6 mental **Occult Innate Spells** DC 24, attack +16 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718) - **2nd** [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=1569) (×3) - **3rd** [Item Facade](/Spells.aspx?ID=1579) **Hallucinatory Home** ([Charm](/Traits.aspx?ID=247), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The silsyche creates a hospitable but illusory environment in an attempt to entice their targets into staying, including food and amenities. Creatures in the building must attempt a DC 24 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature perceives the home to be as the silsyche's illusion presents it but isn't compelled to stay.
**Failure** The creature becomes [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) with the building and refuses to leave. The creature can attempt another Will save at the end of each turn. On a success, it's no longer fascinated.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature becomes [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=80) to anyone who tries to make it leave the building. The creature can only attempt another Will save to end the effect at the end of any turn in which it attacks an ally.
Range Of Possession Most silsyches bond with a single home, typically a small building with one or two floors. More powerful silsyches can bond with larger structures, like fortresses or castles, or even large vehicles like sailing ships. Legends of entire towns being claimed by a single, incredibly powerful silsyche persist in stories to this day.
name: Silsyche
resistance_markdown: all damage 5 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), or [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510); double resistance vs. non-magical)
