**Site Bound** Yuni can stray only a short distance from where it was killed or the place it haunts. Yuni is bound to room **E2**.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 23 

**Fort** +11 

**Ref** +15 

**Will** +17 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

(rejuvenation, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83))

</row>

**Immunities**
[bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95)

**Resistances**
all damage 5 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), or [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509); double resistance vs. non-magical)

**Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) Returning her sister's charm and revealing her fate allows Yuni to move on (although she'll return as a ghost next year if the PCs don't break the Willowshore mindscape before then).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** fly 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
ghostly hand +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d8+8 void

**Frightful Moan** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) Yuni laments her fate, forcing each living creature within 30 feet to attempt a DC 2 Will save. On a failure, a creature becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2 (or frightened 3 on a critical failure). On a success, a creature is temporarily immune to Yuni's frightful moan for 1 minute.

**Tiger Attack** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) Yuni cries out in pain as a ghostly tiger appears to rip and tear at her body, only for additional ghostly tigers to appear and do the same to all creatures in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). Creatures in this area take 4d6 slashing damage (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).

</column>

markdown:
_Nethys Notes: No description has been provided for this creature. This creature, in its original printing, was presented with an AC of 2243. Given this creature is a variant [Kurobozu](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1215) and matches this creature's stat block, we have decided to give this entry a 24 AC._ **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Monk Of Kugaptee](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4676) **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 135 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +14 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +16 **Will** +14 **HP** 90 ([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) --- **Speed** 35 feet **Melee** fist +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d6+8 bludgeoning plus 1d6 void and sagebane **Brawling Critical** When a monk of Kugaptee scores a critical hit with a fist Strike or a weapon with the [monk](/Traits.aspx?ID=656) trait, the target must succeed at a DC 24 Fortitude save or be [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 until the end of the monk's next turn. **Sagebane** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) When the monk of Kugaptee damages a living creature with a fist Strike or a weapon with the [monk](/Traits.aspx?ID=656) trait, the target must succeed at a DC 24 Fortitude save or become [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 minute. Further damage dealt by the monk of Kugaptee resets the duration to 1 minute and increases the stupefied value by 1 on a failed save, to a maximum of stupefied 4. **Scarlet Apoxia** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The target can't recover from the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) condition caused by scarlet apoxia until the disease is cured; **Saving Throw** DC 24 Fortitude; **Stage 1** carrier with no ill effect (1 day); **Stage 2** [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 and fatigued (1 day); **Stage 3** drained 2 and fatigued (1 day); **Stage 4** dead (a creature that dies of scarlet apoxia reincarnates as a [nindoru](/Traits.aspx?ID=904) of equal or lower level 24 hours after death unless the body is given a proper funeral; once reincarnation occurs, the dead creature can't be restored to its original life until after the nindoru it reincarnates into is first slain) **Steal Breath** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Requirements** The monk of Kugaptee is adjacent to a living creature that's [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92), [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93), or [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95); **Effect** The monk of Kugaptee leans over and sucks the breath from the target, who must attempt a DC 22 Fortitude save. Regardless of the result, the creature is exposed to scarlet apoxia and its breath reeks of rotting wildflowers for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature can't speak for 1 minute.
**Failure** The creature becomes [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 2 and can't speak for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As failure, plus the creature falls unconscious and begins [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=2439). **Stunning Flurry** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The monk of Kugaptee makes two fist Strikes. If both hit the same creature, combine their damage for the purposes of resistances and weaknesses, and the target must succeed at a DC 22 Fortitude save or be [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1 (or stunned 3 on a critical failure); this save has the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) trait.
name: Monk Of Kugaptee

**Immunities**
[bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95)

**Resistances**
all damage 5 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), or [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509); double resistance vs. non-magical)

**Fleeting Existence** These waldgeists are only fleeting manifestations of a failed exorcism. They vanish without a trace after 1 minute has passed from their first manifestation in the mindscape.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** fly 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
lignifying root +16 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d8+5 void plus papery lignification

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 22, attack +14
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Tangle Vine](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713)
- **3rd**
[Wall of Thorns](/Spells.aspx?ID=1752)
- **Constant (3rd)**
[Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681)

**Papery Lignification**  ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The touch of a Willowshore waldgeist transforms flesh into thick rolls and compressed sheets of paper. A living creature damaged by the waldgeist's lignifying root Strike must succeed at a DC 24 Fortitude save or become [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 (or slowed 2 on a critical failure). Further failed saves against papery lignification increases the slowed condition. Once a creature's actions are reduced to 0 by papery lignification, that creature becomes [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87), except they're transformed into a statue of compressed paper (much like paper-mache) instead of turned to stone. Fortunately, this condition isn't permanent, and once the Willowshore waldgeists vanish a minute after they've manifested, creatures that have been turned to paper revert to living flesh, but are at 0 Hit Points and [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) (but not dying) from the trauma of the harrowing transformation.

**Storm of Paper** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The Willowshore waldgeist causes thousands of sheets of paper to fly out of the ruins of Cloud Paper House to slice and cut flesh. All creatures in a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 90 feet of the Willowshore waldgeist take 8d6 slashing damage (DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature that critically fails this save becomes partially buried in mounds of paper sheets and is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round.

</column>

This curse has an unlimited duration.

**Cantrips (4th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=1673), [Vitality Lash](/Spells.aspx?ID=1744) - **1st** [Bane](/Spells.aspx?ID=1447), [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [Sanctuary](/Spells.aspx?ID=1660) - **2nd** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Peaceful Rest](/Spells.aspx?ID=1625), [Spiritual Armament](/Spells.aspx?ID=1687) - **3rd** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Holy Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1557) - **4th** [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=1499), [Talking Corpse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712) **Ritual** - **2nd** [Consecrate](/Rituals.aspx?ID=115) **Purifying Flames** ([[divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) The exorcist attempts to Strike a creature with their torch, attacking with great precision and causing the torch's fires to flare up. This Strike gains a +2 status bonus to its attack roll and inflicts an additional 2d6 fire damage on a hit instead of just 1d6 fire damage. An undead, spirit, or transmigrated creature hit by this attack must also attempt a DC 26 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected by this additional effect, and some of the fire flares back onto the exorcist, inflicting 2d6 fire damage to them (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).
**Success** The creature is unaffected by this additional effect but still takes normal torch damage.
**Failure** The purifying flames cause the creature to become [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 until the end of its next turn.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but clumsy 2 and stupefied 2 for 1 minute. **Torch Wielder** The exorcist treats a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2760) as a light mace that deals an additional 1d6 fire damage on a hit.
name: Exorcist

**Fearful of Ghosts** All this talk about haunted ruins has the mercenary enforcers at Karahai on edge. At the start of any combat against the PCs while Karahai's Terror Point threshold is at least 2, a mercenary enforcer must succeed at a DC 20 Will save or become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 (or frightened 2 on a critical failure). Once Karahai's Terror Point threshold is 7 or higher, a mercenary enforcer also becomes [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) whenever they become frightened.

**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=75)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** longsword +17 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d8+10 slashing **Ranged** composite longbow +15 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=730)), **Damage** 1d8+8 piercing **Sneak Attack** A mercenary enforcer deals 1d6 extra precision damage to creatures who are [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58).

**Cantrips (5th)** [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498), [Forbidding Ward](/Spells.aspx?ID=1535), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689), [Vitality Lash](/Spells.aspx?ID=1744) - **1st** [Bless](/Spells.aspx?ID=1451), [Cleanse Cuisine](/Spells.aspx?ID=1468), [Sanctuary](/Spells.aspx?ID=1660) (3 slots) - **2nd** [Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445), [Clear Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1469), [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) (3 slots) - **3rd** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Holy Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1557) (3 slots) - **4th** [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=1499), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Planar Tether](/Spells.aspx?ID=1636) (3 slots) - **5th** [Divine Immolation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1496), [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665) (2 slots) **Ritual** - **2nd** [Consecrate](/Rituals.aspx?ID=115) **Spiritual Arrows** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The shrine maiden nocks an arrow and whispers a soft chant under her breath before unleashing a torrent of holy power in the form of dozens of ghostly glowing arrows. Creatures in a 60-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) take 10d6 force damage (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). On a critical failure, the creature is also [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) ([Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) DC 28) for 1 minute. **Twang** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=765)) The shrine maiden strums her bowstring, creating a pulse of energy that lashes out at a target within 20 feet. That creature must attempt a DC 28 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** No effect.
**Success** The target is pushed 5 feet back from the shrine maiden.
**Failure** The target is pushed 10 feet back from the shrine maiden and falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
**Critical Failure** The target is flung 15 feet away from the shrine maiden, falls prone, and is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round.
name: Shrine Maiden

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="tiny">

**[Recall Knowledge - Fungus](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 31

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 9">[Kugaptee's Drakauthix](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4685)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Huge" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=600" />
<trait label="Fungus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=614" />
<trait label="Nindoru" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=904" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 218

**Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), sporesight ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407)) 60 feet

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +15, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +7

**Dex** +2

**Con** +6

**Int** +0

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Sporesight** Kugaptee's drakauthix exude clouds of spores that they use to see. This is an imprecise sense that functions only in areas without strong wind currents. Sporesight does not function underwater.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 25 

**Fort** +21 

**Ref** +13 

**Will** +15 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 190

</row>

**Resistances**
[void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 10

**Weaknesses**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10, [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) 10, slashing 10

**Obscuring Spores** Whenever the drakauthix takes at least 10 slashing damage, it releases a cloud of brown spores in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) that persists for 1 minute (or until dispersed by a strong wind). All creatures within the spores become concealed, and all creatures outside the spores become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) to creatures within it. Within this cloud of spores, the drakauthix's sporesight becomes a precise sense. The spore cloud does not move with the drakauthix nor is it displaced by its movement.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, fly 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tentacle +20 ([reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 3d8+6 piercing plus 1d6 mental and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66)

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 27, attack +24
- **Cantrips (5th)**
[Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482)
- **3rd**
[Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524)
- **5th**
[Vision of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742)
- **6th**
[Spirit Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1685)

**Reel In**   The drakauthix pulls all creatures it has [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) 15 feet closer to itself.

**Spore Tendrils** <actions string="Single Action" />  The tendrils that cover the drakauthix's body reach out and infest adjacent creatures, dealing 4d6 poison damage and 1d6 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) (DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save; a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) by the drakauthix takes a –4 circumstance penalty to this save).

**Whirlwind of Hooks** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The drakauthix whirls, whipping creatures around it. It makes a tentacle Strike against every creature within reach. These attacks count toward the drakauthix's multiple attack penalty, but the multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after the drakauthix makes all its attacks.

</column>
name: Kugaptee's Drakauthix

<title level="1" pfs="">[False Governor](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4686)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

_Nethys Notes: No description has been provided for this creature. The Soul Shred ability mentions "As [argyrzei](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4635), but DC 36." Since there is no DC within the ability, we have decided to leave it out in the entry._

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 35

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/False_Governor.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 12">[False Governor](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4686)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=600" />
<trait label="Nindoru" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=904" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 221

**Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165)

**Skills**
[Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +25, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +23, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +22, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +24

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +7

**Dex** +6

**Con** +4

**Int** +4

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +7
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 32 

**Fort** +20 

**Ref** +24 

**Will** +22 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 220

</row>

**Resistances**
[void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 15

**Weaknesses**
[silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) 15

**Soul Shred** Creatures killed by the False Governor are difficult to bring back to life or animate as undead. The False Governor automatically attempts to counteract any attempt to resurrect or animate any creature that they've killed (counteract modifier +23, counteract rank 7). If the False Governor who slew the creature is slain, the creature can be brought back from death or become undead normally.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 3d4+14 slashing plus 1d6 mental

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 32, attack +24
- **Cantrips (6th)**
[Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598)
- **2nd**
[Item Facade](/Spells.aspx?ID=1579)
- **4th**
[Honeyed Words](/Spells.aspx?ID=1558), [Talking Corpse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712)
- **5th**
[Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=1632), [Wave of Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757)
- **6th**
[Illusory Creature](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567), [Phantasmal Calamity](/Spells.aspx?ID=1630)

**Manifest Body**  ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The false governor has manifested a hollow humanoid body around their slender skeletal frame that looks identical to that of Governor Heh Shan-Bao. While wearing this manifested body, the false governor loses their claw Strike, and their voice sounds like that of Heh Shan-Bao's. The false governor's manifested body decays swiftly if they don't take 10 minutes once per day to maintain the manifestation of the false flesh. The body has 10 Hit Points and the same defenses as the false governor. When the body is destroyed, the false governor emerges unharmed, and the fleshy shell left behind scatters into a cloud of red butterflies that swiftly fade into red mist. This emergence is so grisly and unsettling that all creatures within 30 feet who witness it must attempt a DC 29 Fortitude save or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 2 (sickened 3 on a critical failure); this has the [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) and [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) traits.

**Stunning Despair** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [incapacitation mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The false governor unleashes several overwhelming sobs of despair. Creatures within 30 feet must attempt a DC 29 Will save to avoid becoming [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1 (stunned 3 on a critical failure). The false governor can't use Stunning Despair again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>
name: False Governor

markdown:
_Nethys Note: No description has been provided for this creature._ **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Hong Meigui](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4687) **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 228 **Perception** +20; breathsense (precise) 60 feet, [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +19, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +21, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +20, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +22, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +20 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +4 **Breathsense** Hong Meigui can't perceive living creatures beyond 5 feet except with breathsense; a living creature that holds its breath is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) to her as long as it holds its breath. **Items** _[+2 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [flaming ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2838) [shock ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2847) [urumi](/Weapons.aspx?ID=306)_, key to chest --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +15 **Will** +22 **HP** 128 ([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 10, one more breath, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696), [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [duskwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2918)) **Bitter Epiphany** When she sees an altar with at least 1 Bulk of food offerings to ancestor spirits, she must attempt a DC 25 Will save with the following results; she is then temporarily immune to this vulnerability for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** She is unaffected.
**Success** She is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 round.
**Failure** She is stupefied 2 for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** She is stupefied 2 and [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 minute.
**Jiang-Shi Vulnerabilities** Hong Meigui has [vampiric revulsion](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=286) against handheld mirrors or the sound of ringing handbells.
**One More Breath** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) Hong Meigui isn't destroyed at 0 HP; instead, she falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) and awakens in 1 minute with 1 Hit Point. Scattering at least 1 Bulk of glutinous rice or hen eggs on her while she's unconscious in this way destroys her permanently, as will any attack from a weapon made of [duskwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2918) or [peachwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3470).
**Warped Fulu** The corrupted fulu attached to Hong Meigui's brow makes her immune to spells cast from a magic item without expending a spell slot, such as from a scroll or wand. A creature can [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2410) the warped fulu, rolling against her Perception DC of 30. This ends her immunity to these effects. If a creature then destroys the warped fulu with an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=3259) action, Hong Meigui also loses her fast healing. She can create a replacement warped fulu by spending 1 uninterrupted hour inscribing a strip of paper with a writing instrument. --- **Speed** 20 feet, rigor mortis **Melee** _urumi_ +23 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)), **Damage** 2d6+11 slashing plus 1d6 electricity and 1d6 fire **Melee** claw +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d10+11 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 29, attack +21 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718) - **3rd** [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622), [Vampiric Feast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1736) - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463), [Vision of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742) - **5th** [Hallucination](/Spells.aspx?ID=1551), [Subconscious Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1692) **Rituals** - **2nd** [Inveigle](/Rituals.aspx?ID=150) **Drain Qi** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Requirements** A [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), or willing creature is within Hong Meigui's reach; **Effect** Hong Meigui drains the victim's life energy through their breath. This requires an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check against the victim's Fortitude DC if the victim is grabbed and is automatic for any of the other conditions. The victim is [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 and Hong Meigui regains 12 HP, gaining any excess as temporary HP. Draining qi from a creature that's already drained doesn't restore any HP but increases the victim's drained condition value by 1, killing them if they reach drained 5. If the victim has the ability to cast qi spells, they can spend 1 [Focus](/Rules.aspx?ID=285) Point to avoid becoming drained by the current attack.
This drained condition value decreases by 1 every week. Once per day, if the victim restricts their diet to glutinous rice for the day and spends 10 minutes engaging in vigorous physical activity by succeeding at a DC 25 Athletics check, they reduce their drained condition by 1. **Rigor Mortis** Hong Meigui's hopping movement lets her [ignore difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2367) and effects that would render her [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). She can't take the [Drop Prone](/Actions.aspx?ID=2295) action, and when she [Leaps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298), she doesn't trigger reactions normally triggered by movement.
name: Hong Meigui

_Nethys Note: No description has been provided for this creature._ **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Quintessivore](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4688) **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 247 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +19, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +19, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +20 **Str** +3 **Dex** +6 **Con** +6 **Int** +7 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +20 **Will** +21 **HP** 180 **Instant Suspension** **Trigger** The quintessivore reduces a creature within 15 feet to 0 HP; **Effect** The quintessivore uses Suspend Soul, targeting the creature. --- **Speed** 40 feet, climb 20 feet **Melee** blade-leg +22 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 1d10+9 slashing plus 1d10 void and tattered soul **Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 29, attack +21 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Frostbite](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715) - **1st** [Enfeeble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1513), [Gentle Landing](/Spells.aspx?ID=1542), [Tailwind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1711) - **2nd** [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663), [Stupefy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1691), [Translate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1723) - **3rd** [Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1506), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677), [Vampiric Feast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1736) - **4th** [Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1466), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534), [Vision of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742) - **5th** [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=1548), [Magic Passage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1591), [Slither](/Spells.aspx?ID=1676) **Feed on Quintessence** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=595), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) Over the course of 1 hour, the quintessivore removes and deconstructs the soul of a creature. The creature targeted by Feed On Quintessence must either be captive or have been dead for no more than 2 hours before the start of the process. At the end of the hour, the quintessivore consumes the quintessence of the creature's soul. For the next month, it gains a +1 status bonus to its spell DC and spell attack roll, and it adds 6th-level [_never mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1614) and [_vampiric exsanguination_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1735) to its arcane prepared spells.
Restoring a creature to life whose soul has been fed upon in this way is difficult, requiring a DC 31 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) or [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) check as part of the effort—failure indicates the attempt to restore life fails and any resources expended in the attempt are lost. Slaying the quintessivore in question ends this complication. **Suspend Soul** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) The quintessivore suspends the life processes of a [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) creature within 15 feet of it. The creature can't decrease or increase its HP or dying value for 10 minutes. The creature can attempt a DC 29 Will save to avoid this effect. If the creature receives magical healing, it can attempt a new save, ending the effect and being healed normally on a success. The effect ends if the quintessivore uses Suspend Soul again. **Tattered Soul** A creature hit by the quintessivore's bladeleg Strike must succeed at a DC 29 Fortitude save or become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (drained 2 on a critical failure). If the creature is already drained, it's immune to this effect.
name: Quintessivore

markdown:
_Nethys Note: No description has been provided for this creature._ **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 38 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 [Jiang-Shi](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4689) **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 251 **Perception** +22; breathsense (precise) 60 feet, [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +20, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +22 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +3 **Breathsense** A Jiang-Shi can't perceive living creatures beyond 5 feet except with breathsense; a living creature that holds its breath is invisible to them as long as it holds its breath. **Items** _[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [mace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=362)_ --- **AC** 31 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +18 **Will** +24 **HP** 130 ([fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 10, one more breath, [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696), [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [duskwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2918)) **Bitter Epiphany** When she sees an altar with at least 1 Bulk of food offerings to ancestor spirits, she must attempt a DC 25 Will save with the following results; she is then temporarily immune to this vulnerability for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** She is unaffected.
**Success** She is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 round.
**Failure** She is stupefied 2 for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** She is stupefied 2 and [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 minute.
**Jiang-Shi Vulnerabilities** Jiang-Shi has [vampiric revulsion](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=480) against handheld mirrors or the sound of ringing handbells.
**One More Breath** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) A Jiang-Shi isn't destroyed at 0 HP; instead, they fall [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) and awakens in 1 minute with 1 Hit Point. Scattering at least 1 Bulk of glutinous rice or hen eggs on her while she's unconscious in this way destroys her permanently, as will any attack from a weapon made of [duskwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2918) or [peachwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3470).
**Warped Fulu** The corrupted fulu attached to a Jiang-Shi's brow makes her immune to spells cast from a magic item without expending a spell slot, such as from a scroll or wand. A creature can [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2410) the warped fulu, rolling against their Perception DC of 30. This ends their immunity to these effects. If a creature then destroys the warped fulu with an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=3259) action, Jiang-Shi also loses their fast healing. They can create a replacement warped fulu by spending 1 uninterrupted hour inscribing a strip of paper with a writing instrument. --- **Speed** 15 feet, rigor mortis **Melee** _mace_ +23 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)), **Damage** 2d6+11 bludgeoning **Melee** claw +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d10+11 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Drain Qi** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Requirements** A [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), or willing creature is within the Jiang-Shi's reach; **Effect** The Jiang-Shi drains the victim's life energy through their breath. This requires an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check against the victim's Fortitude DC if the victim is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) and is automatic for any of the other conditions. The victim is [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 and the Jiang-Shi regains 13 HP, gaining any excess as [temporary HP](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321). Draining qi from a creature that's already drained doesn't restore any HP but increases the victim's drained condition value by 1, killing them if they reach drained 5. If the victim has the ability to cast qi spells, they can spend 1 [Focus](/Rules.aspx?ID=285) Point to avoid becoming drained by the current attack.
This drained condition value decreases by 1 every week. Once per day, if the victim restricts their diet to glutinous rice for the day and spends 10 minutes engaging in vigorous physical activity by succeeding at a DC 25 Athletics check, they reduce their drained condition by 1. **Rigor Mortis** A Jiang-Shi's hopping movement lets her [ignore difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2367) and effects that would render her [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). She can't take the [Drop Prone](/Actions.aspx?ID=2295) action, and when she [Leaps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298), she doesn't trigger reactions normally triggered by movement.
name: Jiang-Shi

_Nethys Note: No description has been provided for this creature._ **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 33 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 [Occult Living Rune](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4690) **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 254 **Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** truescript **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +24, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +25 **Str** +1 **Dex** +6 **Con** +1 **Int** +8 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +5 **Truescript** A living rune can't speak, but it can sculpt its form into complicated scripts and pictographs that can be understood by any creature with the ability to read any written language. A living rune can also understand any written or spoken language. --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +23 **Will** +27 **HP** 245 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [diseased](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [unconsciousness](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Backdrop** When a creature attempts to [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) a living rune and critically misses, the attacker hits the surface behind the living rune. A weapon capable of inflicting piercing or slashing damage becomes stuck in the wood of the floor, door, or wall it strikes in this case. A stuck weapon can be yanked free with an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action.
**Surface-Bound** A living rune can move only along flat surfaces like walls, doors, and floors. If the surface it's on is destroyed, the living rune takes 7d6 damage and is shunted to the nearest flat surface. If there is no flat surface within 5 feet, the living rune is destroyed. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Ranged** wrathful blast +25 ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d12 mental **Occult Innate Spells** DC 35, attack +27 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) - **5th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1471), [Invoke Spirits](/Spells.aspx?ID=1578), [Slither](/Spells.aspx?ID=1676), [Synaptic Pulse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1710) - **6th** [Phantasmal Calamity](/Spells.aspx?ID=1630), [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=1632), [Spirit Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1685), [Wave of Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757) - **7th** [Vision of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742), [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1754) - **Constant (6th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727)
name: Occult Living Rune

_Nethys Note: No description has been provided for this creature._ **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Corpse Sugi](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4692) **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 263 **Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +26, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +20 **Str** +7 **Dex** +3 **Con** +5 **Int** -2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 **Sugi Tree** When it isn't in danger, the corpse sugi spends 1 minute rooting to the earth, becoming planted in place. While the corpse sugi is planted and immobile, creatures must actively [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) and succeed at a DC 38 Perception Check to detect the Corpse Sugi's true nature. --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +20 **Will** +20 **HP** 242 ([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Resistances** bludgeoning 10, piercing 10 **Weaknesses** axe vulnerability, fire 10 **Axe Vulnerability** The corpse sugi takes 10 additional damage from axes.
**Thoughtblight** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) 30 feet. The mind of a non-[nindoru](/Traits.aspx?ID=739) sapient living creature that enters or starts their turn in the corpse sugi's aura becomes infested with invasive thoughts of cruelty and anguish, and must succeed at a DC 29 Will save or become [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 (or stupefied 2 on a critical failure). This condition persists for 1 minute after the creature leaves the aura. A creature that succeeds on the saving throw is temporarily immune to thoughtblight for 1 minute. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** branch +26 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+10 bludgeoning plus 1d6 mental **Melee** root +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)), **Damage** 3d12+10 bludgeoning plus 1d6 mental and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Ranged** rotten fruit +22 ([range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699)), **Damage** 3d4+7 bludgeoning plus 3d6 poison splash damage **Take Root** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per round; **Requirements** The corpse sugi has a creature grabbed or restrained; **Effect** The corpse sugi buries its roots into the creature, dealing 1d6+13 piercing damage and draining the target's life force (DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). On a failure, the creature is [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (or increases the value by 1 if already drained), and the corpse sugi regains 10 HP. If this would make a creature drained 5, the creature dies.
name: Corpse Sugi

markdown:
The goblin artists of Stumpfield love a good song, as most goblins do. While they aren't primarily warriors, these goblins have taken up their dogslicers to fight for the liberation of Isger. Their main advantage as a troop asset in skirmish encounters, though, is their ability to bolster their allies and flummox their enemies with their chants. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Stumpfield War Chanter Choir](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4717) **Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 218 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Goblin](/Languages.aspx?ID=160) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +17, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +15, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +13 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +16 **Will** +14 **HP** 120 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 10, splash damage 10 **In Our Wake, Hell Will Break!** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 30 feet. The war chanters constantly sing and cheer to spur their Hellbreaker allies. Each allied creature in the aura gains a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effects.
[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 25, attack +17 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Eat Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=1352), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718) - **1st** [Bless](/Spells.aspx?ID=1451), [Signal Skyrocket](/Spells.aspx?ID=1362), [Soothe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1678) (3 slots) - **2nd** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Laughing Fit](/Spells.aspx?ID=1583), [Shatter](/Spells.aspx?ID=1670) (3 slots) - **3rd** [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553), [Shatter](/Spells.aspx?ID=1670), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677) (3 slots) - **4th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1471), [Whispers of the Void](/Spells.aspx?ID=2046) (2 slots) **Goblins Slice and Goblins Dice!** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The war chanters swing their dogslicers, attacking each enemy in a 5-foot emanation, with a DC 22 basic Reflex save. The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.
1d6+2 slashing damage
2d6+8 slashing damage
3d6+11 slashing damage **Hark! The Goblin Chanters Sing!** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The war chanters break out into a familiar song with unfamiliar rhyme. A number of enemies equal to the troop's current number of segments within 30 feet must attempt a DC 25 Will save. Regardless of the outcome, a target becomes temporarily immune to this ability for 1 minute. On a failure, the target is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 round as they get distracted by the war chanters' rhymes (stupefied 2 on a critical failure).
name: Stumpfield War Chanter Choir

Stumpfield's artists enjoy the chaos of creation, which makes them well suited for the parallel chaos of destruction. Isn't sabotage just another form of art? Stumpfield certainly thinks so, and its denizens are happy to use their creative skills to waylay enemies and ambush parties. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Stumpfield War Saboteurs](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4718) **Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 219 **Perception** +13 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Goblin](/Languages.aspx?ID=160) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +12, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +12, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +14, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +14 **Str** +4 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +2 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +15 **Will** +12 **HP** 99 **Weaknesses** area damage 5, splash damage 5 [Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86)
**Duck, Duck, Loose!** **Trigger** The saboteurs are targeted by a Strike or an effect that requires a Reflex save; **Effect** The saboteurs scramble for the nearest cover. They become [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) but gain [standard cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) until the beginning of their next turn. --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Rush and Steal** Quickly moving in with grasping hands, the Stumpfield war saboteurs take what they can. The saboteurs Stride up to twice their Speed. During this movement, the saboteurs Interact to pick up an unattended object no larger than 2 Bulk or attempt to Steal an item from a creature they're adjacent to; the goblins can pick up or Steal as many objects as they have remaining segments in any combination. **Slapstick Traps** **Frequency** once per minute; **Requirements** The saboteurs' last action was to successfully [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404); **Effect** The saboteurs laid down falling obstacles as they move unseen. The saboteurs [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) twice. Until the beginning of the saboteurs' next turn, any non-ally creature that moves into a square that the saboteurs snuck through takes 5d8 bludgeoning damage (DC 24 basic Reflex save). On a critical failure, the creature also falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). Once this trap has been triggered, the rest of the squares the saboteurs snuck through become difficult terrain until a creature spends 1 hour clearing it. **Slice and Dice!** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The saboteurs engage in a coordinated melee attack against each enemy in a 5-foot emanation, with a DC 21 basic Reflex save. The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.
1d8 slashing damage
2d8+5 slashing damage
2d8+9 slashing damage
name: Stumpfield War Saboteurs

Swiftrun is a small settlement dedicated to Erastil, barely large enough to be considered a village. Its priests are devoted to community and peace, nature and nurture. And yet, sometimes the time comes when one must take up arms to protect their community, when words and gentleness aren't enough. The Erastilian clergy is known more for their farming techniques than their magic, but their rural ways shouldn't fool anyone into thinking they can't defend themselves. Each priest is equipped with the divine power of Old Deadeye as well as sharpshooting skills conducive to Erastil's favored weapon, the longbow. These priests are prepared to defend their homes with their skills when they must, though they are never eager to shed blood and would rather spend their days tending their crops and community. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Swiftrun Clergy](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4719) **Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 220 **Perception** +16 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155) **Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +12, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +14, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +13, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +11, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +12 **Str** +2 **Dex** +2 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +14 **Will** +16 **HP** 96 **Weaknesses** area damage 5, splash damage 5 [Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 24, attack +16 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671), [Vitality Lash](/Spells.aspx?ID=1744) - **1st** [Infuse Vitality](/Spells.aspx?ID=1574), [Protection](/Spells.aspx?ID=1641), [Sure Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709) - **2nd** [Cleanse Affliction](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467) (×2), [Clear Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1469) - **3rd** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Holy Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1557), [Wall of Thorns](/Spells.aspx?ID=1752) **Longbow Volley** The Swiftrun clergy draw their longbows, then launch a ranged attack in the form of a volley. This volley is a 10-foot burst within 60 feet that deals 3d6 piercing damage with a DC 21 basic Reflex save. When the regiment is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Swift Daggers** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** Swiftrun's clergy swiftly strike with their daggers against each enemy in a 5-foot emanation, with a DC 21 basic Reflex save. The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.
1d4+1 piercing damage
[two-actions] 2d4+7 piercing damage
[three-actions] 2d4+10 piercing damage **Troop Spellcasting** When the Swiftrun clergy Casts a Spell, their constituent members combine their efforts into casting a more powerful version of the spell than any one member could achieve alone. When Casting a Spell that has an area of a burst, cone, or line and doesn't have a duration, increase the area of that spell. Add 5 feet to the radius of a burst that normally has a radius of at least 10 feet (a burst with a smaller radius is not affected). Add 5 feet to the length of a cone or line that is normally 15 feet long or smaller, and add 10 feet to the length of a larger cone or line.
name: Swiftrun Clergy

The gnomes of Umok have a reticence to fire after the pyrrhic wildfire that razed the Chitterwood during the Goblinblood Wars. To help protect their home from unwanted arson, many gnomes of Umok have learned to communicate with animals, whose instincts can quickly sniff out unauthorized smoke or flame. The two groups work together to keep the town safe, with the animals serving as an early-warning system when threats approach. Several Umok beastspeakers have joined the war effort, harnessing their primal magic and their connection with animals against the armies of Cheliax. Their powerful magics are used to summon beasts of the wild to defend Isger from forces that threaten the peace of their home, pitting primal power against fiends. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Umok Beastspeaker Circle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4720) **Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 221 **Perception** +16; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Gnomish](/Languages.aspx?ID=159) **Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +15, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +17, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +15, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +17 **Str** +1 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +2 **Animal Empathy** The beastspeaker circle can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the Diplomacy skill with animals. --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +12 **Will** +18 **HP** 120 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 10, splash damage 10 [Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=86) --- **Speed** 25 feet; [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Primal Prepared Spells** DC 25 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Gouging Claw](/Spells.aspx?ID=1546), [Vitality Lash](/Spells.aspx?ID=1744) - **1st** [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1550), [Pummeling Rubble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1642), [Thunderstrike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1721) - **2nd** [Animal Messenger](/Spells.aspx?ID=1441), [Pummeling Rubble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1642), [Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=1680) - **3rd** [Mad Monkeys](/Spells.aspx?ID=1590), [Wall of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1753) - **4th** [Hydraulic Torrent](/Spells.aspx?ID=1562), [Weapon Storm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1758) **Muster Animals** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The beastspeakers summon a group of wild animals to attack all creatures in a 10-foot burst within 60 feet. The animals deal 3d8 slashing damage (DC 22 basic Reflex save). When the beastspeaker circle is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Staff Assault** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The beastspeakers grip their staves with two hands and attack all enemies in a 5-foot emanation, with a DC 22 basic Reflex save. The amount of damage depends on the number of actions used.
1d8+1 bludgeoning damage
[two-actions] 2d8+6 bludgeoning damage
[three-actions] 2d8+10 bludgeoning damage **Troop Spellcasting** When the beastspeaker circle Casts a Spell, the individual members combine their efforts into casting a more powerful version than any one member could achieve alone. When Casting a Spell that has an area of a burst, cone, or line and doesn't have a duration, increase the area of that spell. Add 5 feet to the radius of a burst that normally has a radius of at least 10 feet (a burst with a smaller radius is not affected). Add 5 feet to the length of a cone or line that is normally 15 feet long or smaller, and add 10 feet to the length of a larger cone or line.

markdown:
Adiutors, while nicknamed auxiliary devils, are not truly devils, but mortals invested with infernal power. These mortals gain a taste of Hell's might as rewards for their loyalty to the cause of tyranny and oppression. Hungry for power, an adiutor ruthlessly enforces Hell's will at any cost in hopes of fulfilling their fiendish ambitions. They're most commonly found in Cheliax and its territories due to the nation's relationship with the forces of Hell, serving as spies and informants for both the Thrunes and powerful devils invested in meddling with mortals. While some adiutors climb the ranks of mortal society to become Hellknights and other supporters of tyranny, most have higher ambitions and desire to become devils themselves. To this end, they seek to serve more powerful devils and make deals that will benefit them so that they can ensure their transformation. There's nothing an adiutor despises more than their own mortality—a sign of weakness, when beings like devils walk the Universe in their perfect Hellish vision. Although they're most likely to be found in places or near people with existing links to Hell, adiutors can be found anywhere, quietly plotting to gain power and status so they can become the lieutenants of Hell they desire to be. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 20 **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Adiutor](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4721) **Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 222 **Perception** +13; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +14, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +15, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +12 **Str** +2 **Dex** +5 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 **Items** [Bayonet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=186) (attached to flintlock musket), [Flintlock Musket](/Weapons.aspx?ID=521), uniform --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +14 **Will** +10 **HP** 72 **Resistances** fire 8 **Weaknesses** holy 4 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** bayonet +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 1d4+5 piercing **Ranged** flintlock musket +14 ([Concussive](/Traits.aspx?ID=846), [fatal d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=597), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 70 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 1d6+3 piercing plus 2d6 fire **Divine Innate Spells** DC 22, attack +14 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671) - **1st** [Bane](/Spells.aspx?ID=1447) (×3) - **2nd** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493) (×2), [Revealing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653) - **3rd** [Alarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1439), [Crisis of Faith](/Spells.aspx?ID=1478) **Avernian Armskote** An adiutor reloads by magically conjuring hellfire rounds crafted in the Fools' Citadels of Avernus. Their Strikes with their flintlock muskets are magical and deal an additional 2d6 fire damage. **Fiendish Reload** When the adiutor reloads their musket, they can cast shield as part of the same action. **Punish the Wayward** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=606), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The adiutor makes a melee or ranged Strike. If this Strike succeeds, the targeted creature takes the normal damage and must additionally attempt a DC 22 Will save. **Critical Success** The creature suffers no additional effects.
**Success** The creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The creature is frightened 1. While it can see the adiutor, it can’t reduce its frightened condition.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature is also [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the adiutor while frightened.
Wannabe Devils The process by which the adiutors are created is supposedly one engineered by the iconoclastic archfiend Barbatos, who’s rumored to not be of true devilish heritage. While none would dare do so in front of the Bearded Lord, true devils take a joy in lording over the adiutors, who are neither of Hell’s elect nor its elite, and in sending them on dangerous tasks to earn some unwinnable respect.
name: Adiutor

markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="">[Aurocan](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4722)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

One can predict what foes are on the battlefield by how they sound. Foot soldiers clang weapons on shields as they march. Cavalry is marked by a rapid drumbeat of hooves. Armies with great monsters under their command don't try to silence their hair-raising screeches.

 No sound prompts as much caution as the steady clicking of gears and hiss of billowing steam, for the only thing that accompanies that chorus is an aurocan: a mechanical creature created in the shape of a massive six-legged bull prone to exploding when destroyed. The aurocan's greatest strength is how its head can detach from its body to rain down scalding steam from above.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 26

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Aurocan.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 8">[Aurocan](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4722)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Huge" />
<trait label="Clockwork" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=874" />
<trait label="Construct" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=563" />
<trait label="Mindless" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=652" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 223

**Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +3

**Con** +4

**Int** -5

**Wis** +1

**Cha** -4
</row>

**[Wind-Up](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=575)** 24 hours, DC 24, standby

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 25 

**Fort** +19 

**Ref** +15 

**Will** +13 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 135

</row>

**Immunities**
bleed, death effects, disease, doomed, drained, fatigued, fire, healing, mental, nonlethal attacks, paralyzed, poison, sickened, spirit, unconscious, vitality, void

**Resistances**
physical 5 (except adamantine or orichalcum)

**Weaknesses**
electricity 10, orichalcum 10

**Explosive End** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) **Trigger** The aurocan drops to 0 HP; **Effect** The aurocan detonates. Every creature in a 30-foot emanation takes 3d12 bludgeoning damage and 3d8 fire damage (DC 23 basic Reflex save). On a critical failure, a creature is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hoof +18 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d8+10 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
horn +18 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d10+10 piercing

**Blistering Touch** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) The aurocan funnels some of the steam powering it into its body, heating up its metal frame. For the next minute, its melee Strikes deal an additional 1d6 fire damage.

**Head of Steam** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)) **Requirements** The aurocan has used Blistering Touch within the past 2 rounds, and its head is attached; **Effect** The aurocan expels a cloud of steam as its head detaches. The head occupies a 5-foot square and has a fly Speed of 15 feet. As part of this action, and every time the aurocan Sustains it on a subsequent round, the head can Fly up to its Speed, blasting steam directly downward as it moves. Each creature whose space it passes no more than 5 feet above during its flight takes 4d6 fire damage (DC 26 basic Reflex save). On a failed save, a creature is also [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) by the steam for 1 round (or [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) on a critical failure). While its head is detached, the aurocan can't make horn Strikes, but it can perform all other actions normally. The head has an AC of 27, Hardness 10, and the same saving throws, immunities, weaknesses, and resistances as the aurocan. If the head takes 20 damage in a single attack, it falls to the ground broken, and the aurocan is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 round. If the head reenters the aurocan's space, it reattaches.

**[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=81)  Large or smaller, hoof, DC 26** <actions string="Three Actions" />  

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Mode Of Transport</title>

The aurocan’s chest hull is large enough to carry up to five Medium or smaller creatures. The creatures benefit from total cover and can leave the aurocan with an Interact action to open a hatch and slide out. Some aurocans are designed specifically as protective tanks for ranged soldiers like snipers and spellcasters, with slots built into their sides for weapons and spells to shoot out of.
</aside>
name: Aurocan

markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="">[Insidiator (Entrapment Devil)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4723)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These devils from the first layer of Hell serve their betters by ensnaring those who are easily tempted and weaving plots that lead to the fall of mortal souls. Insidiators are commonly recruited to set up sting operations that entice mortals into committing terrible sins or lay bare the machinations of fiendish rivals. They delight in seeing their victims fall prey to their well- crafted schemes and might spend years on the perfect plan, though the most efficient are more likely to rise through Hell's ranks.

 An insidiator's talent for subtlety extends to their physical form. Their bat-like wings appear gossamer but, like the spider webs they resemble, are excellent for catching prey, sticking to enemies and trapping them in melee. When the mood strikes or circumstances warrant, entrapment devils can alter the color of their skin and wings to avoid detection.

 Insidiators generally seek to avoid physical combat, preferring to toy with their targets in games of subterfuge and mental manipulation. Their specialty is leading a target down a convoluted path that ends with the target incriminating or damning themselves, forcing them to agree to the insidiator's demands. Should a would-be victim see through the insidiator's schemes, the devil concedes temporary defeat and leaves to begin plotting anew. When all else fails and physical tactics become necessary, entrapment devils are capable fighters who use their spiderlike webs and camouflage abilities to their advantage, employing sneak tactics to throw their opponents off-guard.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Insidiator.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Insidiator](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4723)</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 224

**Perception** +10; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117)

**Skills**
[Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +7, [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +6, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +6, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +6

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +2

**Con** +1

**Int** +3

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +4
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 15 

**Fort** +4 

**Ref** +7 

**Will** +9 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 20

</row>

**Immunities**
fire

**Resistances**
physical 3 (except silver), poison 3

**Weaknesses**
holy 3

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, fly 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 1d4+3 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
wing +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)),
**Damage** 1d6+3 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
web +9 ([range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** web trap

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 17
- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482)
- **1st**
[Bane](/Spells.aspx?ID=1447) (at will), [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=1740)

**Chameleon Skin** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629)) The insidiator changes the coloration of their skin to match their surrounding environment. Doing so allows them to Hide even if they don't have cover. When the insidiator moves to a different environment, they must use this action again or lose its effects.

**Change Shape** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The insidiator can take on the appearance of any Medium humanoid. This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage bonuses with their Strikes but might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning).

**Hidden Movement**   If the insidiator starts their turn undetected or hidden to a creature, that creature is off-guard against the insidiator's attacks until the end of the turn.

**Web Trap**   A creature hit by the insidiator's web Strike is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) and stuck to the nearest surface until it Escapes (DC 17).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Spidery Inclinations</title>

The insidiator often takes after specific spiders, even bearing webbed markings on their face, which shows in their tactics. Those inspired by jumping spiders are quick to hunt down their prey, attacking relentlessly with every tool they have, while those closer to the patient trapdoor spiders integrate deeply into the society they infiltrate.
</aside>

name: Insidiator

Wherever learning and instruction is called for within the burning shadows of Hell, from the bureaus of Dis and coffers of Erebus to the war colleges of Malebolge and Caina's courtrooms, docent devils ply their unassuming and perspicacious trade. The instillo, so named for their creativity and talent in instilling a respect for Hell's ways and legitimacy to captive audiences, are valued for their ability to transform recently conquered lands into compliant, even loyal, client-states. The conquered are taught to be ashamed of their origins; the despairing are issued philosophies that displace blame and uncertainty upon the mistakes of history and geography; the orphaned are taught to respect those who orphaned them. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Instillo](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4724) **Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 225 **Perception** +13; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Utopian](/Languages.aspx?ID=36); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet, [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +16, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +18, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +18, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +16, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +18, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +18, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +18 **Str** +2 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +6 **Items** [fine clothing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2715), [+1 striking light mace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=362), [Writing Set](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2762) --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +15 **Will** +18 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 135 **Immunities** fire **Resistances** physical 8 (except silver) **Weaknesses** holy 8 **Correcting Veil** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [subtle](/Traits.aspx?ID=522)) 100 feet. The instillo's telepathic presence constantly monitors perspectives and molds thought toward an infernal clarity. Creatures within the aura gain the [Dubious Knowledge](/Feats.aspx?ID=5142) skill feat but are unaware of this effect. The instillo is instantly aware when any creature within the aura fails a [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) check and influences the erroneous information that the creature gains.
**Nod of the Tongue** Creatures who can understand Diabolic take a –2 status penalty to their Will saves against the instillo's effects with the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) or [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) trait. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** light mace +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d4+6 bludgeoning **Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 26, attack +18 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Bullhorn](/Spells.aspx?ID=1971), [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Live Wire](/Spells.aspx?ID=2008), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715) - **1st** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463), [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [Fleet Step](/Spells.aspx?ID=1531) - **2nd** [Deafness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1483), [Laughing Fit](/Spells.aspx?ID=1583), [Stupefy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1691) - **3rd** [Enthrall](/Spells.aspx?ID=1516) (×2), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622) - **4th** [Containment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1981), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 23, attack +15 - **2nd** [Status](/Spells.aspx?ID=1690) (at will) - **4th** [Dispelling Globe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1494), [Dull Ambition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1990) **Rituals** DC 26 - **1st** [Diabolic Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=143) **Quiet Confidence** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) The instillo can hide the source of their magic. If the next action the instillo uses is to Cast a Spell, that spell gains the [subtle](/Traits.aspx?ID=522) trait. The trait hides only the spell's spellcasting actions and manifestations, not its effects. **Sneak Attack** The instillo's Strikes deal an additional 2d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures. **Superlative Rhetoric** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) Frequency once per round; Requirements The instillo's most recent action was to cast a spell with the auditory trait; Effect The instillo immediately casts daze at a creature that can hear them.
Anti-Intellectualists Instillos know the importance of diverse thinking and media literacy, which leads them to target educational institutions and free thinkers. They seed organizations with false information and selfish intellectuals who use their knowledge for themselves instead of others, plying them with power and information before exposing their corruption so society turns against them.
name: Instillo
